blocks:
Ayah

بَرَآءَةٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ عَـٰهَدتُّم مِّنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱)
[This is] a [declaration of] repudiation by Allah and His Apostle [addressed] to the polytheists with whom you had made a treaty: (1)

Key Points

From the fact that this Surah is named "Bara'ah" (Immunity) and "Tawbah" (Repentance) in the narrations, we understand that it is not part of Surah Al-Anfal, but rather an independent Surah. From the fact that the Surah begins without the Basmalah (Bismillahi-r-Rahmani-r-Rahim) due to its forceful content, we understand that the Basmalah in every Surah belongs to that Surah and is part of it, and is not merely a formality or... that appears at the beginning of every Surah.

The declaration of immunity (Bara'ah) was due to the breaking of covenants by the disbelievers, which is discussed in Ayah 7 and 8. Otherwise, the general rule is the observance of covenants, and as long as the opposing party remains faithful to the covenant, it must be upheld. As stated in Ayah 4: «الاّ الّذینَ عاهَدتُم من المشركِینَ ثمّ لَم ینقُصوكم شیئاً ولم یُظاهروا علیكم اَحداً فاتّموا الیهم عهدهم الى مدّتهم» (Except those of the polytheists with whom you have a treaty and who have not failed you in anything nor supported anyone against you, so complete for them their treaty until their term). Remain faithful to your covenant until the end of the term with those polytheists who have not broken the treaty or plotted [against you]. Furthermore, the Muslims agreed to that contract due to weakness; otherwise, the desire of the Muslims is the swift eradication of polytheism.

The incident of the announcement of these verses: Mecca was conquered in the eighth year of the Hijra, but the polytheists continued to come to Mecca to perform their religious rites, which were mixed with superstitions and deviations. One of their customs was that they would give away the clothes they used for Tawaf (circumambulation) as charity. A woman who wanted to perform more Tawafs, since she had no other clothes, was forced by the disbelievers to perform Tawaf naked, and people looked at her. This situation was intolerable for the Prophet and the Muslims, who were at the peak of their power. The Prophet was waiting for Allah's command until this Surah was revealed in Medina.

The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله commissioned Abu Bakr to recite some of its verses to the people of Mecca. Perhaps the selection of Abu Bakr was because he was an old man and no one was sensitive toward him. But when he reached near Mecca, Gabriel brought a message from Allah that the recitation of the verses must be performed by someone from the Prophet's household. The Prophet commissioned Ali عليه السلام for this task and said: "I am from him, and he is from me." Ali عليه السلام took the verses from Abu Bakr mid-way, went to Mecca, and recited them to the polytheists.

The incident of the recitation of the verses of this Surah by Ali عليه السلام is also mentioned in the books of the Ahl al-Sunnah, and companions such as Abu Bakr, Ali عليه السلام, Ibn Abbas, Anas ibn Malik, and Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari have narrated it, and it has been reported in many sources. [3]

Some of the Ahl al-Sunnah [4] have tried to portray this as an ordinary matter so that it would not be considered a merit for Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, claiming that the transfer of the recitation mission to Ali عليه السلام was done to win his heart, not as a distinction for him. Whereas, to win someone's heart, a harmless task is assigned to him, not the recitation of the verses of immunity (Bara'ah) from the polytheists, especially in the region of polytheism and by someone who had killed many of the polytheist leaders in battles and whom many held a grudge against!

When Allah commanded Hadrat Moses عليه السلام to go to Pharaoh and invite him to Monotheism, he said: "O God, I have killed one of their men, and I fear they will kill me in retaliation, so send my brother with me." But Ali عليه السلام, who had killed a large number of the leaders of polytheism, went alone and recited the verses of Bara'ah with utmost tranquility, and that too in a sensitive place like Mina, next to the Jamrat al-Aqabah.

The points announced to the disbelievers by Ali عليه السلام were: 1- Declaration of immunity and cancellation of covenants. 2- Prohibition of polytheists participating in Hajj starting the following year. 3- Prohibition of performing Tawaf while naked. 4- Prohibition of polytheists entering the House of Allah.

In many instances in the Quran, Allah and the Messenger are mentioned together, including: A: In gifts and favor. «اغناهم اللَّه ورسوله» [5] B: In allegiance (Bay'ah). «انّ الذین یبایعونك انما یبایعون اللَّه» [6] C: In obedience and following. «من یُطع الرسول فقد اطاع اللَّه» [7] D: In immunity and disavowal from others. «برائة من اللَّه و رسوله»

3) Musnad Ahmad Hanbal, (Vol. 3, pp. 212 & 283; Vol. 1, pp. 151 & 330), Mustadrak Sahihayn, (Vol. 3, p. 51), Tafsir Al-Manar, (Vol. 10, p. 157), Tafsir Tabari, (Vol. 10, p. 46), Tafsir Ibn Kathir (Vol. 2, pp. 322 - 333), Ihqaq al-Haqq, (Vol. 5, p. 368) and Fada'il al-Khamsah, (Vol. 2, p. 342). And in Al-Ghadir, (Vol. 6, p. 338) the names of 73 people who narrated this incident are mentioned. 4) Such as Fakhr Razi and Alusi in their commentaries. 5) At-Tawbah, 74. 6) Al-Fath, 10. 7) An-Nisa, 80.

Messages

1- The cancellation of the validity of a covenant with polytheists is among the prerogatives of the leader. «برآءةٌ من اللّه و رسوله» (The covenants made with the polytheists were concerning non-aggression against each other.)

2- Fulfillment of the covenant, yes; submission to conspiracy, never. «برآءةٌ ... إلى الَّذین عاهدتم»

3- Although legally we are obliged to be faithful to the covenant, inner immunity (Bara'ah) from polytheists and deviants is a religious principle. [8] «برآءةٌ من اللّه و رسوله»

4- Although Allah is the Lawgiver, «لایشرك فى حكمِه احداً» [9] (He does not share His rule with anyone), yet in practice and conduct, Allah and the Messenger are together. «من اللَّه و رسوله»

5- Bara'ah (Immunity) is a sign of decisiveness and authority, just as silence in the face of conspiracies and covenant-breaking is a sign of weakness and humiliation. «برآءةٌ من اللّه و رسوله»

6- Making covenants with polytheists is permissible under certain conditions. «الّذین عاهدتم»

7- If a contract is canceled due to fear of conspiracy and treachery, it must be announced to the opponents so that they are not taken by surprise. «برآءةٌ من اللَّه ...إلى ...المشركین»

8) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan.

9) Al-Kahf, 26.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Tawbah

This Surah, which is the ninth Surah of the Holy Quran, has 129 verses and was revealed in the ninth year of the Hijra. (1) Parts of it were revealed before the Battle of Tabuk, a section during the battle, and another part after it. The most famous names of this Surah in narrations are "Tawbah" and "Bara'ah." "Tawbah" (Repentance), because in this Surah, human repentance and the return of Divine favor are mentioned repeatedly; and "Bara'ah" (Immunity), because this Surah begins with the declaration of immunity from the polytheists.

Some commentators, due to the connection of the contents of this Surah with Surah Al-Anfal, have considered this Surah to be its continuation and said that for this reason, it did not need the Basmalah («بسم اللَّه الرحمن الرحیم»). However, in our view, which we have derived from the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, this Surah is independent, and the absence of the Basmalah is due to its forceful tone against the polytheists, because the phrase Basmalah is a sign of mercy and security, and this Surah begins with a declaration of aversion toward the covenant-breaking polytheists. As Hadrat Ali عليه السلام says: "The Basmalah is for granting security, and Bara'ah is for lifting security." (2)

Regarding the importance of this Surah, it is sufficient that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "The Surahs of Bara'ah and Tawhid were revealed accompanied by seventy thousand rows of angels."

Ayah

فَسِيحُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِى ٱللَّهِ ۙ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخْزِى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲)
Travel [unmolested] in the land for four months, but know that you cannot frustrate Allah, and that Allah shall disgrace the faithless. (2)

Key Points

The declaration of disavowal (Bara'ah) was on the tenth day of Dhul-Hijjah (Eid al-Adha). Therefore, the end of the four-month grace period would be the tenth of Rabi' al-Thani. [10] According to narrations, [11] the four-month period was for those who previously had no treaty, but the period for those who had treaties was until the end of their contract duration, whether it was less than four months or more than that.

10) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 290. 11) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- After nullifying the contracts and treaties, give the enemy a chance to think. «فسیحوا... اربعة اشهر» (According to historical accounts, many polytheists embraced Islam during these four months.)

2- Do not attack without prior announcement, even during times of power. «فسیحوا... اربعة اشهر»

3- Those who do not wish to accept Islam should know that wherever they go, they cannot escape the dominion of Allah. «غیر معجزى اللَّه»

4- War against Islam is war against Allah. «غیر معجزى اللَّه»

5- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that if you lose the opportunity to return (repent/revert), you will become wretched. «مخزى الكافرین»

Ayah

وَأَذَٰنٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَوْمَ ٱلْحَجِّ ٱلْأَكْبَرِ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَرِىٓءٌ مِّنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ ۙ وَرَسُولُهُۥ ۚ فَإِن تُبْتُمْ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِى ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۳)
[This is] an announcement from Allah and His Apostle to all the people on the day of the greater Hajj: that Allah and His Apostle repudiate the polytheists: If you repent that is better for you; but if you turn your backs [on Allah], know that you cannot frustrate Allah, and inform the faithless of a painful punishment (3)

Key Points

The first Ayah of this Surah was a declaration of disavowal (bara'ah) to the polytheists themselves; here, it is a declaration of disavowal from the polytheists to the general public. "al-Hajj al-Akbar" (The Greater Pilgrimage) is either the Day of Eid al-Adha (the Sacrifice) (12), or the Day of 'Arafah, or the Hajj rituals (13), in contrast to the 'Umrah rituals, which is al-Hajj al-Asghar (The Lesser Pilgrimage).

12) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 290. 13) Tafsir Burhān.

Messages

1- To prevent the enemy from feigning victimhood and campaigning against you, inform the people of the situation. «اذانٌ .... الى النّاس»

2- In propagation (Da'wah), do not neglect the elements of time and place. «یوم الحج الأكبر»

3- Utilize both encouragement and threat. «ان تبتم... خیرلكم، ان تولیّتم فاعلموا»

4- When severing relations, leave a window open for reconciliation. «فان تبتم»

5- The four-month grace period is a sign of Allah's grace, not His inability. «غیر معجزى اللَّه»

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ عَـٰهَدتُّم مِّنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَنقُصُوكُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَمْ يُظَـٰهِرُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ أَحَدًا فَأَتِمُّوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِمْ عَهْدَهُمْ إِلَىٰ مُدَّتِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤)
(barring the polytheists with whom you have made a treaty, and who did not violate any [of its terms] with you, nor backed anyone against you. So fulfill the treaty with them until [the end of] its term. Indeed Allah loves the Godwary). (4)

Key Points

The declaration of disavowal (برائت) only includes the polytheists who broke their covenants and plotted (against Islam). Otherwise, those like Banu Damrah and Banu Khuzaymah, who remained faithful, are exempted by this Ayah. Ali عليه السلام said: Remain faithful to the covenant you have made with anyone for its specified duration, and grant a four-month grace period for the covenant of anyone whose term is unspecified.

Messages

  1. Those who respect covenants, their covenants must be respected. «عاهدتم من المشركین ثم لم ینقصوكم»
  2. Fulfillment of the covenant is necessary, even if it is with polytheists. «عاهدتم من المشركین... فاتموا»
  3. Whoever aids your enemy is your enemy. «لم یظاهروا علیكم احداً» [14]
  4. Fulfillment of the covenant is a sign of piety (Taqwa). «فاتمّوا... انّ اللَّه یحبّ المتّقین»

(14) Since one of the conditions of the covenants was not to aid the enemies of Islam, now that they have provided aid, they have violated the condition and are therefore your enemies.

Ayah

فَإِذَا ٱنسَلَخَ ٱلْأَشْهُرُ ٱلْحُرُمُ فَٱقْتُلُوا۟ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدتُّمُوهُمْ وَخُذُوهُمْ وَٱحْصُرُوهُمْ وَٱقْعُدُوا۟ لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ ۚ فَإِن تَابُوا۟ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَوُا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ فَخَلُّوا۟ سَبِيلَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٥)
Then, when the sacred months have passed, kill the polytheists wherever you find them, capture them and besiege them, and lie in wait for them at every ambush. But if they repent, and maintain the prayer and give the zakat, then let them alone. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (5)

Key Points

The disbelievers who harassed the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the Muslims for 13 years in Mecca, and who, even after the Hijra (migration), did not refrain from any conspiracy for 9 years, deserve severe suppression. The word «حیث» (hayth) is used both for time and for place, meaning wherever and whenever you find the polytheists. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The path to repentance from polytheism (shirk) is to embrace faith (Iman). [15]

15) كافى، ج ۵، ص ۱۰.

Messages

1- After completing all stages of invitation, argumentation, and covenant-making, if the polytheists still conspire and show enmity, use all methods to suppress them, such as killing, taking captives, blocking roads, and besieging. «فاقتلوا، خذوا، احصروا»

2- Both decisiveness and leniency are necessary. «اقتلوا، خلّوا»

3- Freedom for the divine religion, yes, but intellectual deviation and the downfall of reason and humanity are intolerable. «فاقتلوا المشركین حیث وجدتموهم»

4- Muslims must always be lying in wait for the conspirators. «واقعدوا لهم كل مرصد»

5- The intelligence apparatus of the Muslims must be such that they monitor conspirators even outside the borders. «كلّ مرصد»

6- To suppress the conspiring enemies, we must control the borders and all routes of infiltration. «واقعدوا لهم كلّ مرصد»

7- Accept repentance even during war, because Islam is a religion of tolerance and magnanimity, not of settling scores and revenge. «فاقتلوا .... فان تابوا»

8- Repentance must be accompanied by action. We should not be deceived by repentance without action. «فان تابوا واقاموا...»

9- Repentance from polytheism (shirk) is faith (Iman), and the sign of true repentance is prayer (Salat) and Zakat. «تابوا واقاموا الصلوة...»

10- Prayer is at the head of all acts of worship. Do not interfere with anyone who respects the religious rites (prayer and Zakat). «اقاموا الصلوة...فخلّوا سبیلهم»

Ayah

وَإِنْ أَحَدٌ مِّنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ ٱسْتَجَارَكَ فَأَجِرْهُ حَتَّىٰ يَسْمَعَ كَلَـٰمَ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ أَبْلِغْهُ مَأْمَنَهُۥ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَعْلَمُونَ (٦)
If any of the polytheists seeks asylum from you, grant him asylum until he hears the Word of Allah. Then convey him to his place of safety. That is because they are a people who do not know. (6)

Messages

1- Respond positively to the enemy's request for asylum for the purpose of researching and understanding Islamic beliefs and ideas. «استجارك فاَجِره»

2- The path of thought and reason is open even for polytheists whose blood is permissible (to shed), and a separate consideration must be given for the potential guidance of even one person. «احدٌ من المشركین»

3- Islam is the religion of compassion, honor, and dignity. «استجارك فاَجِره»

4- Give the enemy an opportunity for thought and choice, and do not prevent people from intellectual growth, even in wartime conditions. «استجارك فاَجِره»

5- Choosing the truth and accepting correct beliefs requires a respite. «اَجِره حتّى یسمع كلام اللَّه»

6- The Quran is understandable speech that prepares the ground for guidance, and it is not such that its comprehension is beyond human capacity. «یسمع كلام اللَّه»

7- The Islamic government must provide the opportunity for the misguided to hear the Word of God, because the deviation of some is due to our lack of propagation, not out of malice. Perhaps if they hear the truth, they will change. «حتى یسمع كلام اللَّه»

8- Islam is the religion of freedom, and faith based on understanding is valuable, not faith based on fear or coercion. «اَجِره، یسمع، ابلغه مأمنه»

9- A school of thought that possesses logic does not rush. Give the enemy an opportunity to hear it, and guarantee his security so that he may choose with a peaceful mind. «یسمع، ابلغه مأمنه»

10- The disbelief of some originates from ignorance, and if they become aware, they will accept the truth. «ذلك بانّهم قوم لایعلمون»

Ayah

كَيْفَ يَكُونُ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ عَهْدٌ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَعِندَ رَسُولِهِۦٓ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ عَـٰهَدتُّمْ عِندَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۖ فَمَا ٱسْتَقَـٰمُوا۟ لَكُمْ فَٱسْتَقِيمُوا۟ لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۷)
How shall the polytheists have any [valid] treaty with Allah and His Apostle?! (Barring those with whom you made a treaty at the Holy Mosque; so long as they are steadfast with you, be steadfast with them. Indeed Allah loves the Godwary.) (7)

Key Points

This Ayah justifies the initial verses of Surah At-Tawbah (Bara'ah) and provides a reason for the command of disavowal (bara'ah), because they were not faithful to their covenants. Since the area extending 48 miles in every direction from the Ka'bah is part of the Sanctuary (Haram), the agreements concluded in these areas are referred to as "عند المسجد الحرام" (near the Sacred Mosque). For example, the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, which was concluded 15 miles from Mecca. Mentioning the word "مسجدالحرام" (Al-Masjid Al-Haram) when stating the location of the agreement indicates the importance of that place; otherwise, other covenants not concluded near Al-Masjid Al-Haram are also obligatory to fulfill.

Messages

1- Since the Jews and the polytheists harbor the greatest enmity toward the Muslims, [16] do not expect faithfulness from most of them. «كیف یكون ...»

2- When criticizing, do not speak generally, and pay attention to the sound individuals within the groups. «الاّ الذین عاهدتم»

3- Deal with your enemies on the basis of reciprocity regarding the fulfillment or violation of covenants. «فما استقاموا لكم...»

4- Piety (Taqwa) and fulfilling covenants are inseparable. «فاستقیموا لهم انّ اللَّه یحبّ المتّقین»

16) لَتجدنَّ اَشدّ الناسِ عَداوةً للّذین آمنوا الیهود و الّذین اشركوا» (Al-Ma'idah, 85).

Ayah

كَيْفَ وَإِن يَظْهَرُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ لَا يَرْقُبُوا۟ فِيكُمْ إِلًّا وَلَا ذِمَّةً ۚ يُرْضُونَكُم بِأَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَتَأْبَىٰ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (۸)
How? For if, they get the better of you, they will observe toward you neither kinship nor covenant. They please you with their mouths while their hearts spurn you; and most of them are transgressors. (8)

Key Points

"Ill" (اِلّ) means kinship, neighborliness, and emotional, human, and customary relations. [17] "Dhimmah" (ذِمّة) means a covenant or pledge whose fulfillment is necessary, and if it is violated, people condemn the covenant-breaker. These verses are also evidence for the command of severity towards the polytheist covenant-breakers. One cannot attack the enemy merely on the basis that if they overcome us, they will do such-and-such; rather, there must be evidence of their conspiracy and aggression, otherwise it would be retribution before the crime.

17) There was kinship and relationship between the early Muslims and the polytheists.

Messages

1- The depth of the enemy's malice must be recognized when they are powerful, not when they are weak. «ان یظهروا علیكم»

2- Silence and naivety regarding an enemy who, if they gain dominance, will observe no consideration, is a sin. «لا یرقبوا فیكم الاّ و لا ذمّة»

3- The polytheists neither observe emotional and neighborly matters nor respect covenants and commitments. «الاّ ولا ذمّةً»

4- The enemies' pretense and political and propaganda games should not deceive us. «یرضونكم بافواههم وتأبى قلوبهم»

5- Covenant-breaking, hypocrisy, pretense, and political maneuvering constitute transgression (Fisq). «تأبى قلوبهم واكثرهم فاسقون»

6- The majority of polytheists are transgressors (Fasiqūn), but sound individuals can also be found among them. «اكثرهم فاسقون» (We must be fair in our attributions.)

Ayah

ٱشْتَرَوْا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا فَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ سَآءَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۹)
They have sold the signs of Allah for a paltry gain, and have barred [the people] from His way. Evil indeed is what they have been doing. (9)

Messages

1- Man possesses the right to choose in his actions and selection of the path. «اشتروا» (18)

2- Whatever we gain in exchange for losing the Divine signs is little. «ثمناً قلیلاً»

3- Worldliness is the cause of fighting religion and preventing people from the path of Allah. «اشتروا بایات اللَّه ثمناً قلیلا فصدّوا عن سبیله»

4- Selling Divine pleasure and eternal Paradise for the fleeting and afflicted world is the worst deed, because the entire world and everything in it is a meager and insignificant commodity compared to the Divine favors granted to the believers. «اشتروا... ثمناً قلیلا... ساء ما كانوا یعملون»

18) Verses that indicate man's transaction (trade) are a sign of man's free will.

Ayah

لَا يَرْقُبُونَ فِى مُؤْمِنٍ إِلًّا وَلَا ذِمَّةً ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُعْتَدُونَ (۱٠)
They observe toward a believer neither kinship nor covenant, and it is they who are the transgressors. (10)

Key Points

This Ayah also explains the wisdom behind Allah's severe command regarding dissociation from the polytheists (mushrikūn). In the two preceding Ayahs, the discussion concerned their disregard for the covenant specifically regarding you who are present, ["لایرقبون فیكم" (they observe no tie of kinship or covenant concerning you)], but in this Ayah, the expression ["لایرقبون فى مؤمنٍ" (they observe no tie of kinship or covenant concerning a believer)] is used, which indicates their hostility towards all people of faith.

Messages

1- The polytheists harbor enmity towards the believers, so have no hesitation in dealing severely with them. ["لایرقبون فى مؤمن"]

2- In the view of the disbelievers (kuffār), the greatest crime of the Muslims is their faith in Allah, and this is the pretext for their hostility towards the people of faith. (19) ["لا یرقبون فى مؤمن"]

3- Breaking a covenant is aggression. ["اولئك هم المعتدون"]

4- Observing the rights of relatives and fulfilling covenants is obligatory, and disregard for them stems from a spirit of aggression. ["اولئك هم المعتدون"]

5- War against the covenant-breaking polytheists is defensive, and as stated in Ayah 13, we were not the initiators of it. ["اولئك هم المعتدون"]

(19) Similar to the Ayah: "و مانقموا منهم الاّ ان یؤمنوا باللَّه العزیز الحكیم" (Burūj, 8).

Ayah

فَإِن تَابُوا۟ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَوُا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ فَإِخْوَٰنُكُمْ فِى ٱلدِّينِ ۗ وَنُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۱)
Yet if they repent and maintain the prayer and give the zakat, then they are your brethren in faith. We elaborate the signs for a people who have knowledge. (11)

Key Points

In the preceding verses, Allah stated: If the polytheists repent, establish the prayer, and pay the Zakat, do not interfere with them anymore, «فخلّوا سبیلهم» (then let them go their way). In this verse, He states: Not only should you not trouble them, but you should also forget the past and treat them fraternally.

Messages

1- In the manner of interaction, observe the principle of gradual, step-by-step approach. First, non-interference, «فخلّوا سبیلهم», and subsequently, affection and brotherhood. «اخوانكم فى الدین»

2- Genuine repentance is accompanied by action. «تابوا و اقاموا...»

3- Those who abandon prayer (Salat) and Zakat are not our religious brothers. «فان... اقاموا الصلوة و آتوا الزكوة فاخوانكم فى الدین»

4- The condition for entering the sphere of religious brotherhood is prayer and Zakat. «اقاموا... فاخوانكم»

5- The foundation of a Muslim's relationships, love, and hatred is the religious doctrine (Maktab). «فان تابوا... فاخوانكم». As stated in the following verse: «فان نكثوا... فقاتلوا»

6- Treat the remorseful and repentant with a fraternal approach. «فان تابوا... فاخوانكم»

7- The objective of Islamic wars is to bring the polytheists back to Monotheism (Tawhid). «فان تابوا... فاخوانكم»

8- Those who were subject to execution (Wajib al-Qatl) until yesterday acquire social rights equal to Muslims through repentance, prayer, and Zakat, and fighting them becomes forbidden (Haram). «فاخوانكم فى الدّین»

9- Knowledge and learning provide the basis for contemplation and reflection upon the divine verses. «لقوم یعلمون»

Ayah

وَإِن نَّكَثُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَهُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ عَهْدِهِمْ وَطَعَنُوا۟ فِى دِينِكُمْ فَقَـٰتِلُوٓا۟ أَئِمَّةَ ٱلْكُفْرِ ۙ إِنَّهُمْ لَآ أَيْمَـٰنَ لَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَنتَهُونَ (۱۲)
But if they break their pledges after their having made a treaty and revile your religion, then fight the leaders of unfaith—indeed, they have no [commitment to] pledges,—maybe they will desist. (12)

Key Points

They asked Ali عليه السلام: Why did you pursue the fugitives of the Battle of Siffin, but you did not concern yourselves with the fugitives in the Battle of the Camel (Jamal)? The Imam replied: In Siffin, the leader of disbelief (kufr) was alive, and the fugitives would gather around him, organize themselves, and launch a renewed attack. However, in the Battle of the Camel, with the killing of their leader, they had no central point for renewed organization and formation. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever mocks your religion certainly becomes a disbeliever (kafir). Then He recited this Ayah. (20)

20) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Apostasy from the religion is one example of breaking a covenant and mocking the school of thought (maktab). «و ان نكثوا» (The word «نكثوا» [they broke] following «تابوا» [they repented] perhaps refers to apostasy.)

2- The punishment for slandering and insulting Islam is execution. «طعنوا، فقاتلوا» (Those who insult religious sanctities must be dealt with severely.)

3- Islamic Jihad is for the defense of the school of thought (maktab). «نكثوا، طعنوا، فقاتلوا»

4- Since covenant-breaking and mocking the religion originate from the leaders of disbelief (kufr), fight them. «فقاتلوا ائمّة الكفر»

5- In combat, the destruction of the heads of the conspiracy, the command center, and the enemy's organization must be the priority. «فقاتلوا ائمّة الكفر»

6- Do not be deceived by every oath. The oaths of covenant-breakers are invalid. «لا ایمان لهم»

7- One of the goals of Islamic Jihad is to prevent the enemy's conspiracy. «لعلّهم ینتهون»

Ayah

أَلَا تُقَـٰتِلُونَ قَوْمًا نَّكَثُوٓا۟ أَيْمَـٰنَهُمْ وَهَمُّوا۟ بِإِخْرَاجِ ٱلرَّسُولِ وَهُم بَدَءُوكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۚ أَتَخْشَوْنَهُمْ ۚ فَٱللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَوْهُ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۳)
Will you not make war on a people who broke their pledges and resolved to expel the Apostle, and opened [hostilities] against you initially? Do you fear them? But Allah is worthier of being feared by you, should you be faithful. (13)

Messages

1- The goals and motivations for war must be clear to the Muslim combatant. «نكثوا، همّوا، بدؤكم» (This explains that: the enemies broke the covenants, intended harm against the Messenger of Allah, and were the initiators of the war.)

2- Your war is defensive, and the enemy was the initiator of the aggression. «هم بدؤكم»

3- During war, compare the fire of war with the fire of Hell, so that the combatants can make better decisions. «اتخشونهم، فاللَّه احقّ ان تخشوه»

4- The true believer fears only Allah. «فاللَّه احقّ ان تخشوه ان كنتم مؤمنین»

Ayah

قَـٰتِلُوهُمْ يُعَذِّبْهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِأَيْدِيكُمْ وَيُخْزِهِمْ وَيَنصُرْكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَيَشْفِ صُدُورَ قَوْمٍ مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱٤)
Make war on them so that Allah may punish them by your hands and humiliate them, and help you against them, and heal the hearts of a faithful folk, (14)

Key Points

Question: Although Ayah 32 of Al-Anfal states that as long as the Prophet is among the people, Allah will not punish them, then how is punishment mentioned for them in this Ayah? Answer: The intent in that Ayah is heavenly and root-eradicating punishments, such as the punishment of the people of 'Ad and Thamud, while in this Ayah, the intent is the punishment and hardships of war.

Messages

1- Battlefronts are the setting for Divine assistance. «قاتلوهم، ینصركم» (Presence and struggle are from you; victory and assistance are from Allah.)

2- Fighters are the arm of truth and the agents for executing Allah's decree. «یعذّبهم بایدیكم»

3- Divine traditions are implemented through natural means and causes. «بایدیكم»

4- Following the military defeat of the enemy is spiritual and political defeat. «یعذّبهم،یخزهم»

5- Allah desires the defeat and humiliation of the enemies of religion and the victory and honor of the believers. «یخزهم و ینصركم»

6- Among the goals of war are the eradication of disbelief, the humiliation of the disbelievers, and the tranquility of the believers. «یعذّبهم ... یخزهم... یشف صدور»

7- Encouragement and propagation are necessary before war. Allah gives the believers a definite promise. «ینصركم، یشف صدور»

8- The hearts of the early Muslims were wounded and filled with suffering and persecution. «یشف صدور قومٍ مؤمنین»

9- Although some are martyred and bereaved in war, the Islamic Ummah (community) lives in honor and tranquility. «صدور قومٍ مؤمنین» (the hearts of a believing people) is used, and He did not say: «صدوركم» (your hearts).

10- The destiny of believers in social matters is interconnected, and your victory is the healing of the hearts of other believers. «ینصركم، یشف قومٍ مؤمنین»

Ayah

وَيُذْهِبْ غَيْظَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَيَتُوبُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱٥)
and remove rage from their hearts, and Allah turns clemently to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise (15)

Messages

1- Although the goal of war is the pleasure of Allah, defense, and preventing polytheism, conspiracy, and covenant-breaking, yet the soothing of hearts and the calming of the soul are also among its consequential and secondary effects. [21] «یذهب غیظ قلوبهم»

2- After victory, welcome those who come to you for solidarity and connection, and do not say: Where were you until now? «و یتوب اللَّه على من یشاء»

3- The acceptance of repentance depends on the will and decree of Allah, not that the repentant person deserves it. «یتوب اللَّه على من یشاء»

4- Beware of not accepting those who come to you out of fear of deceit! Allah is aware of genuine or hypocritical repentance, but according to Divine wisdom, whoever declares Islam must be accepted—while maintaining security principles. «علیم حكیم»

(21) This truth is pointed out in verses such as «سیجعل اللَّه بعد عسرٍ یُسرا» (Talaq, 7) and «فانّ مع العسر یُسراً» (Inshirah, 6): This time, bitterer than poison, shall pass; once again, time like sugar shall come.

Ayah

أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تُتْرَكُوا۟ وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ جَـٰهَدُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا رَسُولِهِۦ وَلَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلِيجَةً ۚ وَٱللَّهُ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٦)
Do you suppose that you will be let off while Allah has not yet ascertained those of you who wage jihad and those who do not take anyone as [their] confidant besides Allah and His Apostle and the faithful? Allah is well aware of what you do. (16)

Key Points

| This Ayah, like the preceding Ayahs, is for encouraging Jihad (struggle/holy war). Waleejah (وَلیجَة), derived from Wulūj (ولوج), is like the word Bitānah (بطانة), meaning secrets and hidden matters. The intention here is a confidant (a keeper of secrets). According to multiple narrations, the intent behind the believers who can be confidants is the heavenly leaders. [22]

22) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Existence and its programs are purposeful. Humans and their future are not left aimless; therefore, one must leave the world of imagination and be realistic. (ام حَسِبتم)

2- The claim of faith is not enough; what separates the ranks is action, Jihad, and trials. (ام حَسِبتم ان تتركوا)

3- Those who succeed in the test of faith are those who do not share the secrets of the Islamic society except with Allah, the Messenger, and true believers. (ولیجة) (In early Islam, some individuals maintained contact with foreigners.)

4- Giving secrets and information to foreigners and considering them confidants is forbidden (Haram), a sign of weakness and lack of faith, and is subject to warning and reproach. (لم یتّخذوا... وَلیجة)

5- The duty of Muslims is to fight against external enemies and to preserve secrets and maintain confidentiality against internal enemies. (جاهدوا, لم یتّخذوا)

6- Allah is aware of the condition of the people and has no need for testing, but examination is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). (و اللَّه خبیر)

7- If you reveal your secrets to a foreigner, Allah knows and will hold you accountable. (واللَّه خبیر بما تعملون)

Ayah

مَا كَانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ أَن يَعْمُرُوا۟ مَسَـٰجِدَ ٱللَّهِ شَـٰهِدِينَ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِم بِٱلْكُفْرِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ وَفِى ٱلنَّارِ هُمْ خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱۷)
The polytheists may not maintain Allah’s mosques while they are witness to their own unfaith. Their works have failed, and they shall remain in the Fire [forever]. (17)

Key Points

Although the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah concerns the repair of the Masjid al-Haram, its ruling applies to all mosques. For this reason, the word "مساجد" (mosques) is used, not "مسجدالحرام" (Masjid al-Haram). The custodianship of the Masjid al-Haram, its repair, and its maintenance were in the hands of the polytheists (mushrikin) in the early days of Islam, before the Conquest of Mecca. One of the declarations announced during the declaration of disavowal (Bara'ah) by Hadrat Ali عليه السلام was that the polytheists do not have the right to repair the Masjid al-Haram, nor do they even have the right to enter it. (This is mentioned in Ayah 27).

Messages

  1. In the construction of mosques and the religious and cultural foundations of Muslims, disbelievers (kuffar) and polytheists (mushrikin) have no right to interfere. «ما كان للمشركین»
  2. In the construction and administration of sacred centers and institutions, do not accept the money of the unqualified, so that they do not interfere, boast, or have expectations. «ما كان للمشركین»
  3. Action alone is not important; intention (niyyah) plays the main role. «ماكان للمشركین أن یعمروا»
  4. Not every income is legitimate, nor is every participation valuable. Beware lest, out of love for the prosperity of mosques, disbelievers infiltrate religious affairs. «أن یعمروا مساجد اللَّه»
  5. Those who openly display irreligion do not have the right to interfere in religious affairs. «شاهدین على انفسهم بالكفر»
  6. The polytheist (mushrik) is the same as the disbeliever (kafir). «مشركین... شاهدین على انفسهم بالكفر»
  7. Disbelief (kufr) is the cause of the destruction, corruption, and worthlessness of the good deeds of the disbelievers. «بالكفر... حبطت اعمالهم»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يَعْمُرُ مَسَـٰجِدَ ٱللَّهِ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَأَقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتَى ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَلَمْ يَخْشَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ ۖ فَعَسَىٰٓ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَن يَكُونُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (۱۸)
Only those shall maintain Allah’s mosques who believe in Allah and the Last Day, and maintain the prayer, and give the zakat, and fear no one except Allah. They, hopefully, will be among the guided. (18)

Key Points

The mosque (Masjid) is an important religious and social base for Muslims. Therefore, both its custodians (mutawalliyān) must be righteous and pure, and its programs must be constructive and educational. Also, its budget must be legitimate and lawful (halāl), and the mosque attendees must be people of piety (taqwā), devoted to God, and held in esteem. Otherwise, if the builders of the mosques are tyrants and kings, and the prayer leaders (pīshnamāzān) are ignorant and fearful individuals, and the servants are lethargic and weak-willed people, naturally, the mosques will deviate from their primary goal, which is spiritual flourishing. According to the late Fayd Kashani in Tafsir al-Safi, the maintenance (ta'mīr) of the mosque includes repair, cleaning and carpeting, lighting, teaching, and propagation (tablīgh). Zakat (alms) is mentioned 32 times in the Quran, 28 of which are stated alongside prayer (Salat). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «اذا رأیتم الرّجُلَ یعتاد المسجدَ فاشهَدُوا له بالأیمان» [23] As soon as you see someone habitually frequenting the mosque, bear witness to his faith. Numerous benefits are mentioned in the traditions for those who frequent the mosque, including: finding a religious friend and brother, gaining useful knowledge, guidance and avoidance of sin, and benefiting from divine grace and mercy. [24]

23) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr. 24) Wasā’il al-Shī‘ah and Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr.

Messages

1- The custodianship and maintenance of the mosque have conditions:

A: In terms of belief (creed): Faith in the Origin (Allah) and the Resurrection (Ma'ād). «آمن باللّه و الیوم الاخر»

B: In terms of action: Establishing prayer (Salat) and paying Zakat. «اقام الصلوة وآتى الزكوة»

C: In terms of spirit: Courage and invulnerability. «لم یخش الا اللّه» (If the mosque custodian is courageous, the mosque will also be a center for anti-oppression movements.)

2- It is the duty of the mosque custodians and maintainers to attend to the deprived. «انّما یعمر... آتى الزكوة»

3- Faith is not separate from action, «آمن... و اقام»; prayer is not separate from Zakat, «اقام الصلوة و آتى الزكوة»; and the mosque is not separate from revolution. «مساجد اللّه... و لم یخش»

4- Despite having faith, righteous deeds, prayer, Zakat, and courage, we should still not become arrogant; the danger of deviation still exists. «فعسى اولئك من المهتدین»

Ayah

۞ أَجَعَلْتُمْ سِقَايَةَ ٱلْحَآجِّ وَعِمَارَةَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ كَمَنْ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَجَـٰهَدَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ لَا يَسْتَوُۥنَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۹)
Do you regard the providing of water to Hajj pilgrims and the maintenance of the Holy Mosque as similar [in worth] to someone who has faith in Allah and [believes in] the Last Day and wages jihad in the way of Allah? They are not equal with Allah, and Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (19)

Key Points

Abbas, the uncle of the Prophet, and Shaybah used to boast to one another. Abbas prided himself on providing water to the pilgrims of the House of God, and Shaybah prided himself on being the key-holder of the Ka'bah. Ali عليه السلام said: But I, despite my young age, pride myself on the fact that you embraced faith through my struggle (Jihad) and my sword. Abbas became upset and complained about Ali عليه السلام to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. This Ayah was then revealed. (25) Ali عليه السلام repeatedly cited this Ayah to assert his priority, because faith and Jihad are superior to services rendered during the era of polytheism (Shirk), which lack spiritual value.

25) Tafsir-e Namuneh; Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 204.

Messages

1- Let us not be obsessed with deeds (alone); a deed without faith is an empty mirage and a lifeless body. «اجعلتم سقایة الحاج... كمن آمن...»

2- Sincere combatants are superior to others, even if others provide services through different occupations. «اجعلتم سقایة الحاج ... كمن... جاهد فى اللّه»

3- Equating believing combatants with others is subject to reproach and is considered one of the social injustices. «اجعلتم... كمن... جاهد... القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَهَاجَرُوا۟ وَجَـٰهَدُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ أَعْظَمُ دَرَجَةً عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ (۲٠)
Those who have believed and migrated, and waged jihad in the way of Allah with their possessions and persons have a greater rank near Allah, and it is they who are the triumphant. (20)

Messages

1- Faith (Iman), migration (Hijrah), and striving (Jihad), like piety (Taqwa), are at the forefront of all values. «آمنوا و هاجروا و جاهدوا... اعظم درجةً»

2- If the criterion for value among people is tribal and racial relations, then in the divine reckoning, faith, migration, and striving are the criteria for value. [26] «عنداللَّه»

3- Faith is the prerequisite for other perfections. «آمنوا»

4- The value of all sacred deeds depends on their intention. «فى سبیل اللَّه»

5- Success (Fawz) and salvation are only achieved through faith, migration, and striving. «آمنوا و... اولئك هم الفائزون»

26) If all Muslims and the Companions of the Prophet were placed in one rank, who would be the first to embrace faith, the one who spent the longest time on the front lines, and the one who endured the most blows, if not Ali عليه السلام?

Ayahs

يُبَشِّرُهُمْ رَبُّهُم بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنْهُ وَرِضْوَٰنٍ وَجَنَّـٰتٍ لَّهُمْ فِيهَا نَعِيمٌ مُّقِيمٌ (۲۱)
Their Lord gives them the good news of His mercy and [His] pleasure, and for them there will be gardens with lasting bliss, (21)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥٓ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (۲۲)
to remain in them forever. With Allah indeed is a great reward. (22)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Allah Himself has given the glad tidings of Paradise to the believing emigrants (Muhajirun) and strivers (Mujahidin). [27] «یبشّرهم ربّهم»

2- A sign of the comprehensiveness of a school of thought is that, besides being in harmony with human nature (fitrah), it gives hope to its followers. «یبشّرهم»

3- Glad tidings (bushra) and reward are among the affairs (shuu'n) of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «یبشّرهم ربّهم»

4- Attaining the mercy (rahmah) and pleasure (ridwan) of Paradise is a manifestation of triumph and salvation (fawz). «الفائزون یبشرّهم ربّهم...»

5- The mercy and pleasure of Allah precede material blessings. «برحمة منه و رضوان و جنّات» (The names of mercy and pleasure are mentioned before Paradise.)

6- The gardens of Paradise are full of blessings. [28] «نعیم»

7- If we forgo fleeting blessings for the sake of Allah, we will attain eternal blessings. «نعیم مقیم»

8- Annihilation and disappearance (fana wa zawal) are the greatest afflictions of worldly blessings, an affliction that does not exist in the Hereafter. «مقیم، خالدین، ابداً»

9- The great reward is exclusively His; the rewards given by others, regardless of who or what they are, are small. «عنده» before «اجر عظیم» is a sign of exclusivity.

10- The God Who refers to the entire world as «قلیل» (little) refers to the reward of the strivers (Mujahidin) as «عظیم» (great).

11- Allah buys our little and fleeting possessions at the most valuable price, even though everything we possess is from Him. «اجر عظیم»

27) Other glad tidings have also been mentioned in other verses, such as: «سقاهم ربّهم» (Al-Insan, 21) and «صلواتٌ من ربّهم» (Al-Baqarah, 157).

28) According to Mufradat, «نعیم» means abundant blessing.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّخِذُوٓا۟ ءَابَآءَكُمْ وَإِخْوَٰنَكُمْ أَوْلِيَآءَ إِنِ ٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟ ٱلْكُفْرَ عَلَى ٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُم مِّنكُمْ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۲۳)
O you who have faith! Do not befriend your fathers and brothers if they prefer faithlessness to faith. Those of you who befriend them—it is they who are the wrongdoers. (23)

Key Points

The Quranic warnings regarding accepting the guardianship (wilayah) of the disbelievers are staggering. For instance, in Surah Al-Ma'idah, it states: «و من یتولّهم منكم فانّه منهم» (29) Whoever accepts their guardianship is one of them. Likewise, in Surah Aal-Imran, He says: «و من یفعل ذلك فلیس مِن اللَّه فى شى ءٍ» (30) Whoever does this has severed his relationship with Allah. Some Muslims, during their migration (Hijra) to Medina, faced the wrath of their disbelieving parents, but they preferred preserving their religion over gaining their parents' satisfaction. Of course, the guardianship (wilayah) of a disbelieving father and mother should not be accepted, but this is different from being kind to them.

29) Ma'idah, 51. 30) Aal-Imran, 28.

Messages

1- A disbelieving father has no guardianship (wilayah) over his Muslim child, and ideological relationships take precedence over familial relationships. «لا تتّخذوا»

2- Emotions must not override ideological values. «لاتتّخذوا ابائكم و اخوانكم»

3- The guardianship (wilayah) of a disbeliever is forbidden, even if they are the closest individuals. «لاتتّخذوا ابائكم... أولیاء ان استحبّوا الكفر»

4- The criterion of religion and ideology takes precedence over every other relationship. «لاتتّخذوا، ان استحبّوا الكفر»

5- Accepting the guardianship (wilayah) of the disbelievers is oppression (zulm). «الظالمون»

Ayah

قُلْ إِن كَانَ ءَابَآؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَآؤُكُمْ وَإِخْوَٰنُكُمْ وَأَزْوَٰجُكُمْ وَعَشِيرَتُكُمْ وَأَمْوَٰلٌ ٱقْتَرَفْتُمُوهَا وَتِجَـٰرَةٌ تَخْشَوْنَ كَسَادَهَا وَمَسَـٰكِنُ تَرْضَوْنَهَآ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَجِهَادٍ فِى سَبِيلِهِۦ فَتَرَبَّصُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِىَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَمْرِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۲٤)
Say, ‘If your fathers and your sons, your brethren, your spouses, and your kinsfolk, the possessions that you have acquired, the business you fear may suffer, and the dwellings you are fond of, are dearer to you than Allah and His Apostle and to waging jihad in His way, then wait until Allah issues His edict, and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot. (24)

Key Points

One day, Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام asked a group: Why do you not migrate to Medina to help Islam and join the Prophet? They replied: We are with our brothers and family, defending our homes. [31] The groups mentioned in this Ayah are examples, and for this reason, 'father' includes 'mother,' 'son' includes 'daughter,' and 'brother' includes 'sister.'

31) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The measure of faith can be recognized when one stands at the crossroads of this world and the Hereafter. «احبّ الیكم من اللّه و رسوله و جهاد»

2- Jihad (striving/holy struggle) is one of the pillars of Islam and is mentioned alongside Monotheism and Prophethood. «من اللَّه و رسوله و جهادٍ»

3- To cross the bridges of materialism, one must strengthen their beliefs and intimacy with Allah. «ان كان آبائكم... و اموال... احبّ الیكم من اللّه و رسوله...»

4- Family affections and material possessions must not prevent Jihad, and wherever they do, they pave the way for Divine wrath. «ان كان آبائكم... و اموال... احبّ... فتربّصوا...»

(In the path of loving Allah and striving in His way, one must sacrifice everything and everyone. This is because preserving religion takes precedence over preserving familial, emotional, and economic relationships.)

5- Affluent, worldly-minded Muslims are like disbelievers. [32] «فتربّصوا»

6- If comfort becomes the principle of life, humanity falls, leading to distance from Allah and the Prophet, and an unknown wrath and anger awaits. «یأتى اللَّه بأمره»

7- Comfort-seekers who evade responsibility are not included in Divine guidance. «لایهدى»

8- Prioritizing material things over spiritual matters is a sign of transgression (fisq). «احبّ الیكم... الفاسقین» (The boundary between faith and transgression is abandoning spirituality.)

32) In the Ayah, the word «فتربّصوا» (then wait) is used when addressing them, and this tone in the Quran is typically used when addressing disbelievers (kuffar). Furthermore, the Ayah indicates that Allah Himself does not speak directly to the worldly-minded, but rather commands the Prophet to convey the message to them. «قُل» (Say). Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

لَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى مَوَاطِنَ كَثِيرَةٍ ۙ وَيَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ ۙ إِذْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ كَثْرَتُكُمْ فَلَمْ تُغْنِ عَنكُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ ثُمَّ وَلَّيْتُم مُّدْبِرِينَ (۲٥)
Allah has certainly helped you in many situations, and on the day of Hunayn, when your great number impressed you, but it did not avail you in any way, and the earth became narrow for you in spite of its expanse, whereupon you turned your backs [to flee]. (25)

Key Points

In the previous verses, there was encouragement for Jihad and a warning against attachments. In this verse, He recounts the divine favors and assistance so that the motivation for Jihad is strengthened. The total number of battles (jang-hā) and expeditions (sarāyā) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, which are referred to in this verse by the phrase «مواطن كثیرة» (many places), was eighty. (33) It is narrated that "Mutawakkil al-Abbasi" fell ill and vowed that if He recovered, He would give «دراهم كثیرة» (many dirhams). After his recovery, there was a discussion about how much «دراهم كثیرة» amounted to. They asked Imam Hadi علیه السلام, and He, citing this verse, said: He must give eighty dirhams. (34)

Portrait of the Battle of Hunayn

The Battle of Hunayn took place in an area near Ta'if, between the Muslims and the tribe of "Hawazin," and for this reason, it is also called the Battle of Hawazin. In the eighth year of the Hijra, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله mobilized an army to that region to counter the conspiracy and aggression of Hawazin. The ten-thousand-strong army of Islam, which had conquered Mecca, along with two thousand new Muslims, set out towards that area. The fighting and engagement occurred after the Fajr (morning) prayer. The Muslims, who were caught by surprise by the attack of the Hawazin tribes, mostly fled, and the order of the Islamic army collapsed. However, due to the fierce resistance of a group, the fugitives eventually returned upon the call of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. The attack recommenced, and with the killing of one hundred polytheists, the surrender of the rest, and the acquisition of much spoils, the Battle of Hunayn concluded in favor of the Muslims with the help of Allah. Regarding the number of captives, the amount of spoils, the manner of their distribution, the issues that arose, and the results of this battle, there is much to be said, and one must refer to books of history and maghāzī (biographies of the Prophet's campaigns).

33) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 464. 34) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

  1. The main factor for victory in the battles during the early days of Islam was divine help and assistance. «لقد نصركم اللّه...»
  2. Remembering divine favors is among the factors that strengthen morale and faith. «لقد نصركم اللَّه»
  3. The Battle of Hunayn was very important. («مواطن كثیرة» includes the Battle of Hunayn, but due to its importance, it is mentioned separately.) «مواطن كثیرة ویوم حنین»
  4. Sometimes the large number of people and material resources lead to arrogance and negligence, and consequently, to defeat. «اعجبتكم كثرتكم»
  5. Divine assistance is needed at all times, whether in a state of weakness or a state of strength. In the Battle of Badr, with few Muslims, they received divine help, and in the Battle of Hunayn, with a large number, divine help still came to their aid. «لقد نصركم اللّه... و یوم حُنین»
  6. Without the Will of Allah, material means are ineffective. «لم تغن عنكم»
  7. To instill a spirit of servitude and humility, sometimes weaknesses must be exposed so that undue arrogance is broken. «ثمّ ولیّتم مدبرین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُۥ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ وَعَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَأَنزَلَ جُنُودًا لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا وَعَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۲٦)
Then Allah sent down His composure upon His Apostle and upon the faithful, and He sent down hosts you did not see, and He punished the faithless, and that is the requital of the faithless. (26)

Key Points

The word "سَكینة" (Sakīnah/Tranquility) is used six times in the Quran, five of which relate to war. In the Battle of Hunayn, Allah bestowed four types of favor upon the believers: Sakīnah, unseen forces, subjugation of the disbelievers, and acceptance of the repentance of those who fled (which is mentioned in the next Ayah). Some of the captured disbelievers would ask the Muslims: Where are those people dressed in white who were killing us? [35] This refers to the angels who appeared to the disbelievers wearing white garments.

35) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The lapse of fighters on the battlefield does not cause them to be deprived of divine unseen aid. «ثمّ ولّیتم مدبرین ثمّ انزل اللّه سكینته»

2- Unseen aid is the secret to the victory of the Muslims. «انزل اللَّه سكینته»

3- Possessing high morale and confidence stemming from faith is one of the main factors for victory in battle. «سكینته»

4- Tranquility and confidence are necessary both for the leader and for His followers. «سكینته على رسوله و على المؤمنین» The Prophet’s concern was the flight of the people, and the people’s unrest was due to fear.

5- The spiritual tranquility of the believers came from Allah, «سكینتهُ». In another Ayah, He says: «سكینتهٌ من ربّكم» [36]

6- Belief in the presence of angels and divine agents in war to aid the believers is a Quranic doctrine. «انزل جنوداً لم تروها»

7- Being killed is an honor for one who believes in the Origin and the Return (Resurrection), but for the disbelievers, it is a source of torment. «عذّب»

8- The ultimate fate of all disbelievers is defeat and humiliation. «عذّب... ذلك جزاء الكافرین»

36) Al-Baqarah, 248.

Ayah

ثُمَّ يَتُوبُ ٱللَّهُ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲۷)
Then Allah shall turn clemently after that to whomever He wishes. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (27)

Key Points

| Regarding what is meant by repentance (Tawbah), there are several possibilities: 1- Repentance from the sin of fleeing the battlefield. 2- Repentance from polytheism (Shirk). 3- Repentance from arrogance and reliance on the large number of people.

Messages

1- The doors of repentance are always open for everyone, even for disbelieving captives and fugitives. «یتوب اللَّه» (The imperfect verb indicates continuity.)

2- Accepting repentance is not obligatory upon Allah, but rather it is a favor related to His own wisdom. «من یشاء»

3- Allah both covers sins and loves mankind. «غفور رحیم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا ٱلْمُشْرِكُونَ نَجَسٌ فَلَا يَقْرَبُوا۟ ٱلْمَسْجِدَ ٱلْحَرَامَ بَعْدَ عَامِهِمْ هَـٰذَا ۚ وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ عَيْلَةً فَسَوْفَ يُغْنِيكُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦٓ إِن شَآءَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲۸)
O you who have faith! The polytheists are indeed unclean: so let them not approach the Holy Mosque after this year. Should you fear poverty, Allah will enrich you out of His grace, if He wishes. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (28)

Key Points

One of the four commands that Imam Ali عليه السلام conveyed to the polytheists during the Hajj of the 9th year of the Hijra was that, starting from the following year, they would not have the right to enter the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram).

Messages

1- Polytheism (Shirk) is impurity. «نجس» (Najis/unclean). True purity is achieved under the shadow of faith.

2- A believer must feel aversion towards the polytheist. «الّذین آمنوا انّماالمشركون نجس»

3- Religious rulings must be implemented decisively, not with mere courtesy. «انّما المشركون نجس»

4- When announcing a program or viewpoint, we must pay attention to our capacity for implementation. «انّما المشركون نجس» (Although the impurity of the polytheists was not specific to the ninth year or the year of the declaration of disavowal (Bara'ah), this command was announced because the Muslims gained the power to implement it in that year.)

5- Since approaching is the prelude to entering, polytheists should not even come near sacred places. «لایقربوا»

6- In the Quran, the philosophy behind some prohibitions and bans has been stated. «نجس فلایقربوا»

7- Not all lands are equal in terms of sanctity. «فلایقربوا المسجدالحرام»

8- We should show forbearance towards opponents and grant them a grace period. «بعد عامهم هذا»

9- In instructions and regulations, we must also pay attention to potential tensions and their consequences. «نجس فلایقربوا... و ان خفتم عیلة...»

10- Economic hardships and financial pressures should not make us neglectful of our commitment to our religion. «ان خفتم عیلةً»

11- Wherever social anxiety is felt, the spirit of hope and reliance (Tawakkul) must be revived among the people. «ان خفتم عیلةً فسوف یغنیكم اللّه من فضله»

12- Our provision (Rizq) is not in the hands of others (travelers and tourists), but rather in the hands of Allah. «یغنیكم اللَّه»

13- We should not worry about cutting off economic ties with disbelievers for the sake of the faith. «یغنیكم اللّه»

14- More than cleverness, management, capital, etc., Divine Grace plays a role in the wealth of the people. «یغنیكم اللَّه من فضله»

15- The rulings of religion are based on Divine Knowledge and Wisdom. «علیم حكیم»

Ayah

قَـٰتِلُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَلَا بِٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَلَا يُحَرِّمُونَ مَا حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥ وَلَا يَدِينُونَ دِينَ ٱلْحَقِّ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ حَتَّىٰ يُعْطُوا۟ ٱلْجِزْيَةَ عَن يَدٍ وَهُمْ صَـٰغِرُونَ (۲۹)
Fight those who do not have faith in Allah nor [believe] in the Last Day, nor forbid what Allah and His Apostle have forbidden, nor practise the true religion, from among those who were given the Book, until they pay the tribute out of hand, degraded. (29)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the discussion was about the impurity of the polytheists and fighting them. In this verse, He explains the method of dealing with the People of the Book, who have one of two paths before them: war, or paying the Jizyah (poll tax). Just as Muslims help the Islamic government by paying Khums (one-fifth tax) and Zakat (alms) and benefit from security and services, the People of the Book also partially fund the budget spent on them by paying a sum called Jizyah. The amount of the Jizyah is determined by the leader of the Muslims. However, it must be an amount that is burdensome for the People of the Book to pay. «وهم صاغرون» (and they are humbled). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If the amount of the Jizyah is small, there will be no change in their morale when paying it, and they will not feel humiliated. [37] Although the People of the Book believe in God (Allah) and the Day of Judgment, the verse describes them as faithless regarding God and the Day of Judgment. This is because the Quran considers those who believe in some parts of the teachings and deny others to be disbelievers (Kafir). [38] Furthermore, if belief in the Origin (Allah) and the Return (Resurrection) is mixed with superstitions, it is considered tantamount to disbelief (Kufr). [39]

37) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 566. 38) یقولون نؤمن ببعضٍ و نكفر ببعض... اولئك هم الكافرون حقّا». An-Nisa, 150. 39) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- If the People of the Book do not act upon the commands of their own prophets, they are considered criminals. «قاتلوا الذین... لایحرّمون ما حرّم اللَّه»

2- The true religion is Islam alone; other religions are not true due to the distortions and superstitions that have been incorporated into them. «و لا یدینون دین الحق»

3- In dealing with the disbelievers among the People of the Book who do not embrace faith, there are two paths: war, or submission and payment of the Jizyah. «قاتلوا... حتّى یعطوا الجزیة»

4- Collecting the poll tax from the People of the Book is mandatory, and it must be done from a position of power and in cash, and they must pay it with humility and submission. «عن یدٍ، صاغرون»

5- The Islamic government must possess such power that others submit to it. «قاتلوا الذین لا یؤمنون ... و هم صاغرون»

6- Religious minorities must be submissive before the Islamic system. «صاغرون»

Ayah

وَقَالَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ عُزَيْرٌ ٱبْنُ ٱللَّهِ وَقَالَتِ ٱلنَّصَـٰرَى ٱلْمَسِيحُ ٱبْنُ ٱللَّهِ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ قَوْلُهُم بِأَفْوَٰهِهِمْ ۖ يُضَـٰهِـُٔونَ قَوْلَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ قَـٰتَلَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ أَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ (۳٠)
The Jews say, ‘Ezra is the son of Allah,’ and the Christians say, ‘Christ is the son of Allah.’ That is an opinion that they mouth, imitating the opinions of the faithless of former times. May Allah assail them, where do they stray?! (30)

Key Points

"Uzayr" (عُزیر) is the Arabized form of "Ezra" (عَزراء), just as "Isa" (عیسى) is the Arabized form of "Yeshua" (یَسوع), and "Yahya" (یحیى) is the Arabized form of "Yohanna" (یوحَنّا). Uzayr was one of the great scholars of the Jews who earned the title "Savior of the Jews." This was because, following the massacre of the people by Nebuchadnezzar, the destruction of the temples, the burning of the Torah, the captivity of the women, and the conquest of Babylon by Cyrus, Uzayr came to Cyrus and asked him to restore order and stability to the Jews. This Ayah is, in a way, an explanation and justification for the previous Ayah, which stated that the People of the Book do not believe in Allah and the Day of Judgment. Researchers believe that many of the teachings of the Torah and the Gospel have been mixed with the superstitions of the Buddhists, Brahmans, and Greeks. In fact, many stories in the Gospel are seen identically in the religions of Buddha and Brahmanism. Of course, today's Jews do not consider Uzayr to be the son of God, but they held this belief during the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله. When the Prophet asked them, "Since the status of Moses is higher, why do you not consider him the son of God?" they had no answer. [40]

40) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Although only a group of Jews considered Uzayr the son of God, because others remained silent, the deviation is attributed to all of them. «قالت الیهود»

2- One must not exaggerate (commit ghuluw) concerning religious and divine personalities. «عزیرابن اللّه... المسیح...»

3- The beliefs of the Jews and Christians are mixed with superstitions. «ذلك قولهم بافواههم»

4- The polytheists considered idols partners of Allah and angels the daughters of Allah; the Jews and Christians considered Uzayr and Isa (Jesus) the sons of God. In this respect, their speech resembled the speech of the disbelievers. «یضاهئون قول الذین كفروا»

5- The root of superstitions in the religions of Judaism and Christianity lies in the beliefs of previous disbelievers. [41] «یضاهئون قول الذین كفروا من قبل»

41) Today's researchers believe that many of the teachings of the Torah and the Gospel have been mixed with the superstitions of the Buddhists, Brahmans, and Greeks. In fact, many stories in the Gospel are seen identically in the religions of Buddha and Brahmanism. Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

ٱتَّخَذُوٓا۟ أَحْبَارَهُمْ وَرُهْبَـٰنَهُمْ أَرْبَابًا مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَسِيحَ ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَمَآ أُمِرُوٓا۟ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا ۖ لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۳۱)
They have taken their scribes and their monks as lords besides Allah, and also Christ, Mary’s son; though they were commanded to worship only the One God, there is no god except Him; He is far too immaculate to have any partners that they ascribe [to Him]! (31)

Key Points

Ahbār (اَحبار), the plural of Hibr (حِبر), means scholar, and Ruhbān (رُهبان), the plural of Rāhib (راهب), means one who renounces the world and lives in a monastery. Despite all their sanctity, they are servants of Allah, not objects of worship. Unconditional obedience to the scholars (Ahbār) and monks (Ruhbān) is a form of worshipping them. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «مَن اطاعَ رجلاً فى معصیة اللَّهِ فقد عَبَدَه» (Whoever obeys a man in the disobedience of Allah has worshipped him.) [42] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The People of the Book did not perform prayer or fasting for their scholars; rather, their scholars had made certain prohibitions lawful and certain lawful things prohibited, and the people followed them. [43] On the Day of Judgment, the polytheists will regret having equated Allah with other partners, and they will say: «تاللَّه اِن كنّا لفى ضلالٍ مبینٍ اذ نُسوّیكم بربّ العالمین» (By Allah, we were certainly in manifest error when we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.) [44]

42) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 43) Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 98. 44) Shu'ara, 98.

Messages

1- Unconditional obedience to anyone other than Allah is a form of worshipping others besides Him. «ما امروا الاّ لیعبدوا الهاً واحداً»

2- Worshipping humans in any form is polytheism (Shirk). No personality should become an idol. «اتّخذوا احبارهم و رهبانهم اربابا من دون اللَّه»

3- The infallible Prophet, whose entire thought and goal is Allah, has a separate standing from the scholars; hence, the name of the Messiah (Christ) is mentioned separately. «احبارهم و رهبانهم... و المسیح»

4- Loves, friendships, and obedience must have limits and boundaries. Any system, spiritual pole (Qutb), master (Murād), guide (Murshid), organizational or party obedience, etc., is Shirk if its source is not Divine Revelation and the command of Allah. «اتّخذوا... اربابا من دون اللَّه»

5- The danger of deviation from Monotheism (Tawhid) to Polytheism (Shirk) has always existed everywhere. «اتّخذوا... اربابا من دون اللَّه»

6- Only Allah has the right to legislate. Those who accept laws other than Allah's are outside the sphere of Islam. «اتّخذوا احبارهم و رهبانهم اربابا من دون اللَّه»

7- Exaggeration (Ghulūw) and excess regarding the Prophets, worshipping the Prophets, or considering them the son of God, is Shirk. «سبحانه عمّا یشركون»

Ayah

يُرِيدُونَ أَن يُطْفِـُٔوا۟ نُورَ ٱللَّهِ بِأَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَيَأْبَى ٱللَّهُ إِلَّآ أَن يُتِمَّ نُورَهُۥ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۳۲)
They desire to put out the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah is intent on perfecting His light though the faithless should be averse. (32)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Allah looked upon the inhabitants of the earth and chose me. Then He looked again and chose Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام. He is the light of the earth after me. Then the Prophet recited this Ayah. [45]

45) Bihar, Vol. 23, p. 320.

Messages

1- The conspiracy of the enemies is continuous and relentless. «یریدون» (the imperfect verb, is the symbol of continuity)

2- The rulings of Islam and the religion of Allah are light, and light is the source of life. «نوراللَّه» (The Light of Allah)

3- Although the groups of disbelievers (Kuffar) strive in various ways, their common goal is to extinguish the radiance of the religion. «یطفئوا نور اللَّه»

4- The efforts of the disbelievers in fighting the religion are like blowing upon the sun. [46] «یطفئوا نور اللَّه بافواههم» (that they may extinguish the Light of Allah with their mouths)

5- The greatest effort of the enemies of religion is through propaganda. «بافواههم» (with their mouths)

6- If the call of Islam from the tongue of the Prophet is light, its continuation in the form of Imamate is the completion of that light. [47] «یتمّ نوره» (He will perfect His Light)

7- Allah has promised victory to the followers of the truth. «ان یتمّ نوره ...» (that He will perfect His Light...)

8- Allah not only preserves the radiance of the religion but also expands it. «یتمّ نوره» (He will perfect His Light)

9- The disbelievers should know that they are opposing the definite Will of Allah for the victory of the true religion, and any effort against the school of Islam is doomed to failure. «یتمّ نوره»

10- Islam is an eternal school, «یتمّ نوره» (He will perfect His Light), and whoever opposes it is a disbeliever. «ولو كره الكافرون» (even though the disbelievers dislike it)

11- The Divine Will is aimed at satisfying His Prophet, even if the disbelievers dislike it. [48] «ولو كره الكافرون» (even though the disbelievers dislike it)

46) Nimrods, Pharaohs, Banu Umayyah, Banu Abbas, and their likes are practical examples of the efforts of the disbelievers.

47) Just as in the Ayah of the perfection of the religion, the issue of Imamate is considered the completion of the blessing: «الیوم اكملت لكم دینكم و اتممت علیكم نعمتى» (This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you). Al-Ma'idah, 3.

48) Also, in the story of the change of the Qiblah, Allah established a Qiblah that would satisfy the Prophet of Islam; «قبلةً ترضاها» (a Qiblah that will satisfy you) (Al-Baqarah, 144). And also: «ولسوف یعطیك ربّك فترضى» (And soon will your Lord give you so that you shall be well pleased) (Ad-Duha, 5).

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ رَسُولَهُۥ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ وَدِينِ ٱلْحَقِّ لِيُظْهِرَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلدِّينِ كُلِّهِۦ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُشْرِكُونَ (۳۳)
It is He who has sent His Apostle with the guidance and the religion of truth that He may make it prevail over all religions, though the polytheists should be averse. (33)

Key Points

This Ayah, in this exact form, has also appeared in two other Surahs besides this one: Surah Al-Fath, Ayah 28, and Surah As-Saff, Ayah 9. Although Islam has always been victorious in terms of logic and argumentation, this Ayah speaks of the apparent dominance and the promise of Islam's sovereignty over the world. Just as in other Ayahs, the word "ظَهَر" (Zahara) has been used to mean gaining dominance, such as: «اِن یظهروا علیكم یرجموكم» (If they gain dominance over you, they will stone you) [49]. And concerning the disbelievers, it is stated: «اِن یظهروا علیكم لایرقبوا فیكم اِلاًّ و لاذمّةً» [50] (If they gain dominance over you, they will not observe any covenant or treaty concerning you). On the one hand, this Ayah has not yet been fully realized, and on the other hand, Allah has promised the complete sovereignty of Islam, and His promise is not false. Consequently, as mentioned in numerous narrations, this Ayah refers to the advent (Zuhur) of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام. The issue of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام and His global uprising has been mentioned in many narrations through both Shi'a and Ahl al-Sunnah sources, and it is discussed in most general books of Hadith, being among the established tenets of Muslim belief. Although some, including the Wahhabis, have falsely claimed this belief is exclusive to the Shi'a. [51] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: A time will come when no one will remain except that he will acknowledge the prophethood of Hadrat Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. [52] There are many narrations concerning the sovereignty of the religion across the entire world; among them, it is narrated from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام that during the advent of the Imam of the Age عليه السلام, there will be no house and no village except that Islam will enter it, whether they wish it or not. And the sound of the Adhan (call to prayer) will be heard every morning and evening in every village. [53]

49) Kahf, 20. 50) Tawbah, 8. 51) Muntakhab al-Athar. 52) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 53) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The axis and foundation of the religion of Islam is truth (Haqq). «دین الحقّ» (All heavenly religions are true, but due to the emergence of distortion within them, their truthfulness has been compromised.)

2- Truth is victorious over falsehood. «لیظهره»

3- We must harmonize our will and movement with the Divine Will, otherwise we will be erased and destroyed. «لیظهره، ولو كره المشركون»

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلْأَحْبَارِ وَٱلرُّهْبَانِ لَيَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَٰلَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ يَكْنِزُونَ ٱلذَّهَبَ وَٱلْفِضَّةَ وَلَا يُنفِقُونَهَا فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَبَشِّرْهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۳٤)
O you who have faith! Indeed many of the scribes and monks wrongfully eat up the people’s wealth, and bar [them] from the way of Allah. Those who treasure up gold and silver, and do not spend it in the way of Allah, inform them of a painful punishment (34)

Key Points

The meaning of Ahbar (scholars) and Ruhban (monks) was discussed in Ayah 31. Dhahab (ذهب) means gold, and it is called Dhahab either because it quickly disappears (is lost) or because people rush towards it. Fiddah (فضّه) is called silver, either because it is quickly scattered, or because people are not attracted to it. Jewish scholars concealed the truth for the sake of their material interests and did not acknowledge the truthfulness of the religion of Islam, or they took bribes and forgave sins, or they engaged in inquisition and slandered others. The third Caliph wanted to remove the letter waw (و) from wa alladhīna (و الّذین) so that the Ayah would only describe the hoarding Ahbar and Ruhban, and not Muslims, but a number of people strongly protested. (54)

Zakat in Narrations They asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: On what amount of wealth does Zakat become obligatory? He replied: Zakat of the apparent or the hidden? They said: Both. He first stated the nisab (threshold) for the apparent Zakat, and then, in explaining the hidden Zakat, He said: «فلا تستأثر على اخیك بما هو احوج الیه منك» (Do not prefer yourself over your brother regarding that which he is more in need of than you are). (55) (Meaning:) That which your religious brother is more in need of than you, you should choose and prefer him. In another narration, He says: «انّما اعطاكم اللَّه هذه الفضول من الأموال لتوجّهوها حیث وجّهها اللَّه تعالى و لم تُعطِّلوها لتكنِزوها» (56) (Meaning:) Allah granted you this surplus wealth so that you may spend it in the path of His pleasure, not that you should hoard and treasure it. According to narrations, when Imam Zaman عليه السلام appears, He will forbid treasures and use all of them for His struggles. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «اِنّ اللَّه فرض على اغنیاء المسلمین فى اموالهم القدرَ الّذى یَسَعُ فقرائهم... اَلا انّ اللَّه یحاسبهم حساباً شدیداً و یعذّبهم عذابا الیما» (57) (Meaning:) Allah has made obligatory in the wealth of wealthy Muslims an amount of Zakat that suffices the poor. Be aware! Indeed, Allah will hold them to a severe reckoning if they do not fulfill their duty. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Any wealth on which Zakat is not paid is kanz (hoarded treasure), even if it is not hidden, or if it is other than gold and silver. (58)

Abu Dharr and the Ayah of Kanz Abu Dharr al-Ghifari, the noble Companion of the Prophet, in protest against the hoarding of wealth by Mu'awiyah, Uthman, and the government agents, and the squandering of public funds, would recite this Ayah loudly morning and evening before Mu'awiyah and then before Uthman, saying: This Ayah is not specific to those who withhold Zakat, but includes every type of wealth hoarding. It is mentioned in the noble Tafsir Al-Mizan that it is apparent from the confrontations of Abu Dharr with Uthman, Mu'awiyah, and Ka'b al-Ahbar that accumulating wealth in a poor society is forbidden (haram), even if it was acquired through lawful means and its Zakat has been paid. Of course, some attributed this to Abu Dharr's personal ijtihad (independent reasoning), but he himself said: «ما قلتُ لهم الاّ ما سَمعتُ من نبیّهم» (I have not told them anything except what I heard from their Prophet). Furthermore, Abu Dharr's frankness and honesty were confirmed by the Prophet. Among the outstanding aspects of Abu Dharr's life is this very act of Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy anil Munkar (enjoining good and forbidding evil) directed at the rulers regarding economic extravagance. His dispute with Uthman was not over wealth or position, but a protest against a social evil. Finally, Uthman exiled this pious and revolutionary Companion to Sham (Syria). From Sham, he was brought back to Medina in the worst condition, and then exiled to "Rabadhah," where he died unjustly in exile. And this was one of the unpleasant and shameful episodes of Uthman's rule. Others, in an attempt to exonerate Uthman, have tried to accuse Abu Dharr of being a socialist, holding communist ideas, and opposing private ownership. However, Allamah Amini قدس سره has an extensive discussion refuting this accusation in Al-Ghadir. (59) Abu Dharr's repeated exiles due to his disputes with the government and his protesting cries against Uthman's financial policy, Mu'awiyah's wealth hoarding, and Ka'b al-Ahbar's justifications, are recorded in the historical books of both Shia and Sunni scholars. (60) Although some have tried to justify this in some way, either by attributing these disputes to freedom of speech and belief during Uthman's era, or by considering the exile of that noble Companion based on the principle that "averting harm is more important than gaining benefit," thus viewing his presence in Medina and Sham as a harm and his exile as serving the public interest (61). However, this shameful behavior toward the sincere and pious companion of the Prophet, whose frank speech and sincere protest were motivated solely by the desire to fulfill his duty against deviations, accepts no justification or interpretation.

54) Tafsir Furqan. 55) Tafsir Safi. 56) Tafsir Safi. 57) Tafsir Safi. 58) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 59) Al-Ghadir, Vol. 8, p. 335. 60) Tafsir Namūneh. 61) Including: Tafsir Al-Manar, Vol. 10, pp. 406 - 407.

Messages

  1. Not all scholars of the People of the Book are bad. (62) «كثیراً من الأحبار»
  2. Misuse of positions is forbidden (haram), and the greatest danger for religious scholars is financial corruption. «لیأكلون اموال الناس بالباطل» (Those who are custodians of endowments and do not spend them according to the dedicated objectives are examples of this Ayah.)
  3. Scholars benefiting from people's wealth while failing to benefit the people leads to public apathy toward religion and is an instance of Sadd 'an Sabilillah (hindering from the path of Allah). «لیاكلون اموال الناس ... و یصدّون عن سبیل اللَّه»
  4. The Jewish and Christian scholars hindering people from the path of truth is due to their desire to attain worldly gain. «یكنزون... یصدّون»
  5. In Islam, there is no limit on capital, but the method of acquiring it has conditions, and hoarding wealth and misusing it are forbidden (haram). «الذین یكنزون... ولاینفقونها فى سبیل اللّه» (If hoarding wealth is forbidden, then hoarding necessities is certainly forbidden.)
  6. Wealth accumulation is a social calamity; worse than that is greed, and worse still is hoarding and concealing it, as it creates many problems for society. «الذین یكنزون... ولا ینفقونها»
  7. The worldliness of scholars and the wealth hoarding of the rich are causes of Divine wrath. «فبشّرهم...»
  8. Collecting gold, silver, and money, and failing to spend it (in charity) and hoarding it, is a major sin, because a promise of punishment has been given for it. «الذین یكنزون... عذاب الیم»

62) A group of them is praised in Ayah 82 of Surah Al-Ma'idah.

Ayah

يَوْمَ يُحْمَىٰ عَلَيْهَا فِى نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوَىٰ بِهَا جِبَاهُهُمْ وَجُنُوبُهُمْ وَظُهُورُهُمْ ۖ هَـٰذَا مَا كَنَزْتُمْ لِأَنفُسِكُمْ فَذُوقُوا۟ مَا كُنتُمْ تَكْنِزُونَ (۳٥)
on the day when these shall be heated in hellfire and therewith branded on their foreheads, their sides, and their backs [and told]: ‘This is what you treasured up for yourselves! So taste what you have treasured!’ (35)

Key Points

The word tukuwā (تُكوى), derived from the root kayy (كىّ), means applying a hot object to parts of the body.
