The selection of these three spots on the body for branding is either because heat penetrates the body faster from these three points, [63] or because the wealthy frown at the poor with their faces, show indifference with their sides (flanks), and turn their backs on them with their loins (backs). Or, alternatively, the face (صورت) refers to the front of the body, the loin (كمر) refers to the back of the body, and the side (پهلو) refers to the two flanks of the body. This means it is a metaphor for melting the entire body. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, citing this Ayah, considered the abandonment of Zakat (alms-tax) to be among the major sins. [64] In a narration, Abu Dharr says: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله stood beside the Ka'bah and said twice: «هم الأخسرون و ربّ الكعبة» (By the Lord of the Ka'bah, they are the most ruined/losers!). I asked: May my father and mother be sacrificed for you! Who do you mean? He replied: The hoarders of wealth. [65]

63) Tafsir-e Nemooneh. 64) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 65) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The God who resurrects humans in their original form can also present inanimate objects, gold, and silver in their worldly form. «هذا» (This).

2- Man's punishment and reward on the Day of Judgment are commensurate with his deeds in this world. «هذا ماكنزتم» (This is what you hoarded).

3- One of the tools of torment on the Day of Judgment is worldly wealth. «هذا ماكنزتم» (This is what you hoarded).

4- On the Day of Judgment, Allah removes the sweetness of wealth and riches from the taste of the hoarders. «فذوقوا» (So taste).

Ayah

إِنَّ عِدَّةَ ٱلشُّهُورِ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا فِى كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ مِنْهَآ أَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُمٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْقَيِّمُ ۚ فَلَا تَظْلِمُوا۟ فِيهِنَّ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۚ وَقَـٰتِلُوا۟ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ كَآفَّةً كَمَا يُقَـٰتِلُونَكُمْ كَآفَّةً ۚ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۳٦)
Indeed the number of months with Allah is twelve months in Allah’s Book, the day when He created the heavens and the earth. Of these, four are sacred. That is the upright religion. So do not wrong yourselves during them. Fight all the polytheists, just as they fight you all, and know that Allah is with the Godwary. (36)

Key Points

A year has twelve months, and the word "شَهر" (month) has also appeared twelve times in the Quran. [66] The number twelve, which has appeared repeatedly in the Quran, is a blessed number. The number of the leaders of the Children of Israel, the number of clear springs that gushed forth by the miracle of Moses (عليه السلام), the number of the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt, and so on, is twelve. The four months during which fighting is forbidden are: Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, and Rajab. The first three months are consecutive. [67] The system of the moon's movement around the Earth and the rotation within the solar system throughout the year, by Divine Will, has governed these celestial bodies since the beginning of creation. The sanctity of the sacred months remains as long as the enemy does not exploit them and does not launch an attack or invasion during these months. Otherwise, if the enemy attacks, defense is necessary. According to Ayah 194 of Surah Al-Baqarah: «والحُرمات قصاص، فمَن اعتَدى علیكم فاعتَدوا علیه بمثل ما اعتدى علیكم» (And the sacred things are subject to retaliation; so whoever commits aggression against you, retaliate against him with the like of what he committed against you), all things held sacred are subject to retaliation. Whoever abuses the law of non-aggression and transgresses against you, you should act against him in the same manner. The expression that this ordinance is fixed and enduring perhaps indicates that the sanctity of the four months has been constant in all heavenly religions (according to narrations). In some narrations from Imam Baqir (عليه السلام), the twelve Imams after the Prophet of Islam have been counted as the esoteric interpretation (ta’wil) of this Ayah. [68]

66) The numerical miracle of the Quran. 67) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 240. 68) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- The best calendar for history and timekeeping is one that is fixed, natural, and universal. Lunar months possess these three characteristics. «عدّة الشهور...»

2- The existence of the twelve months did not form gradually; rather, it has existed since the creation of the heavens and the earth. «اثنا عشر شهراً... یوم خَلق»

3- Combatants and their families need visitation, training, rest, and attention to their homes and lives. Therefore, one-third of the year, fighting must necessarily cease to provide an opportunity for these needs. «منها اربعة حرم» Even in war, principles must be observed: the ignorant, the deceived, women, the elderly, and children must not be attacked, and attacks must not be launched without prior justification and warning.

4- Not all times are equal. Sanctity and sacredness are relevant even in time. «منهااربعة حرم»

5- Islam has both a secure place (Mecca) and a secure time (the sacred months). It is interesting that the axis of three months (Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, and Muharram) out of these four sacred months is the month of Hajj (Dhul-Hijjah).

6- The harm of disregarding the sacred months returns to the people themselves. «فلا تظلموا فیهنّ انفسكم»

7- The principle in Islam is not war, but rather thought, proof, wisdom, admonition, and peaceful coexistence; however, if enemies resort to aggression, Muslims have the right to defend themselves, even during the sacred months. «قاتلوا المشركین»

8- Do not allow the enemy to exploit your religious sanctities and jurisprudential rulings. «قاتلوا المشركین كافه» If the enemy attacks during the sacred months, do not become sanctimonious; rather, retaliate.

9- Attacking the enemy must be commensurate with the dimensions of the enemy's attacks. «كمایقاتلونكم»

10- Victory is achieved through piety (taqwa). «ان اللَّه مع المتقین»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ٱلنَّسِىٓءُ زِيَادَةٌ فِى ٱلْكُفْرِ ۖ يُضَلُّ بِهِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُحِلُّونَهُۥ عَامًا وَيُحَرِّمُونَهُۥ عَامًا لِّيُوَاطِـُٔوا۟ عِدَّةَ مَا حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ فَيُحِلُّوا۟ مَا حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ زُيِّنَ لَهُمْ سُوٓءُ أَعْمَـٰلِهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۳۷)
Indeed nasee is an increase in unfaith, whereby the faithless are led [further] astray. They allow it in one year and forbid it another year, so as to fit in with the number, which Allah has made inviolable, thus permitting what Allah has forbidden. Their evil deeds appear to them as decorous, and Allah does not guide the faithless lot. (37)

Key Points

The terms Nasi' (نَسیى ء) and Nasi'ah (نِسیه) share the same root. To practice nasi'ah means to take goods and delay the payment of money. The act of shifting the days during which fighting is forbidden is called Nasi'. The disbelievers would sometimes act as reformers, tampering with the Divine law. In the heat of battle, when they encountered the Sacred Months, in order not to stop the fighting, they would say: We will continue, and instead of this month, we will declare a ceasefire in another month. [69]

69) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Let us respect the sacred things. Tampering with and playing games with Divine law is disbelief (kufr). «انّما النسى ء زیادة فى الكفر» (Indeed, the Nasi' is an increase in disbelief). The issue is not merely four months of cessation, but rather disobedience to what Allah has commanded is what is important.

2- Disbelief (kufr) has degrees and stages. «زیادة فى الكفر» (an increase in disbelief).

3- One of the factors leading to the misguidance of the disbelievers is the shifting of the Sacred Months. «یضلّ به الذین كفروا» (By which those who disbelieve are led astray).

4- One of the factors causing people's deviation is incorrect interpretations and analyses used to tamper with Divine rulings and laws. «یُحِلَّونه عاماً...»

5- Although the Arabs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) shifted the months, they still believed in the total number of months (that were sacred). «لیواطئوا عدّة ما حرّم اللّه»

6- He who considers his action bad might be guided, but he who sees his evil deeds as beautiful is not capable of guidance. «زیّن لهم سوء اعمالهم واللَّه لایهدى...» (The evil of their deeds has been made fair-seeming to them, and Allah does not guide...).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَا لَكُمْ إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمُ ٱنفِرُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ٱثَّاقَلْتُمْ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ أَرَضِيتُم بِٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا مِنَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ فَمَا مَتَـٰعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ (۳۸)
O you who have faith! What is the matter with you that when you are told: ‘Go forth in the way of Allah,’ you sink heavily to the ground? Are you pleased with the life of this world instead of the Hereafter? But the wares of the life of this world compared with the Hereafter are but insignificant. (38)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to the Battle of Tabuk, in which some showed reluctance in participating. The factors and excuses for this sluggishness were the great distance from Medina to Tabuk (about 600 kilometers), the heat of the weather, the harvest season, and the propaganda of the hypocrites regarding the greatness and power of the Roman army.

Messages

  1. Believers must also sometimes be subject to criticism and accountability. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا ما لكم...»

  2. Agility and combat readiness are essential for Muslims, but who the commander is, is not important. The phrase «اذا قیل لكم انفروا...» does not have a specified subject (agent) and is presented in the passive voice.

  3. Attachment to the world and materialism prevents man from separating himself from the earth and soil and reaching perfection and the heavens (celestial spheres). «اثّاقلتم...» (The seeker of the world is low in ambition).

  4. The value and status of man are such that the world is insignificant to him. «ارضیتم»

  5. He who considers the Hereafter superior to the meager world turns towards the battlefield, not the world. «الاّ قلیل»

Ayah

إِلَّا تَنفِرُوا۟ يُعَذِّبْكُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا وَيَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ وَلَا تَضُرُّوهُ شَيْـًٔا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۳۹)
If you do not go forth, He will punish you with a painful punishment, and replace you with another people, and you will not hurt Him in the least, and Allah has power over all things. (39)

Key Points

In this Ayah and the preceding Ayah, the issue of Jihad is emphasized through eight means: You who are believers, why the weakness? («یا ایها الذین آمنوا»), a question accompanied by reproach («ما لَكم»), Jihad is the way of Allah («سبیل اللَّه»), the command to move out («انفروا»), the threat of punishment («یعذّبكم»), the threat of replacement («یستبدل»), the threat that your abandonment of the front will be ineffective (i.e., will not harm Allah) («لاتضرّوه»), and the threat related to Divine power. («واللَّه على كلّ شى ءقدیر»)

Based on some narrations, the people meant by the replacement individuals in («قوماً غیركم») are people from Iran and Yemen who will rise up to aid the religion of Allah. [70]

70) Tafasir Namuneh, Kanz al-Daqaiq.

Messages

1- The penalty for abandoning the front is both worldly punishment and humiliation, and the torment of Hellfire in the Hereafter. («عذاباً الیما و یستبدل قوماً غیركم»)

2- The Hand of Allah is open to replace others in our stead, and whenever He wills, He will do so. («یستبدل»)

3- The harm of inaction and stagnation reaches us, not Allah. («لاتضرّوه شیئاً») (Weakness and laziness cause others to be replaced in our stead.)

4- It is the Sunnah (way) of Allah that if the performance of a people weakens, He replaces them with another people. («یستبدل») The imperfect verb (present/future tense) indicates continuity.

5- The unlimited power of Allah enables Him to replace others in our stead. («یستبدل... على كلّ شى ء قدیر»)

6- He who possesses infinite power is never harmed or injured. («لاتضرّوه شیئاً... على كلّ شى ء قدیر»)

Ayah

إِلَّا تَنصُرُوهُ فَقَدْ نَصَرَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِذْ أَخْرَجَهُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ثَانِىَ ٱثْنَيْنِ إِذْ هُمَا فِى ٱلْغَارِ إِذْ يَقُولُ لِصَـٰحِبِهِۦ لَا تَحْزَنْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَنَا ۖ فَأَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُۥ عَلَيْهِ وَأَيَّدَهُۥ بِجُنُودٍ لَّمْ تَرَوْهَا وَجَعَلَ كَلِمَةَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلسُّفْلَىٰ ۗ وَكَلِمَةُ ٱللَّهِ هِىَ ٱلْعُلْيَا ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (٤٠)
If you do not help him, then Allah certainly helped him when the faithless expelled him, as one of two [refugees], when the two of them were in the cave, he said to his companion, ‘Do not grieve; Allah is indeed with us.’ Then Allah sent down His composure upon him, and strengthened him with hosts you did not see, and He made the word of the faithless the lowest; and the word of Allah is the highest; and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (40)

Key Points

The Ayah refers to the dangerous plot of the polytheists to kill the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله). In the story of "Laylat al-Mabit" (The Night of the Stay), a person from every tribe was prepared, and they decided to kill the Prophet at night. That حضرت (His Holiness) made Ali ibn Abi Talib (عليه السلام) sleep in his place and went towards the Cave of Thawr at night, accompanied by Abu Bakr. The disbelievers pursued the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) until they reached the mouth of the cave, but upon seeing the spider's web over the cave, they turned back and left. The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) departed for Medina after three days. During that time, Abu Bakr's servant, (Amir ibn Fuhayrah), brought them food, and Ali (عليه السلام) prepared the arrangements for the journey to Medina. After three days, three camels were prepared, and the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله), Abu Bakr, and the guide set off for Medina. [71]

A Few Questions and Answers

Question: Was Abu Bakr's companionship intended to defend the life of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله)? Answer: What defense could one old man offer against the general mobilization of the polytheists?

Question: Does the application of the word "companion of the Prophet" (sahib) to Abu Bakr in the Ayah not indicate his worthiness for the succession (Caliphate) of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله)? Answer: The word sahib (companion) does not imply worthiness. Sometimes two people with two different thoughts, tastes, and methods become companions. As we read in Ayah 37 of Surah Al-Kahf: «قال له صاحبه وهو یحاوره» (His companion said to him while he was conversing with him), even though there was a great difference between those two people.

Question: Is the fact that, among all the companions, only Abu Bakr was with the Prophet not the highest value for him? Answer: That same night, Ali (عليه السلام) also slept in the Prophet's bed. Abu Bakr was with the Prophet that night due to a duty. Two people performed two different duties.

Question: Was Abu Bakr's fear for his own life or for the life of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله)? Answer: Even if he feared for the Prophet's life, it is the duty of every Muslim to be concerned about the leader's condition in times of danger.

Question: The Companions of the Cloak (Ahl al-Kisa), who possess virtue, were five people, and the Companions of the Cave were two. Is this not more exclusive and important? Answer: The prayers (supplications) of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) for the Companions of the Cloak, who were his Ahl al-Bayt (Household), and the obligation upon Muslims to send blessings (Salawat) upon them in the tashahhud of every prayer—every night and day, always and everywhere—separates the account of these two scenes.

Question: In this Ayah, the Prophet says: «انّ اللَّه معنا» (Indeed, Allah is with us), so Allah is with Abu Bakr. Other Ayahs state that Allah is with the righteous (muttaqin) and the doers of good (muhsinin). Therefore, is he also among the muttaqin and muhsinin? Answer: As long as a person remains within the orbit of goodness (ihsan) and piety (taqwa), Allah is with him. But if he leaves that orbit, he is not covered by Allah's grace. One must examine where the path of his movements, stances, and reactions to Ghadir Khumm and the allegiance (Bay'ah) ultimately led.

Question: The Ayah says: Allah sent down tranquility (sakinah) upon him (Abu Bakr). Is this not a distinction? Answer: In this Ayah, there are five pronouns, all of which refer back to the Prophet. How could the pronoun «علیه» (upon him) refer back to Abu Bakr?!

[71] Tafsir Al-Mizan and Durr al-Manthur.

Messages

  1. Islam is not dependent on our support; the Protector of Islam is Allah. Therefore, let us not be arrogant about our own help. «الاّ تنصروه»
  2. Allah guaranteed the victory of the Muslims in Tabuk. «الاّ تنصروه فقد نصره اللّه»
  3. The victories of the Muslims are an example of Divine power. «على كلّ شى ء قدیر الاّ تنصروه فقد نصره اللّه»
  4. The Prophet's departure from Mecca was due to the atmosphere of intimidation and pressure exerted by the disbelievers. «اخرجه الذین كفروا»
  5. If Allah wills, He protects the noblest of creation (the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله) with the weakest of houses (the spider's web). «اذ هما فى الغار...»
  6. Tranquility (sakinah) and assurance are a Divine gift and cannot be achieved through material means. «فانزل اللّه سكینته»
  7. Angels aid the believers by the command of Allah. «ایّده بجنودٍ لم تروها»
  8. Migration (Hijra) is the cause of the glory of Islam and the collapse of disbelief. «اخرجه... كلمة اللَّه هى العلیا»
  9. The Will of Allah is superior to every will and desire. «كلمة اللَّه هى العلیا»
  10. Arrogant forces, despite all their expertise and resources, are helpless against believers who possess tranquility and assurance. [72] «جعل كلمة الذین كفروا السفلى »
  11. The glory and elevation of the Word of Allah (Kalimatullah) and the neutralization of the plots of the disbelievers are manifestations of Allah's Might and Wisdom. «فقد نصره اللّه... انّ اللّه عزیز حكیم»

[72] Reference to Ayah 30 of Surah Al-Anfal: «واذ یمكر بك الذّین كفروا ...»

Ayah

ٱنفِرُوا۟ خِفَافًا وَثِقَالًا وَجَـٰهِدُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (٤۱)
Go forth, whether [armed] lightly or heavily, and wage jihad with your possessions and persons in the way of Allah. That is better for you, should you know. (41)

Key Points

The meaning of the two words khifāf (خِفاف) and thiqāl (ثِقال) is one of these contrasting meanings: single and married, poor and rich, pedestrian and mounted, small family and large family, unburdened and burdened, trade and agriculture. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of khifāf and thiqāl is the old and the young. [73] In the commentary Fi Zilal al-Qur'an, it is mentioned: Individuals such as Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, Miqdad, and Abu Talhah would prepare for the battlefield in their old age. When they were told that they were elderly, they would recite this Ayah in response.

73) بحار، ج ۹۷، ص ۴۸.

Messages

1- Do not create obstacles in the face of the general mobilization command for the battlefield. «انفروا خفافاً وثقالاً» (Life's difficulties are not an excuse for failing to participate in Jihad.)

2- Sometimes the mobilization of all people against the enemy is necessary, even if military equipment is scarce. «انفروا خفافاً وثقالاً»

3- The ideology (faith) is more valuable than wealth and life. Everything is sacrificed for the ideology, both wealth and life. «اموالكم و انفسكم» and He did not say: «اموالكم او انفسكم»

4- For the poor, Jihad with life is obligatory, and for the rich, Jihad with wealth and life is obligatory. «اموالكم و انفسكم»

5- The criterion for values is sincerity. «فى سبیل اللّه»

6- Divine decrees, including the command for Jihad, are aimed at the real benefits and interests of humanity. «ذلكم خیر لكم»

7- People do not know their true interests. «خیر لكم ان كنتم تعلمون»

8- Attaining true values is possible in the light of knowledge and recognition. «ذلكم خیر لكم ان كنتم تعلمون»

Ayah

لَوْ كَانَ عَرَضًا قَرِيبًا وَسَفَرًا قَاصِدًا لَّٱتَّبَعُوكَ وَلَـٰكِنۢ بَعُدَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلشُّقَّةُ ۚ وَسَيَحْلِفُونَ بِٱللَّهِ لَوِ ٱسْتَطَعْنَا لَخَرَجْنَا مَعَكُمْ يُهْلِكُونَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (٤۲)
Were it an accessible gain or a short journey, they would have surely followed you; but the distance seemed too far to them. Yet they will swear by Allah: ‘If we could, we would have surely gone forth with you.’ They [merely] destroy themselves. Allah knows that they are indeed liars. (42)

Key Points

This Ayah is among the unseen reports of the Quran, which informs about an event before its occurrence, and it relates to the Battle of Tabuk, because the region of Tabuk was very far from Medina. Currently, Tabuk is located on the border of Hijaz. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of «عرَضاً قریباً» (a near gain) is a nearby spoil (or booty). (74)

74) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Worldly wealth and spoils are transient goods. «عرضاً»

2- War is a good criterion for testing people. «لو كان عرضاً... لاتّبعوك»

3- Exposing the spirit and inner nature of the hypocrites is necessary. «لو كان عرضاً... لاتّبعوك»

4- Hypocrites participate in some battles to achieve material benefits. «لو كان عرضاً... لاتّبعوك»

5- Accepting easy and highly profitable tasks is not a sign of maturity or self-sacrifice. «لاتّبعوك»

6- Being a Muslim is incompatible with self-indulgence and luxury-seeking. «بَعُدَت علیهم الشقه»

7- Lying and hypocrisy are inseparable, and hypocrites swear falsely to evade work. «سیحلفون»

8- False swearing is a hypocritical act. «سیحلفون»

9- The consequences of abandoning Jihad cannot be eliminated through improper justification and lies; rather, wretchedness and destruction are its definite outcomes. «یهلكون انفسهم»

10- The consequences of fleeing the battlefield afflict the fugitives themselves. «یهلكون انفسهم»

Ayah

عَفَا ٱللَّهُ عَنكَ لِمَ أَذِنتَ لَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكَ ٱلَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا۟ وَتَعْلَمَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (٤۳)
May Allah excuse you! Why did you grant them leave [to stay behind] before those who told the truth were evident to you and you had ascertained the liars? (43)

Key Points

It seems some hypocrites came to the Prophet without a valid excuse and offered apologies for not going to the Tabuk front. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله granted them permission. This Ayah implicitly censures this granting of permission so that leave of absence is reserved for those with genuine excuses, not for those seeking pretexts. In the next Ayah, it is mentioned that Allah has taken away the success (Tawfiq) of going to the front from the hypocrites, "كره اللَّه انبعاثهم..." (Allah disliked their mobilization...). Therefore, the Prophet's permission was under the shadow of Allah's dislike for the hypocrites participating in the war, and the criticism is for exposing them sooner (than necessary). Furthermore, in Ayah 47, the harmfulness of the hypocrites' presence at the front is also discussed. Thus, this permission was neither a sin nor a sign of the Prophet's mismanagement.

Messages

1- Management and planning must be learned from Allah, Whose criticism is alongside forgiveness and mercy. "عفى اللَّه عنك لم اذنت لهم"

2- During military operations, leave of absence is forbidden. "لِمَ اذنت لهم"

3- Close the paths of pretext-seeking to the hypocrites. "لِمَ اذنت لهم"

4- Non-participation in war requires the leader's permission. "لِمَ اذنت لهم"

5 - War is a suitable means for recognizing the true nature of individuals. "حتّى یتبیّن..."

6- Concealing faults is not always a virtue; sometimes, exposure is necessary. "حتّى یتبیّن... وتعلم الكاذبین"

7- In recognizing hypocrites, one must examine their performance during times of war and Jihad. "و تعلم الكاذبین"

8 - The Islamic society must move so sincerely that the hypocrites are exposed. "یتبیّن ... وتعلم الكاذبین"

9- The presence of hypocrites at the front is not a virtue, but their flight from the front leads to their disgrace. "تعلم الكذبین"

Ayah

لَا يَسْتَـْٔذِنُكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ أَن يُجَـٰهِدُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤٤)
Those who believe in Allah and the Last Day do not ask you for leave [exempting them] from waging jihad with their possessions and their persons, and Allah knows best the Godwary. (44)

Key Points

The true believer, who is devoted to Jihad and martyrdom, does not fear death and does not seek permission for exemption. Just as there were people in early Islam who insisted on asking the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to dispatch them to the front, and when the Prophet refused them due to lack of resources, they wept out of distress. [75] When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله set out for Tabuk, He left Ali عليه السلام as His successor in Medina. Ali عليه السلام was affected (by this), so the Prophet consoled him and said: "Your position to me is like the position of Aaron to Moses."

75) Tawbah, 92.

Messages

1- The believer does not flee from duty. «لایستأذنك الذین یؤمنون»

2- Those who lagged behind from the Battle of Tabuk were a group lacking faith. «لایستأذنك الذین یؤمنون»

3- Faith in the Origin (Allah) and the Resurrection (the Hereafter) is the main factor for piety, seeking martyrdom, and presence on the fronts. «یؤمنون باللَّه والیوم الاخر»

4- True Mujahideen must sacrifice both with their wealth and their lives. «باموالهم و انفسهم»

5- Piety prevents lagging behind from Jihad with wealth and life. «ان یجاهدوا... واللَّه علیم بالمتّقین»

6- Belief in Allah's knowledge is a source of encouragement in Jihad. «ان یجاهدوا... واللَّه علیم بالمتّقین»

7- The pious person (Muttaqi) must be recognized in war and on the front, not at home or during times of peace. «واللَّه علیم بالمتّقین»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا يَسْتَـْٔذِنُكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَٱرْتَابَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ فَهُمْ فِى رَيْبِهِمْ يَتَرَدَّدُونَ (٤٥)
Only those seek a leave [of exemption] from you who do not believe in Allah and the Last Day, and whose hearts are in doubt, so they waver in their doubt. (45)

Key Points

| Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Whoever remains in doubt and uncertainty, the devils will crush and trample him. [76]

76) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 31.

Messages

1- War is a testing ground for beliefs and convictions. «یستئذنك الذین لایؤمنون باللَّه»

2- Since the hypocrites lack faith in the goal, they seek excuses, escape, and permission to be excused (or leave). «لا یؤمنون باللَّه»

3- The main factor for presence on the front line or flight from war is the existence or non-existence of faith. «یؤمنون، لا یؤمنون»

4- Worse than doubt is the continuation and persistence in skepticism. «فى ریبهم یتردّدون»

Ayah

۞ وَلَوْ أَرَادُوا۟ ٱلْخُرُوجَ لَأَعَدُّوا۟ لَهُۥ عُدَّةً وَلَـٰكِن كَرِهَ ٱللَّهُ ٱنۢبِعَاثَهُمْ فَثَبَّطَهُمْ وَقِيلَ ٱقْعُدُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلْقَـٰعِدِينَ (٤٦)
Had they desired to go forth, they would have surely made some preparations for it; but Allah was averse to arouse them, so He held them back, and it was said [to them], ‘Be seated with those who sit back.’ (46)

Key Points

Allah's prevention and restraint from participating in the front (of war) and Jihad means the withdrawal of divine success (Tawfiq), not a practical prohibition.

Messages

1- The hypocrites never intend to go to the front. «ولو ارادوا...لاعدّوا»

2- The prerequisite for an obligation is itself obligatory. «لو ارادوا...لاعدّوا»

3- The believer is prepared for Jihad and awaits going to the front, not indifferent or unmotivated. «لاعدّوا له عدّة»

4- Participation in Jihad is a divine success (Tawfiq) that has been withdrawn from the unworthy. «فثبّطهم»

5- Those who abandon war and Jihad must be humiliated. «اقعدوا مع القاعدین»

Ayah

لَوْ خَرَجُوا۟ فِيكُم مَّا زَادُوكُمْ إِلَّا خَبَالًا وَلَأَوْضَعُوا۟ خِلَـٰلَكُمْ يَبْغُونَكُمُ ٱلْفِتْنَةَ وَفِيكُمْ سَمَّـٰعُونَ لَهُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤۷)
Had they gone forth with you, they would have only added to your troubles, and they would have surely spread rumours in your midst, seeking to cause sedition among you. They have some spies among you, and Allah knows best the wrongdoers. (47)

Key Points

| «خَبال» (khabāl) means anxiety and doubt. «خَبَل» (khabal) means madness, and «خَبْل» (khabl) means corruption. «اَوضعوا» (awḍa‘ū) is derived from «اِیضاع» (īḍā‘), meaning speed in movement and penetration. «فِتنة» (fitnah) here means discord and misguidance. «سَمّاع» (sammā‘) means a spy who spreads rumors according to the interests of the enemy.

Messages

1- The forces of the army of Islam must be select and pure; the number of individuals and quantity is not the criterion. «لو خرجوا... مازادوكم الاّ خبالاً»

2- Allah reassures the believers not to worry because a group of hypocrites stayed behind from the front. «لو خرجوا... مازادوكم الاّ خبالاً»

3- The presence of hypocrites on the battlefronts is a factor that weakens morale, causes discord, and creates doubt. «لاوضعوا خلالكم»

4- The movements of the hypocrites are very swift. «اوضعوا»

5- Not all Muslims perceive the danger posed by the hypocrites, and some naive, gullible people are influenced by them. «سمّاعون لهم»

6- The hypocrite is an oppressor (ẓālim), and Allah has warned them. «واللّه علیم بالظّالمین»

Ayah

لَقَدِ ٱبْتَغَوُا۟ ٱلْفِتْنَةَ مِن قَبْلُ وَقَلَّبُوا۟ لَكَ ٱلْأُمُورَ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَظَهَرَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ وَهُمْ كَـٰرِهُونَ (٤۸)
They certainly sought to cause sedition earlier, and upset the matters for you, until the truth came and Allah’s command prevailed, much as they were averse. (48)

Messages

1- The hypocrites pursue sedition with deliberate intention and motive. «ابتغوا»

2- In cases where division and sedition arise, search for the trace of the hypocrites. «ابتغوا الفتنة»

3- Pay attention to the background of individuals who have not truly repented. «من قبل»

4- Distorting realities and providing fake reports to the leader is a hypocritical act. «قلّبوا لك الامور»

5- The leader of the Islamic Ummah must be vigilant and should not trust every report. «قلّبوا لك الامور»

6- Sedition and sowing division have always been the continuous work of the hypocrites, and they will cause mischief until the definite victory of the front of truth. «ابتغوا الفتنة من قبل... حتّى جاء الحق»

7- Divine assistance ruins the plans of the hypocrites. Despite all the seditions raised against Islam, Islam experiences growth instead of decline. «جاءالحق وظهر امر اللَّه»

8- The hearts of the hypocrites never submit to the Islamic system. «كارهون»

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ ٱئْذَن لِّى وَلَا تَفْتِنِّىٓ ۚ أَلَا فِى ٱلْفِتْنَةِ سَقَطُوا۟ ۗ وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمُحِيطَةٌۢ بِٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٤۹)
Among them there are some who say, ‘Give me leave, and do not put me to temptation.’ Look! They have already fallen into temptation and indeed hell besieges the faithless. (49)

Key Points

One of the elders of the Banu Salama tribe, who was among the hypocrites, asked the Messenger of Allah for permission not to join the Battle of Tabuk. His excuse was that if his eyes fell upon the Roman women, he would be tempted and fall into sin. The Prophet granted permission. This Ayah was revealed and declared him sinful and fallen into temptation (fitnah) due to his failure to participate in the front. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله removed him from the leadership of the tribe and appointed Bushr ibn Bara', who was generous and good-natured, in his place. [77]

77) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Commandership-in-chief is one of the responsibilities of the Prophet and the leader in the Islamic society. «منهم من یقول ائذن لى»

2- The impolite hypocrites considered the Messenger of Allah to be the cause of temptation (fitnah). «ائذن لى و لاتفتنّى»

3- In the view of the hypocrites, striving (Jihad) in the way of Allah is a calamity and distress. «و لا تفتنّى»

4- War is the crucible of temptation and trial (fitnah and test). «و لا تفتنّى»

5- Some hypocrites use religious titles and issues to deceive the pious. «و لا تفتنّى» (Under the pretext of not looking at Roman girls, they violate the command of Allah and His Messenger!)

6- Those who are worried about and flee from the temptation of war will fall into a greater temptation. «اَلا فى الفتنة سقطوا»

7- Fleeing from the Divine test is impossible. [78] Although they said: «لاتفتّنى» (Do not tempt me), they fell into temptation. «فى الفتنة سقطوا»

8- Hypocritical excuse-making to flee from Jihad leads to disbelief (kufr). «ائذن لى... كافرین»

9- The encompassing of Hellfire around the disbelievers is due to the encompassing of sin around their existence. [79] «محیطة»

78) اَحَسِبَ الناس ان یُتركوا ان یقولوا آمنّا وهم لایُفتنون». العنكبوت، 2.

79) In another place we read: »مَن كَسب سیّئةً واحاطت به خطیئتُه فاولئك اصحاب النّار« البقرة، 81.

Ayah

إِن تُصِبْكَ حَسَنَةٌ تَسُؤْهُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْكَ مُصِيبَةٌ يَقُولُوا۟ قَدْ أَخَذْنَآ أَمْرَنَا مِن قَبْلُ وَيَتَوَلَّوا۟ وَّهُمْ فَرِحُونَ (٥٠)
If some good should befall you, it upsets them; but if an adversity befalls you, they say, ‘We had already taken our precautions in advance,’ and they go away boasting. (50)

Messages

1- The Prophet and the leader are the image of the Islamic system and the Ummah. «تُصبك» instead of «تصبكم».

2- Whoever is distressed by the success of others possesses envy and a hypocritical disposition. «ان تصبك حسنة تسؤهم»

3- We must recognize our friends and enemies during hardships and bitter and sweet events. «ان تصبك حسنة تسؤهم...»

4- In the wars of the Prophet, there was both victory and defeat. «حسنة...مصیبة»

5- Opportunistic hypocrites exploit the misfortunes of Muslims for their own objectives and promote their own shrewdness and foresight. «اخذنا امرنا من قبل»

Ayah

قُل لَّن يُصِيبَنَآ إِلَّا مَا كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَنَا هُوَ مَوْلَىٰنَا ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۱)
Say, ‘Nothing will befall us except what Allah has ordained for us. He is our master, and in Allah let all the faithful put their trust.’ (51)

Messages

1- The leader and the community share in sorrow and joy. The previous Ayah used "تصبك" (it afflicts you), and here it uses "یصیبنا" (it afflicts us).

2- We are commanded to fulfill our duty (taklif), not guaranteed the result. We strive (perform Jihad), but destiny is in the hands of Allah. "الاّ ما كتب اللَّه لنا" (except what Allah has decreed for us).

3- True believers see even the hardships of the battlefield as being in line with their own interests and welfare. "كتب اللَّه لنا" (Allah has decreed for us).

4- Human destiny is predetermined (written beforehand). "كتب اللَّه لنا" (Allah has decreed for us).

5- Whatever Allah has decreed for the believer is good, because a Master (Mawla) does not decree ill for His servant. "كتب اللَّه لنا هو مولینا" (Allah has decreed for us; He is our Protector/Master).

6- The believer considers himself to be under the guardianship (Wilayah) of Allah. " هو مولینا" (He is our Protector/Master).

7- The highest degree of Monotheism (Tawhid) is moving within the orbit of the Divine law and entrusting one's destiny to the wise God. "هو مولینا" (He is our Protector/Master).

8- One must rely only upon Allah. "على اللَّه" (upon Allah).

9- The condition of faith (Iman) is reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul). "فلیتوكّل المؤمنون" (let the believers rely).

Ayah

قُلْ هَلْ تَرَبَّصُونَ بِنَآ إِلَّآ إِحْدَى ٱلْحُسْنَيَيْنِ ۖ وَنَحْنُ نَتَرَبَّصُ بِكُمْ أَن يُصِيبَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِعَذَابٍ مِّنْ عِندِهِۦٓ أَوْ بِأَيْدِينَا ۖ فَتَرَبَّصُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مَعَكُم مُّتَرَبِّصُونَ (٥۲)
Say, ‘Do you not await one of the two excellent things to happen to us? But we await that Allah shall visit on you a punishment, from Him, or by our hands. So wait! We too are waiting along with you.’ (52)

Key Points

This Ayah is an explanation of the previous Ayah, which declared Allah as the Protector (Mawla) of the believers—a God Who decrees nothing but good, and even if He ordains martyrdom, it is good and virtuous. Imam Baqir عليه السلام, in interpreting "احدى الحسنیین" (one of the two best things), said: The meaning is either death in the way of Allah or witnessing the advent (ظهور) of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام. (80)

80) كافى، ج 8، ص 286.

Messages

1- The perspective of "احدى الحسنیین" (one of the two best things) must be explained and propagated to both friends and enemies. "قل" (Say).

2- Values and criteria for evaluation differ between the outlook of believers and hypocrites. In the view of the people of faith regarding "احدى الحسنیین," leaving (dying) or staying (living) is not important; what is important is being on the right path. Therefore, victory or martyrdom are both triumphs.

3- If hypocrites are killed in war and conflict with the Islamic system, they are destined for Hellfire, and even if they die without fighting, they are people of Hellfire. Thus, they will be afflicted by punishment either at the hands of the believers or by Allah. "من عنده او بایدینا" (from Him or by our hands).

4- The believer has certainty regarding the goal and the outcome of the action. "فتربّصوا انّا معكم متربّصون" (So wait; indeed, we are waiting with you).

Ayah

قُلْ أَنفِقُوا۟ طَوْعًا أَوْ كَرْهًا لَّن يُتَقَبَّلَ مِنكُمْ ۖ إِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ قَوْمًا فَـٰسِقِينَ (٥۳)
Say, ‘Spend willingly or unwillingly, it shall never be accepted from you; for you are indeed a transgressing lot.’ (53)

Key Points

According to the commentaries, the hypocrites who did not participate in the Battle of Tabuk wanted to consider themselves partners in the victory by providing financial aid to the front. The non-acceptance of the hypocrites' charity (infaq) either means that financial aid will not be received from them in this world, or that they will have no reward in the Hereafter.

Messages

  1. Charity (Infaq) is not solely for filling stomachs; the reformation of the soul and spiritual growth are also intended by Islam. «انفقوا طوعاً او كرهاً»
  2. The spirit, intention (niyyah), and inner state (batin) of individuals affect the value of their deeds. The hypocrite who is displeased by the victory of the Muslims and happy when they suffer harm, his deeds are ineffective due to this inner corruption. «انفقوا... لن یُتقبّل»
  3. The services and charities (infaq) of the hypocrites are worthless and will be nullified (habṭ). «انفقوا...لن یُتقبّل»
  4. We should not accept every aid from everyone. «لن یتقبّل منكم»
  5. The condition for the acceptance of deeds is piety (taqwa) and purity of heart, and political, social, devotional, and moral issues are interconnected. «لن یُتقبّل... كنتم قوماً فاسقین»
  6. Transgression (fisq) prevents the acceptance of deeds. «انّكم ... فاسقین»

Ayah

وَمَا مَنَعَهُمْ أَن تُقْبَلَ مِنْهُمْ نَفَقَـٰتُهُمْ إِلَّآ أَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَبِرَسُولِهِۦ وَلَا يَأْتُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ إِلَّا وَهُمْ كُسَالَىٰ وَلَا يُنفِقُونَ إِلَّا وَهُمْ كَـٰرِهُونَ (٥٤)
Nothing stops their charities from being accepted except that they have no faith in Allah and His Apostle and do not perform the prayer but lazily, and do not spend but reluctantly. (54)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, it was stated hypothetically that if the hypocrites spend (in charity), whether willingly or unwillingly, it will not be accepted. Here, the actual situation and external reality are stated: that their spending is done out of reluctance, not willingness.

The following are among the advantages of the Islamic government's revenues (such as Khums, Zakat, Sadaqat, and general spending/donations) over other governmental revenues: 1- Individuals pay willingly, by choice, and based on religious conscience. 2- They pay without fear and with the intention of seeking closeness (to Allah). 3- They consider these financial payments as spoils (of war/gain) and a provision for the Day of Judgment. 4- They choose a just scholar (or leader) as the recipient of the wealth. 5- They know and monitor the area of expenditure. 6- They consider simple living a condition for the recipient and thank Allah.

Messages

1- Good deeds are not accepted when accompanied by disbelief (kufr), laziness, and reluctance. «ما منعهم ان تقبل منهم نفقاتهم...»

2- Allah has repeatedly considered the hypocrite a disbeliever (Kafir) in the Quran. «انّهم كفروا»

3- Those who lagged behind from the Battle of Tabuk were outwardly Muslims but inwardly disbelievers. «انّهم كفروا»

4- What gives value to actions is intention, vitality, and love. Yes, motivation is important, not physical movements! [81] «كُسالى , كارهون»

5- The goal of spending (in charity) in Islam is selective and spiritual growth, not merely filling stomachs. Because while the help of hypocrites may fill stomachs, spiritual growth is not achieved for the helper. «كفروا باللّه... و هم كارهون»

6- Among the signs of hypocrisy are prayer performed lazily, «كُسالى », and spending done reluctantly. «كارهون»

81) In the story of Joseph (Yusuf), Zulaikha closed all the doors with her own hands and desired Joseph. But Joseph عليه السلام fled toward the door, and the woman ran after him. In this scene, Joseph and Zulaikha were both running in terms of outward appearance and physical movement, but the motivation of one was fleeing from sin, and the motivation of the other was committing sin. This is the meaning of the role of intention and motivation in human actions.

Ayah

فَلَا تُعْجِبْكَ أَمْوَٰلُهُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُهُمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُم بِهَا فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَتَزْهَقَ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَهُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (٥٥)
So let not their wealth and children impress you: Allah only desires to punish them with these in the life of this world, and that their souls may depart while they are faithless. (55)

Key Points

"Tazhaq" (تَزهَق) means exiting accompanied by difficulty, regret, and sorrow. Allah's prohibition against astonishment does not prove that such astonishment existed in the Prophet, but rather serves a preventative purpose. In Nahj al-Balaghah, the moments of death for a group are depicted where their wealth appears before their eyes, and they suffer torment because they are leaving it behind and departing. [82] The ways in which wealth causes torment include: torment in acquiring it, in protecting it from theft, fire, and envious people, leaving it behind and departing at the moment of death, and accountability on the Day of Judgment regarding how it was acquired and spent.

82) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 108.

Messages

1- The believers contemporary to the Prophet were financially weak compared to the hypocrites. «فلا تعجبك اموالهم...»

2- How many blessings are inwardly curses, and how many economic capabilities become a means of destruction and misery. «لیعذّبهم بها»

3- Bringing humiliation upon wealthy hypocrites is one of the Divine traditions (Sunan). «لیعذّبهم بها»

4- Divine punishment is not only in the Hereafter; it also exists in this world. «فى الحیاة الدنیا»

5- Death is the separation of the soul from the body, not annihilation or perishing. «تَزهق»

6- The soul of the hypocrite is not worthy of being taken; it exits by itself. «تَزهق»

7- The wealthy hypocrite dies with difficulty. «تزهق انفسهم»

8- The abundant wealth and children of the hypocrites set the stage for them to die as disbelievers. «فلا تعجبك اموالهم... تزهق انفسهم و هم كافرون»

9- Pay attention to the outcome of matters, not their superficial appearances. «فلا تعجبك اموالهم... تزهق انفسهم و هم كافرون»

Ayah

وَيَحْلِفُونَ بِٱللَّهِ إِنَّهُمْ لَمِنكُمْ وَمَا هُم مِّنكُمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ يَفْرَقُونَ (٥٦)
They swear by Allah that they belong to you, but they do not belong to you. Rather, they are a frightened lot. (56)

Key Points

«یَفرَقون», means intense fear, as if their hearts are shattered by terror.

Messages

1- One of the tools used by the hypocrites is false swearing. «یحلفون»

2- Do not rush to accept the repentance of the hypocrite or believe his claim, because they are liars. «و ما هم منكم»

3- Exposing the true face of the hypocrites is necessary. «و ما هم منكم»

4- Inner fear and terror are among the other signs of the hypocrites. «قوم یفرقون»

Ayah

لَوْ يَجِدُونَ مَلْجَـًٔا أَوْ مَغَـٰرَٰتٍ أَوْ مُدَّخَلًا لَّوَلَّوْا۟ إِلَيْهِ وَهُمْ يَجْمَحُونَ (٥۷)
If they could find a refuge, or a hideout, or a hole [to creep into], they would turn to it in frantic haste. (57)

Key Points

  • Malja’ (مَلجَأ) is a refuge, and maghārāt (مَغارات) is the plural of maghārah (مَغارة), meaning cave. Muddakhal (مُدّخل) is a hidden path or a subterranean, tunnel-like passage. Yajmahūn (یَجمحون) is derived from jimāh (جماح), meaning a hasty movement that cannot be restrained; an unruly horse is called jamūh (جموح). Hypocrites declare faith either out of fear or out of greed for wealth and status. The Ayah refers to the first group. [83]

83) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The hypocrite is fearful and seeks an opportunity to escape the current situation. «لو یجدون ...»

2- The environment of the Islamic society is intolerable for the hypocrites. «لو یجدون ...»

(The life of hypocrites is one of displacement, and they live with Muslims under duress.)

3- The suffering and anxiety of the hypocrites in the Islamic society, despite having wealth and children, is an example of their worldly punishment. In the two previous Ayahs, He said: «یرید اللّه لیعذّبهم بها فى الحیوة الدنیا» and in this Ayah, He says: «لو یجدون»

4- Hypocrites are sharp and agile in their conflict and evasion against the believers. «یجمحون»

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَلْمِزُكَ فِى ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتِ فَإِنْ أُعْطُوا۟ مِنْهَا رَضُوا۟ وَإِن لَّمْ يُعْطَوْا۟ مِنْهَآ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْخَطُونَ (٥۸)
There are some of them who blame you regarding [the distribution of] the charities: if they are given from them, they are pleased, but if they are not given from them, behold, they are displeased. (58)

Key Points

«لَمز» (Lamz) is fault-finding face-to-face, and if they find fault behind one's back, it is «هَمز» (Hamz). A person who later became the leader of the Khawarij and the Mariqin (those who deviated) objected to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding the distribution of the spoils of the Battle of Hunayn and said: Act justly!! The Prophet said: Who is more just than I? Umar wanted to kill him because of this insolence. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Leave him, he will have followers who will worship so intensely that you will consider your own acts of worship insignificant compared to theirs (referring to their dry worship devoid of Wilayah [allegiance]). Despite all that worship, they will exit the religion, just as an arrow exits the bow. Ultimately, that person was killed by the hand of Ali عليه السلام in the Battle of Nahrawan.

Messages

1- The hypocrite even stings and shows insolence toward the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. «یلمزك»

2- The sharp edge of the hypocrites' attacks and their negative propaganda is directed at the leadership. «یلمزك»

3- Not all companions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله were just. «و منهم من یلمزك»

4- Officials of the Islamic system should not be influenced by the fault-finding of the hypocrites. «و منهم من یلمزك»

5- The distribution of public funds is so precise and sensitive that sometimes even the Prophet is accused. «یلمزك فى الصدقات»

6- The beginning of some deviations is disrespect and insolence toward the leader, stemming from selfishness. (Considering the occasion of revelation).

7- The motive behind criticisms is sometimes personal gain, not genuine concern. «فان اعطوا»

8- Policy-making regarding the financial and economic affairs of the Islamic society is among the duties of the Prophet and the prerogatives of the leader. «فان اعطوا... و ان لم یعطوا»

9- The hypocrite is not content with his due right; he is selfish and illogical. «فان اعطوا منها رضوا و ان لم یعطوا... یسخطون»

10- The malice, enmity, slander, and unjust judgment of some people are due to the loss of material and personal interests. [84] «و ان لم یعطوا...یسخطون» (Indeed, the root of some incorrect analyses lies in internal motives).

84) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: More than two-thirds of the people are like this. Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 228.

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ رَضُوا۟ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥ وَقَالُوا۟ حَسْبُنَا ٱللَّهُ سَيُؤْتِينَا ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَرَسُولُهُۥٓ إِنَّآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ رَٰغِبُونَ (٥۹)
[It would have been better] if they had been pleased with what Allah and His Apostle gave them, and had said, ‘Allah is sufficient for us; Allah and His Apostle will give to us out of His grace. Indeed to Allah do we eagerly turn.’ (59)

Key Points

Four stages are presented in this Ayah: 1- Acceptance and submission to Divine decree. «رضوا ما آتاهم اللَّه» 2- Expressing satisfaction verbally. «قالوا حسبنا اللَّه» 3- Hope for Divine grace and generosity. «سیؤتینا ...» 4- Disregard for the world and desire for Allah. «الى اللَّه راغبون»

Messages

1- The hypocrites are never satisfied with Allah or His decrees. «ولو»

2- Policy-making and decision-making regarding the distribution and expenditure of public funds (Bayt al-Mal), and financial and economic affairs, rest with Allah, the Prophet, and the leader of the Islamic Ummah. «آتاهم اللّه ورسوله»

3- One should not look only at the hardships; patience brings forth a better future. «سیؤتینا»

4- The bitterness of worldly deprivations is sweetened by Allah’s definite promises to the believers and the blessings of Paradise. «سیؤتینا»

5- We are not creditors of Allah; whatever He bestows is out of His grace. «فضله»

6- Divine favors reach us through the Prophets and the Awliya (saints/guardians). «فضله و رسوله»

7- Love for Allah is the foundation for patience, contentment (Rida), and satisfaction (Qana'ah). «الى اللَّه راغبون»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّمَا ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتُ لِلْفُقَرَآءِ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ عَلَيْهَا وَٱلْمُؤَلَّفَةِ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَفِى ٱلرِّقَابِ وَٱلْغَـٰرِمِينَ وَفِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ ۖ فَرِيضَةً مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (٦٠)
The charities are only for the poor and the needy, and those employed to collect them, and those whose hearts are to be reconciled, and for [the freedom of] the slaves and the debtors, and in the way of Allah, and for the traveller. [This is] an ordinance from Allah, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (60)

Key Points

"Sadaqah" (charity) and "Sidaq" (dowry) derive from "Sidq" (truthfulness/sincerity). "Sadaqah" is a sign of sincerity in faith in Allah, and "Sidaq" or the dowry is a sign of sincerity and truthfulness in affection for one's spouse. The intent of sadaqah in this Ayah is the obligatory Zakat. "Faqir" (poor) derives from faqr, meaning someone whose lack of means breaks his backbone. And "Miskin" (destitute) derives from maskan (dwelling), meaning one who has become homebound due to destitution. According to some narrations, the faqir is the poor person who does not ask people [for help], while the miskin is the one who, due to extreme poverty, asks and requests [help]. (85)

Since in the previous Ayahs the hypocrites criticized the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding the distribution of wealth: «یلمزك فى الصدقات» (They criticize you concerning the charities), and in this Ayah, the charities are permitted only for specific groups, we understand that the hypocrites had their eyes on the Zakat. «العاملین علیها» (those employed to administer it) refers to all Zakat administrators who work hard in collecting, safeguarding, distributing, and calculating Zakat, and their wages are paid from the Zakat funds. «مؤلّفة قلوبهم» (those whose hearts are to be reconciled) are those who lack a predisposition toward Islam, and by spending a portion of the Zakat, their hearts can be attracted. Of course, paying Zakat for the reconciliation of hearts does not mean buying faith with money, but rather creating the groundwork for understanding and education, followed by knowledge and faith. «یتألّفهم و یعلّمهم كى ما یعرفوا» (He reconciles them and teaches them so that they may know). (86)

«الغارمین» (the debtors), who are one of the uses of Zakat, are those debtors who have fallen into debt through no fault of their own, such as those who have lost their existence and wealth in fires, floods, shipwrecks, and other natural disasters. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «ایّما مؤمن او مسلمِ ماتَ وترك دیناً و لم یكن فى فسادٍ ولا اسرافِ فعلى الأمام ان یقضیه» (Any believer or Muslim who dies and leaves behind a debt, provided it was not incurred through corruption or extravagance, then it is incumbent upon the Imam to pay it off). (87) «فى سبیل اللَّه» (in the way of Allah) refers to all deeds pleasing to Allah and includes matters such as religious propagation, welfare services, and solving the problems of Muslims. Although its clear manifestation in most interpretations is identified as war and Jihad. «ابن السبیل» (the wayfarer) is someone who is wealthy in his homeland but has become stranded and penniless during a journey.

Portrait of Zakat

The ruling of Zakat was revealed in Mecca, but due to the small number of Muslims and the scarcity of Zakat funds, people paid it themselves. After the establishment of the Islamic government in Medina, the issue of collecting Zakat from the people, depositing it into the public treasury (Bayt al-Mal), and centralizing it by the Islamic ruler was raised. «خذ من اموالهم صدقة» (Take charity from their wealth). (88)

Zakat is not exclusive to Islam; it existed in previous religions as well. Prophet Jesus عليه السلام spoke in the cradle and said: «اوصانى بالصلوةِ والزّكوة» (He has enjoined upon me prayer and Zakat). (89) And Prophet Moses عليه السلام addressed the Children of Israel, saying: «اقیموا الصلوة واتُوا الزّكوة» (Establish the prayer and give the Zakat). (90) And concerning the Prophets in general, we read: «وجعلناهم ائمّةً یهدون بامرنا واوحینا الیهم فعلَ الخیراتِ واقامَ الصلوة وایتاء الزكوة» (And We made them leaders guiding by Our command, and We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, the establishment of prayer, and the giving of Zakat). (91)

Four expressions for Zakat are mentioned in the Quran: 1. Giving wealth. « وآتى المال على حبّه ذوى القربى» (And gives wealth, in spite of love for it, to relatives). (92) 2. Sadaqah. « خذ من اموالهم صدقة» (Take charity from their wealth). (93) 3. Spending (Infaq). «یقیموا الصلوة وینفقوا» (They establish prayer and spend). (94) 4. Zakat. «یقیمون الصلاة ویؤتون الزكوة» (They establish prayer and give Zakat). (95)

In the Quran, Zakat usually comes alongside prayer, and according to narrations, the condition for the acceptance of prayer is the payment of Zakat. This connection signifies the relationship with Allah and the relationship with people. None of the other religious obligations in the Quran are mentioned in such close conjunction with prayer.

Receiving Zakat is forbidden for the Sayyids (descendants of the Prophet). A group from Bani Hashim requested the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to be responsible for collecting the Zakat on livestock so that they might receive a share as Zakat administrators. The Prophet said: Zakat is forbidden for me and for you. (96) Unless both the giver and the recipient of the Zakat are Sayyids.

The law of Zakat does not imply Islam's desire for the existence of a poor class receiving Zakat and a wealthy class paying Zakat, but rather it is a solution for an external reality of society. The wealthy also sometimes face phenomena like theft, fire, accidents, war, and captivity, and in the Islamic system, there must be a budget for social security. It is stated in narrations: Allah has placed a right in the wealth of the rich sufficient to solve the problems of the needy, and if He knew it would not be enough for them, He would have increased it. If people paid the rights of the poor to them, everyone would have a good life, and if the wealthy paid Zakat, there would be no poverty. (97)

Contrary to the belief of some regarding preventing the increase of individual income and restricting it, Islam believes that people should be given relative freedom to grow through effort, innovation, and utilization of nature, but they must also pay taxes.

The condition for spending Zakat in the way of Allah, «فى سبیل اللَّه», is not poverty; rather, it can be spent wherever it helps the dominance of the path of Islam. (98)

Zakat can be used to save society from the evil of wicked individuals, and this falls under «والمؤلفة قلوبهم» (those whose hearts are to be reconciled). (99) If blood money (Diyah) is established upon someone and he is unable to pay, he falls under «والغارمین» (the debtors), and he can be helped with Zakat. (100) Perhaps the expression «وفى الرقاب» (for [freeing] slaves) also includes spending Zakat for the release of prisoners or covering their expenses.

The distribution of Zakat among the eight categories does not have to be equal; rather, it is distributed under the supervision of the Islamic ruler and according to need and necessity.

Zakat is a factor in wealth adjustment. Zakat is a practical expression of gratitude for divine gifts. Zakat reduces class disparity and removes resentment between the poor and the wealthy. Zakat revives the spirit of generosity and mercy in man, reducing worldliness and material attachment. Zakat is the foundation of social security for the deprived. It tells the poor: Do not worry; it tells the bankrupt: Try again; it tells the traveler: Do not fear being stranded; it tells the employee: Your share is protected; it promises freedom to slaves; it boosts the market for divine services and attracts the hearts of others to Islam. Neglect of the remembrance of Allah, exploitation of people, hard-heartedness, rebellion, and debauchery are the fruits of accumulation and hoarding of wealth, and Zakat is the remedy for this illness. Zakat, besides alleviating deprivation, increases inclination toward Islam or at least causes individuals to cease cooperation with the enemies of Islam. As stated in narrations, sometimes individuals with weak faith strengthen their belief through financial aid and proximity to Islam. (101) Zakat, which is a manifestation of the Islamic system, paves the way for social justice, poverty eradication, provision for employees, international popularity, freedom for slaves and those in bondage, mobilization of forces, preservation of the religion and the entity of Muslims, and the expansion of public services.

(85) Tafsir Al-Mizan. (86) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (87) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqaiq. (88) Tawbah, 103. (89) Maryam, 32. (90) Baqarah, 34. (91) Anbiya, 73. (92) Baqarah, 77. (93) Tawbah, 103. (94) Ibrahim, 31. (95) Ma'idah, 55. (96) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 58. (97) Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 4. (98) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. (99) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani. (100) Tafsir Maraghi. (101) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Zakat is a sign of sincerity in declaring faith. «انّما الصدقات»
  2. The distribution of Zakat is based on revelation, not expectations. In response to the criticism of the hypocrites, He said: «انّما الصدقات للفقراء و...»
  3. Spending Zakat outside the eight categories mentioned in this Ayah is not permissible. «انّما الصدقات»
  4. One of the principles of Islamic social justice is the insurance and provision of life for the deprived. «انّما الصدقات للفقراء»
  5. Not all of a person's wealth belongs to him; others also have a share in it. «للفقراء و ...»
  6. Zakat payers should not impose favors upon the poor, because the Zakat share is the property of the poor themselves. «للفقراء»
  7. Poverty eradication must be at the forefront of the Islamic system's programs. «فریضة من اللَّه» (An obligation from Allah). First, «للفقراء» was mentioned, and then the other categories.
  8. Providing for the livelihood of those who seek to establish religious rulings and obligations is necessary. «والعالمین علیها»
  9. Every job has a price and value, whether the worker performing it is needy or wealthy. «العاملین علیها»
  10. Just as a judge must be provided for so that he does not contemplate bribery, Zakat administrators must also be provided for so that they do not contemplate bribery or embezzlement. «العاملین علیها»
  11. For the collection of Zakat, individuals must be appointed by the Islamic government. «العاملین علیها»
  12. Material and spiritual matters are intertwined. «والمؤلفة قلوبهم»
  13. Islam, unlike lying and boastful colonialists, expands its doctrine by solving the economic problems of societies, with the intention of seeking proximity [to Allah], accompanied by proof and guidance. «المؤلفة قلوبهم»
  14. It is one of the duties of the Islamic state to use wealth as a means of attracting hearts. (102) «والمؤلفة قلوبهم»
  15. Islam has allocated a budget for the freedom of slaves. «وفى الرقاب»
  16. The expenses of the Zakat organization are not imposed on the government; rather, it is self-sufficient. «العاملین علیها»
  17. The Islamic government is responsible for collecting Zakat and paying it appropriately, so that the poor do not go directly to the wealthy themselves and feel humiliated. (103) «العاملین علیها»
  18. The budget for Jihad and the defense of Islamic territory comes from Zakat and rests upon the people. «فى سبیل اللَّه»
  19. Wealth must be a means of proximity to Allah. «فى سبیل اللَّه»
  20. By providing a budget for those stranded during travel, Islam has given value to the issue of travel, trade, and tourism. «ابن السبیل»
  21. The law of Zakat is a calculated and wise law. «انمّا الزكاة... فریضةً... علیم حكیم»
  22. Islam is not only a religion of worship; it also has economic laws. «فریضةً»

(102) In Islam, matters such as gifts, souvenirs, and feeding [the poor] are considered levers for attracting hearts. (103) Another Ayah states: «خذ من اموالهم صدقة» (Take charity from their wealth). Tawbah, 103.

Ayah

وَمِنْهُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱلنَّبِىَّ وَيَقُولُونَ هُوَ أُذُنٌ ۚ قُلْ أُذُنُ خَيْرٍ لَّكُمْ يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَيُؤْمِنُ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٦۱)
Among them are those who torment the Prophet, and say, ‘He is an ear.’ Say, ‘An ear that is good for you. He has faith in Allah and trusts the faithful, and is a mercy for those of you who have faith.’ As for those who torment the Apostle of Allah, there is a painful punishment for them. (61)

Key Points

The intent of minhum (منهم, "from among them") is either the group of hypocrites, or some weak-willed individuals who did not participate in the battle out of fear and offered excuses and pretexts after the war. According to narrations, due to the constant contact of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله with Ali عليه السلام, a number of hypocrites characterized the Prophet as naive and gullible. [104] The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «مَن آذانى فى عترتى فعلیه لعنة اللَّه» (Whoever harms my Ahl al-Bayt, the curse of Allah be upon him). Regarding Lady Fatimah عليها السلام, He also said: «مَن آذاها فقد آذانى» (Whoever harms Fatimah has harmed me). [105]

104) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 105) Mulhaqāt Ihqāq al-Haqq, Vol. 18, pp. 458 and 439.

Messages

1- A group constantly harmed the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. (Yu'dhūna - یؤذون, the imperfect tense verb, signifies continuity.)

2- Not all companions of the Prophet were just; some harmed the Prophet. (Yu'dhūna - یؤذون)

3- Leadership of the Ummah necessitates enduring harm from both insiders and outsiders. (Yu'dhūna al-Nabī - یؤذون النبى)

4- Harming the Prophet is disbelief (kufr). [106] (The contrast between al-ladhīna yu'dhūna - الّذین یؤذون and al-ladhīna āmanū - الذین آمنوا)

5- The hypocrites engaged in propaganda and created a negative atmosphere even against the Prophet. (Yaqūlūna huwa udhun - یقولون هو اُذن)

6- Allah informs His Prophet of the secret and internal conversations of the opponents. (Yaqūlūna huwa udhun - یقولون هو اُذن)

7- Allow all people to speak so that they do not say you are unwilling to listen to them, even if you are accused of being gullible. (Yaqūlūna huwa udhun - یقولون هو اذن)

8- Among the manners of listening is paying full attention to the speaker's words. The word udhun (اُذن) means that He is entirely ear (all-hearing).

9- Sometimes, for the sake of the Ummah's welfare, one must overlook certain things that are heard. (Udhunu khayrun lakum - اُذن خیرلكم)

10- Sometimes the educational, social, or political effects of silence are greater than confrontation and taking a stand. (Udhun - اذن)

11- The characteristics of an aware leader include broad-mindedness, listening to all groups, dealing with them kindly, concealing faults, and leaving the door open for people's excuses and repentance. (Qul huwa udhunu khayr - قل هو اُذن خیر)

12- Listen to people's words with an open mind and out of goodwill. (Udhunu khayrin lakum - اُذن خیرٍ لكم)

13- Silence in the face of what is heard is not always a sign of approval. (Udhunu khayrun lakum - اذن خیر لكم)

14- Support the one who has been harmed. In response to the enemy's statement calling the Prophet udhun (اُذن), Allah mentions four virtues for Him: Udhunu khayrun lakum (اذن خیر لكم), Yu'minu bi-Allāh (یؤمن باللَّه), Yu'minu li-al-mu'minīn (یؤمن للمؤمنین), and Rahmatun li-al-ladhīna āmanū (رحمة للذین آمنوا).

15- If a believer gives news, confirm his word. (Yu'minu li-al-mu'minīn - یؤمن للمؤمنین)

16- Although the Prophet is a mercy to the worlds, benefiting from this mercy is exclusive to the people of faith. (Rahmatun li-al-ladhīna āmanū - رحمة للذین آمنوا)

17- Fear the punishment for harming the Prophet. (Lahum ‘adhābun alīm - لهم عذاب الیم)

106) Tafsir al-Manar.

Ayah

يَحْلِفُونَ بِٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ لِيُرْضُوكُمْ وَٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥٓ أَحَقُّ أَن يُرْضُوهُ إِن كَانُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ (٦۲)
They swear to you by Allah, to please you; but Allah and His Apostle are worthier that they should please Him, should they be faithful. (62)

Messages

1- The hypocrite is constantly in fear, terror, and anxiety, and seeks to exploit people's religious beliefs and attract their attention through oaths and pretense. «یحلفون...لكم»

2- Do not be deceived by every oath, because sometimes sacred things become tools in the hands of the profane. «یحلفون باللَّه»

3- For the believer, the satisfaction of Allah is the principle, not the approval of people. «واللَّه...احقّ ان یرضوه»

4- The satisfaction of the Messenger is the same as the Divine satisfaction. «اللَّه و رسوله احقّ ان یرضوه» and He did not say: «یرضوهما» (107)

5- Whoever prefers the satisfaction of the creation over the satisfaction of the Creator has a share of hypocrisy and must be reprimanded. «اللَّه و رسوله احقّ ان یرضوه ان كانوا مؤمنین»

(107) Of course, the reason He did not say «یرضوهما» might be that no one, not even the Prophet, is equal to Allah the Almighty.

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُۥ مَن يُحَادِدِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ فَأَنَّ لَهُۥ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدًا فِيهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْخِزْىُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (٦۳)
Do they not know that whoever opposes Allah and His Apostle, there awaits him the Fire of hell, to remain in it [forever]? That is the great disgrace. (63)

Key Points

| «یُحادِد اللَّه» (Yuhādid Allāh) either means limiting the power of Allah, as if Allah is restricted in inflicting His wrath upon them, or it means they imagined Allah to be powerless (having His hands tied). Fakhr Razi considered this word to be derived from “hadīd” meaning obstinacy (or hardness/severity), and stated: “Muhāddah” (mutual opposition) either means transgressing the Divine law, or it means considering oneself to be on one side and Allah on the other.

Messages

| 1- Opposition to rightful leadership is opposition to Allah. «مَن یُحادد اللَّه و رسوله» | 2- Preserving one's reputation through false oaths is an example of enmity and obstinacy toward Allah. «یحلفون باللّه... یحادد اللّه» | 3- The fruit of obstinacy shown by knowledgeable individuals against the religion of Allah is eternal Hellfire. «ألم یعلموا... فانّ له نار جهنّم» | 4- Remembering Hell (the Fire) can prevent obstinacy and stubbornness. «فانّ له نار جهنّم»

Ayah

يَحْذَرُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ أَن تُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُورَةٌ تُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ ۚ قُلِ ٱسْتَهْزِءُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا تَحْذَرُونَ (٦٤)
The hypocrites are apprehensive lest a surah should be sent down against them, informing them about what is in their hearts. Say, ‘Go on deriding. Allah will indeed bring out what you are apprehensive of.’ (64)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of the revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah, it is said: A group of hypocrites decided to startle the Prophet's camel on a mountain pass during the return from the Battle of Tabuk so that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله would be killed. The Messenger of Allah was informed of their decision through revelation. While Ammar and Hudhayfah were guarding the Prophet from the front and the back, they reached the pass, and the hypocrites attacked. The Prophet recognized them and told their names to Hudhayfah. He asked: Why do You not command their execution? He replied: I do not want people to say that Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, once He gained power, killed Muslims. [108] In the Prophet's absence, the hypocrites mockingly said: He wants to conquer the palaces of Syria (Sham)! This Ayah was revealed and threatened them with disgrace. [109] The word "Surah" means a collection of Divine verses (Ayat). This word has become the technical term for the 114 Surahs of the Quran, and it was known to everyone in the early days of Islam (Sadr al-Islam).

108) Tafasir Al-Mizan, Fi Zilal al-Quran, Majma' al-Bayan, Kabir Fakhr Razi, and Namuneh. 109) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The hypocrite is constantly worried about his true face being exposed. «یحذر المنافقون»

2- The hypocrites knew that Allah was aware of their actions and that the Prophet of Islam was truthful and had a connection with God; this is why they were worried about the revelation of a Surah and their exposure. «یحذر المنافقون...»

3- The verses of the Quran were revealed gradually and based on needs. «ان تنزّل سورة...»

4- Mockery is the method of the hypocrites. «استهزؤوا» (Perhaps the intent of mockery here is hypocrisy itself.)

5- The Divine tradition and promise regarding the hypocrites is exposure; therefore, we should not worry about their stings. «انّ اللَّه مخرجٌ»

6- The Will of Allah prevails over the inclination of the hypocrites. «یحذر... انّ اللَّه مخرج»

7- Threatening the hypocrites is necessary. «انّ اللَّه مخرج»

Ayah

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُمْ لَيَقُولُنَّ إِنَّمَا كُنَّا نَخُوضُ وَنَلْعَبُ ۚ قُلْ أَبِٱللَّهِ وَءَايَـٰتِهِۦ وَرَسُولِهِۦ كُنتُمْ تَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٦٥)
If you question them [regarding their conduct], they will surely say, ‘We were just gossiping and amusing ourselves.’ Say, ‘Were you deriding Allah, His signs, and His apostles? (65)

Key Points

| The word khawḍ (خَوْض) in the Arabic language means stepping into mud, and in the Qur'an, it refers to engaging in undesirable actions. The Ayah relates to the Battle of Tabuk, where the hypocrites intended to kill the Prophet upon his return. One of them had said, "If the plot is exposed, what should we do?" Another replied, "We will say it was jesting and joking." In reality, they offered an excuse worse than the sin itself. [110]

110) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Lying and offering justifications are constant practices of the hypocrites. «لئن سألتهم لیقولنّ»

2- Retreat, feeling weak, deceit, and insistence on falsehood, even after the plot is exposed, are signs of a hypocrite. «لیقولنّ نخوض و نلعب»

3- Allah has informed [us] about the justifications and the future of the hypocrites, thereby exposing them. «لیقولنّ نخوض و نلعب»

4- To exonerate themselves, the hypocrites both swore an oath and claimed their intention was 100% jesting. (The lām in «لیقولنّ» signifies an oath, and «انّما» conveys exclusivity/restriction.)

5- The Prophet is commanded to rebuke the hypocrites. «قل أباللّه وآیاته...»

6- Jesting with religious sanctities is not permissible. «أباللّه وآیاته ورسوله كنتم تستهزؤن»

Ayah

لَا تَعْتَذِرُوا۟ قَدْ كَفَرْتُم بَعْدَ إِيمَـٰنِكُمْ ۚ إِن نَّعْفُ عَن طَآئِفَةٍ مِّنكُمْ نُعَذِّبْ طَآئِفَةًۢ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ (٦٦)
Do not make excuses. You have disbelieved after your faith.’ If We forgive a group among you, We will punish another group, for they have been guilty. (66)

Key Points

Regarding which group of hypocrites will be forgiven, there are several opinions: A: Those who repent. [111] B: Those who were on the periphery and alongside the mockers. [112] However, the leaders among them will not be accepted (for forgiveness). C: Those whose forgiveness is in the interest of the system and the government. [113] D: Those who deviated only for a short period, as opposed to those whose crime is deep-rooted, long-standing, and continues persistently. «كانوا مجرمین»

111) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 112) Tafsir Kabir (Fakhr al-Razi). 113) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The excuses of liars and mockers are not accepted everywhere. «لاتعتذروا...»

2- Apostasy (or turning back to disbelief) is the reason for rejecting any excuse. «قد كفرتم بعد ایمانكم»

3- We should not be arrogant about our current faith, as there is a danger of an evil end (su' al-'aqibah) and turning back to disbelief. «قد كفرتم بعد ایمانكم»

4- Insulting and mocking Allah, the Prophet, and divine signs (Ayat) is disbelief (Kufr). «تستهزؤن، كفرتم»

5- The hypocrite is, in reality, a disbeliever (Kafir). «كفرتم»

6- When threatening opponents and plotting hypocrites, we should not close all avenues (of return/forgiveness). «نعف»

7- Some hypocrites are forgivable, provided they repent. «نعف عن طائفة» (Hypocrisy also has levels/degrees.)

8- The reason for the punishment of the hypocrites is their long-standing disbelief (Kufr) and crime. «نعذّب... كانوا مجرمین»

Ayah

ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقَـٰتُ بَعْضُهُم مِّنۢ بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْمُنكَرِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَقْبِضُونَ أَيْدِيَهُمْ ۚ نَسُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (٦۷)
The hypocrites, men and women, are all alike: they bid what is wrong and forbid what is right; and are tight-fisted. They have forgotten Allah, so He has forgotten them. The hypocrites are indeed the transgressors. (67)

Key Points

In the early days of Islam, there were hypocrite women who were effective in corruption, just like the men.

Question: Since Allah does not forget, "و ما كان ربّك نسیّاً" [114] (and your Lord is never forgetful), what is the meaning of this verse which states: Allah forgot them?

Answer: Attributing forgetfulness to Allah is metaphorical; it means that Allah deals with them like those who have been forgotten (not that He actually forgets them). Imam al-Ridha عليه السلام said: The punishment for the one who forgets Allah and the Day of Judgment is that he forgets himself. [115] And Hadhrat Ali عليه السلام said: Forgetting Allah means that He deprives them of goodness. [116]

114) Maryam, 64. 115) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 116) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Both men and women play a role in the reform or corruption of society. «المنافقون، المنافقات»

2- Some hypocrites play a leadership role, while others are influenced. «بعضهم من بعض» (Hypocrisy has levels/degrees)

3- The people of hypocrisy, despite their different degrees, share common principles and actions. «بعضهم من بعض»

4- Do not believe the hypocrites, even if they swear they are among you, because they belong to their own faction. «بعضهم من بعض»

5- There is a strong relationship among the hypocrites. «بعضهم من بعض»

6- Spreading obscenity, inviting to evil deeds (munkarat), and forbidding good deeds (ma'ruf) are signs of hypocrisy. «یأمرون بالمنكر و ینهون عن المعروف»

7- In the culture of the hypocrites, there is no benevolence or charity. «یقبضون ایدیهم»

8- Abandoning the enjoining of good (Amr bil Ma'ruf), forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), and spending in the way of Allah are signs of forgetting Allah. «نسوا اللَّه»

9- Deprivation of Divine grace and being forgotten are the results of forgetting Allah. «فنسیهم»

10- Divine punishments are commensurate with human actions. «نسوااللَّه فنسیهم»

11- In their calculations, hypocrites consider people, not Allah. «نسوااللَّه»

12- Hypocrisy and duplicity are transgression (fisq). «هم الفاسقون»

Ayah

وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقَـٰتِ وَٱلْكُفَّارَ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ هِىَ حَسْبُهُمْ ۚ وَلَعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ (٦۸)
Allah has promised the hypocrites, men and women, and the faithless, the Fire of hell, to remain in it [forever]. That suffices them. Allah has cursed them, and there is a lasting punishment for them. (68)

Messages

1- Men and women are equal concerning divine obligations. «المنافقین و المنافقات»

2- Divine punishments are the result of our own actions and are a reciprocal response (tit-for-tat). (In Ayah 65, the hypocrites mocked and ridiculed the Muslims. In this Ayah, Allah, by using the words «وعد» (promise) and «حسبهم» (sufficient for them)—which usually signify a promise of goodness and satisfactory sufficiency—has, in a way, mocked and humiliated them.)

3- The promise of Hellfire is first for the hypocrites, then for the disbelievers. «وعد اللّه المنافقین و المنافقات والكفّار»

4- Although the hypocrites place themselves alongside the believers in this world, in the Hereafter, they will be ranked with the disbelievers. «المنافقین... والكفّار»

5- Hell is the totality of every kind of suffering and affliction; therefore, it is sufficient for the hypocrites and the disbelievers. «هى حسبهم»

Ayah

كَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ كَانُوٓا۟ أَشَدَّ مِنكُمْ قُوَّةً وَأَكْثَرَ أَمْوَٰلًا وَأَوْلَـٰدًا فَٱسْتَمْتَعُوا۟ بِخَلَـٰقِهِمْ فَٱسْتَمْتَعْتُم بِخَلَـٰقِكُمْ كَمَا ٱسْتَمْتَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُم بِخَلَـٰقِهِمْ وَخُضْتُمْ كَٱلَّذِى خَاضُوٓا۟ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (٦۹)
[Hypocrites! Your case is] similar to those who were before you, who were more powerful than you and more abounding in wealth and children: they enjoyed their share [of worldly existence]; you too enjoy your share, just like those who were before you enjoyed their share, and you have gossiped [impiously] as they gossiped. They are the ones whose works have failed in this world and the Hereafter; and it is they who are the losers. (69)

Key Points

The term «خَلاق» in the Ayah means an absolute share or portion, although linguistically it refers to the ethics and morals that a person acquires. The intent behind the nullification (حبط) of deeds in this world is perhaps the exposure of the true face of the hypocrites and the devaluation of their good works.

Messages

1- The history and destiny of nations are similar to one another. «كالّذین من قبلكم»

2- The path of disbelief (kufr) and hypocrisy has always existed, and the Divine tradition (Sunnah) is also uniform. «كالّذین من قبلكم»

3- Military power («قوّة»), economic power («اموالاً»), and human resources («اولاداً») are not obstacles to Divine wrath.

4- Hypocrites and disbelievers rely on their power, wealth, and children. «كانوا اشدّمنكم قوّةو...»

5- Faith is not a prerequisite for benefiting from worldly gains; disbelievers and hypocrites also have a share. «فاستمتعوا»

6- Worldly enjoyments and successes are fleeting. «فاستمتعوا بخلاقهم»

7- The reason for the success of the opponents of the prophets in this world is their character, disposition, and effort. «فاستمتعوا بخلاقهم»

8- Not every success or blessing is a sign of Divine favor. «فاستمتعوا... حبطت اعمالهم»

9- Allah has appointed a share for those who deviate from the truth in this world. «خلاقهم» (their portion). But in the Hereafter, they have no share or portion whatsoever. [117]
