10- Sinking and drowning in corruption and hostility toward religion is a factor of downfall. «خضتم، خاضوا» (You plunged, they plunged). Otherwise, repentance and reconstruction after every sin can be salvific.

11- Disbelief (kufr) and hypocrisy cause the nullification of deeds. «حبطت»

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَأْتِهِمْ نَبَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ وَقَوْمِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَأَصْحَـٰبِ مَدْيَنَ وَٱلْمُؤْتَفِكَـٰتِ ۚ أَتَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ ۖ فَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (۷٠)
Has there not come to them the account of those who were before them—the people of Noah, ‘Ad, and Tham£d, and the people of Abraham, the inhabitants of Midian, and the towns that were overturned? Their apostles brought them manifest proofs. So it was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves. (70)

Key Points

The people of Noah were destroyed by drowning; the people of 'Ad (the people of Prophet Hud) by cold and poisonous gales; the people of Thamud (the people of Prophet Salih) by earthquake; the people of Madyan (the companions of Prophet Shu'ayb) by a fiery cloud; and the people of Lot by the overturning of their settlements. Mu'tafikāt (مؤتَفِكات) derives from i'tikāf (ائتكاف), meaning overturning, which in this context refers to the punishment of the people of Lot.

Messages

1- Worldly punishment and retribution is among the divine traditions (Sunan). «ألم یأتهم...» means that this is also the case concerning you.

2- Whoever is more aware is more responsible. «ألم یأتهم...» The people of the Prophet's era were aware of the fate of past nations.

3- The history and news of past nations are very important and beneficial. (Naba' (نبأ) is used for important and beneficial news.)

4- Whoever does not learn a lesson from history should be reproached. «ألم یأتهم نبأ الذین من قبلهم» The best lesson for life today is taking heed from past history.

5- Divine wrath always follows the completion of the proof (Hujjah). «اتتهم رسلهم»

6- Disobeying divine commands is oppression against oneself. «انفسهم یظلمون»

7- Man possesses free will and can make decisions despite all the miracles. «اتتهم رسلهم... و لكن كانوا انفسهم یظلمون»

8- Divine wrath is due to insistence and persistence in oppression. «كانوا انفسهم یظلمون»

Ayah

وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءُ بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ وَيُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَيُطِيعُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ سَيَرْحَمُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۷۱)
But the faithful, men and women, are comrades of one another: they bid what is right and forbid what is wrong and maintain the prayer, give the zakat, and obey Allah and His Apostle. It is they to whom Allah will soon grant His mercy. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (71)

Key Points

Ayah 67 used the expression «بعضهم من بعض» (some of them are from others) regarding the hypocrites, but this Ayah says concerning the believers: «بعضهم اولیاء بعض» (some of them are allies of others). Perhaps the difference in expression is due to the fact that, contrary to the believers, there is no deep unity or bond of wilayah (guardianship/alliance) among the hypocrites. When personal interests arise, they are not loyal, and their unity is merely apparent and formal, as the Quran states: «تحسبهم جمیعاً وقلوبهم شتّى» [118] (You think they are united, but their hearts are scattered). Since enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) and forbidding evil (Nahy anil Munkar) are factors for establishing other obligations, they are mentioned in the Ayah before prayer (Salat) and charity (Zakat). [119]

118) Al-Hashr, 14. 119) Tuhaf al-Uqul, p. 237.

Messages

1- Both men and women are influential in reforming society. «المؤمنون والمؤمنات»

2- In the Islamic society, believers have the right of wilayah (guardianship/alliance) and supervision, accompanied by affection, over one another from Allah, and they are not indifferent. «بعضهم اولیاء بعض»

3- Enjoining good and forbidding evil, which is obligatory upon all believing men and women, stems from the right of wilayah over one another. «بعضهم اولیاء بعض یأمرون... وینهون»

4- Enjoining and forbidding must be done from a position of strength, not weakness. «یأمرون بالمعروف و ینهون عن المنكر»

5- Enjoining and forbidding are executable under the shade of affection and wilayah. «بعضهم اولیاء، یأمرون...»

6- Attention to the lives of the deprived and the social security of the poor is alongside other acts of worship. «یُقیمون، یُؤتون الزكوة و یُطیعون اللّه...»

7- Faith plays a significant role in the reform of the individual and society. «المؤمنون و المؤمنات... یأمرون، یَنهون، یُقیمون الصلوة و یؤتون الزكوة و یطیعون اللّه»

8- Enjoining good, forbidding evil, establishing prayer, paying Zakat, and obeying Allah and the Messenger are the continuous duties and actions of the believers. «یَأمرون، یَنهون، یُقیمون، یُؤتون، یُطیعون» (The present tense verb signifies continuity).

9- Those who invite others to good deeds must themselves be people of action. «یأمرون، یقیمون الصلوة و یؤتون الزكوة و یطیعون»

10- Obedience to Allah and the Messenger must be willing and voluntary. «یُطیعون اللّه و رسوله» (Obedience [Ita'ah] linguistically means following willingly).

11- Obedience to Allah is necessary in acts of worship, and obedience to the Messenger is necessary in governmental programs. «یطیعون اللَّه ورسوله»

12- Human actions pave the way for Divine Mercy. «یَأمرون، یَنهون، یُقیمون، یُؤتون، یُطیعون، سیرحمهم اللّه»

13- Men and women are equal in receiving Divine Mercy. «المؤمنون والمؤمنات... سیرحمهم اللّه»

Ayah

وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا وَمَسَـٰكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِى جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ۚ وَرِضْوَٰنٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۷۲)
Allah has promised the faithful, men and women, gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], and good dwellings in the Gardens of Eden. Yet Allah’s pleasure is greater [than all these]; that is the great success. (72)

Key Points

This Ayah explains how the mercy mentioned in the previous Ayah, "سیرحمهم" (He will have mercy on them), is realized.

According to narrations, "جنّاتِ عَدن" (Gardens of Eden) is the best and most noble region of Paradise, the abode of the Prophets, the truthful (Siddiqūn), and the martyrs (Shuhadā'), a region whose goodness has not occurred to anyone's imagination. [120]

Comparison between the people of hypocrisy (Munāfiqūn) and the people of faith (Mu'minūn) (Ayahs 67 and 68 versus 71 and 72): 1- The people of hypocrisy are "بعضهم من بعض" (some of them are from others), but the people of faith are "بعضهم اولیاء بعض" (some of them are protectors of others). 2- The people of hypocrisy "یأمرون بالمنكر و ینهون عن المعروف" (enjoin evil and forbid good), but the people of faith "یأمرون بالمعروف و ینهون عن المنكر" (enjoin good and forbid evil). 3- The people of hypocrisy "نسوااللَّه" (forgot Allah), but the people of faith "یقیمون الصلوة" (establish prayer). 4- The people of hypocrisy "یقبضون ایدیهم" (withhold their hands), but the people of faith "یؤتون الزكوة" (give Zakat). 5 - The people of hypocrisy are "فاسقون" (transgressors), but the people of faith "یطیعون اللَّه" (obey Allah). 6- Allah has promised the people of hypocrisy "نار جهنّم" (the Fire of Hell), but He has promised the people of faith "جنّات, مساكن" (Gardens, dwellings). 7- For the people of hypocrisy, "لعنهم اللَّه" (Allah has cursed them), but for the people of faith, "رضوان من اللَّه" (pleasure from Allah). 8 - For the people of hypocrisy, "عذاب مقیم" (lasting punishment), but for the people of faith, "فوز عظیم" (great success).

120) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Men and women are equal in receiving divine reward. "وعداللَّه المؤمنین و المؤمنات" (Allah has promised the believing men and believing women).

2- The greatest advantage of Paradise is eternal residence therein and the everlasting nature of its blessings. "خالدین فیها" (abiding therein eternally).

3- Life in the Hereafter is not only spiritual but also material and physical. "جنّات, مساكن طیّبة" (Gardens, pleasant dwellings).

4- A good dwelling is valuable when it is in a good region. "مساكن طیّبة فى جنّات عدن" (pleasant dwellings in Gardens of Eden).

5 - We should utilize humanity's natural inclinations toward water, greenery, gardens, parks, and homes to encourage spiritual orientation. "وعداللَّه... جنّات تجرى من تحتها الانهار..." (Allah has promised... Gardens beneath which rivers flow...).

6- Spiritual pleasures are superior to the material pleasures of Paradise. "رضوان من اللَّه اكبر" (Pleasure from Allah is greater).

7- Paradise also has degrees and ranks. "جنّات تجرى" (Gardens beneath which flow), "جنّات عدن" (Gardens of Eden), and "رضوان من اللَّه اكبر" (Pleasure from Allah is greater).

8 - Attaining the reward of the Hereafter and divine satisfaction is the great success. "الفوزالعظیم" (the great success).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ جَـٰهِدِ ٱلْكُفَّارَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَٱغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۷۳)
O Prophet! Wage jihad against the faithless and the hypocrites, and be severe with them. Their refuge shall be hell, and it is an evil destination. (73)

Key Points

As long as the hypocrites have not started war, have not plotted, and have not become like the warring disbeliever (kāfir ḥarbī), jihad against them is only through the tongue (speech). (121) The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, before this Ayah, treated the hypocrites with noble conduct, but after the revelation of this Ayah, the Prophet's approach intensified. (122)

121) Tafsir Al-Manar. 122) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an.

Messages

1- Jihad must be according to the command and view of the leader of the Muslims. «یا ایها النبى جاهد»

2- In the Islamic system, the Commander-in-Chief of the forces is the leader. «یا ایها النبى جاهد»

3- Islam is the religion of jihad and struggle against disbelief (kufr) and hypocrisy (nifāq). «جاهد الكفّار و المنافقین»

4- We must struggle both against the manifest external enemies, «جاهد الكفّار», and against the hidden internal enemies. «و المنافقین»

5- The leader of the Muslims must be decisive against arrogance (istakbār). «یا ایهاالنّبى جاهد، واغلظ»

6- Although the Prophet is the source of mercy, due to the disbelief and hypocrisy of the enemies, he is commanded to be severe. «واغلظ علیهم»

7- Jihad against the disbelievers and hypocrites is their worldly recompense, and their punishment in the Hereafter is Hell. «و مأویهم جهنم»

8- The hypocrite has an evil end. «بئس المصیر»

Ayah

يَحْلِفُونَ بِٱللَّهِ مَا قَالُوا۟ وَلَقَدْ قَالُوا۟ كَلِمَةَ ٱلْكُفْرِ وَكَفَرُوا۟ بَعْدَ إِسْلَـٰمِهِمْ وَهَمُّوا۟ بِمَا لَمْ يَنَالُوا۟ ۚ وَمَا نَقَمُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ أَنْ أَغْنَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ ۚ فَإِن يَتُوبُوا۟ يَكُ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَتَوَلَّوْا۟ يُعَذِّبْهُمُ ٱللَّهُ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ وَمَا لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۷٤)
They swear by Allah that they did not say it. But they certainly did utter the word of unfaith and renounced faith after their Islam. They contemplated what they could not achieve, and they were vindictive only because Allah and His Apostle had enriched them out of His grace. Yet if they repent, it will be better for them; but if they turn away, Allah shall punish them with a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter, and they shall not find on the earth any guardian or helper. (74)

Key Points

In Ayah 66, the Prophet was quoted as saying to the hypocrites: «كفرتم بعد ایمانكم» (you disbelieved after your belief). In this Ayah, Allah says: «كفروا بعد اسلامهم» (they disbelieved after their submission/Islam). The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used the term "Iman" (belief), while Allah used the term "Islam" (submission), because the Prophet, based on outward appearances, considered them believers (Mu'minin), but Allah, Who is aware of the secrets, considered them Muslims, not believers. [123]

The Ayah encompasses all the conspiracies that the hypocrites hatched against the Prophet and Islam. However, most Shi'a and Sunni exegeses refer to the conspiracy of Laylat al-'Aqabah (The Night of the Pass), where the hypocrites ambushed the Prophet in a mountain pass intending to startle the Prophet's camel, but their plot was discovered, and they did not achieve their goal. «همّوا بما لم ینالوا» (They intended what they did not attain).

It is narrated that during the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله speech in Tabuk, a hypocrite named Hallas said: "If the Prophet speaks the truth, then we are worse than donkeys." One of the companions (Amir ibn Qays) informed the Prophet of this insolence. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله summoned him. He denied it, while Amir insisted that he was lying and had indeed said that. By the Prophet's order, both swore an oath near the pulpit, but with the revelation of this Ayah, the hypocrite was exposed. [124]

123) Tafsir al-Mizan. 124) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Lying and false oaths are signs of a hypocrite. «یحلفون باللَّه» (They swear by Allah).

2- Hypocrites are ungrateful. They have achieved status under the shade of Islam, yet they do not cease finding fault and speaking ill. «و مانقموا الاّ ان اغناهم اللَّه...» (And they resented nothing except that Allah enriched them...).

3- Mentioning the name of the Messenger of Allah alongside the name of Allah and attributing an action to both is not polytheism (Shirk). «اغناهم اللَّه ورسوله» (Allah and His Messenger enriched them).

4- The source of grace (فضل) is Allah alone. «من فضله» (from His grace), and He did not say: "from the grace of both of them." [125]

5- Islam, besides intellectual and moral training, also improved the material condition of life. «اغناهم اللَّه ورسوله»

6- The path of repentance is open even for those who plotted to kill the Prophet. «فان یتوبوا» (But if they repent).

7- Hypocrites are also punished in this world, because the system of causes and effects in the universe is based on truth. «یعذّبهم ... فى الدنیا» (He will punish them... in the world). (Wheat grows from wheat, barley from barley).

8- The punishment of the hypocrites in this world includes helplessness, confusion, and inner terror, as they are constantly anxious. [126] «یعذّبهم... فى الدنیا»

9- Hypocrites are ultimately without helpers; their organizations collapse, and they become isolated and humiliated. They benefit neither from the support of the people nor the support of governments. «ما لهم فى الارض من ولىٍ ولانصیر» (They will have on earth no protector and no helper).

125) Similar to Ayah 62 of this same Surah: «اللَّه ورسوله احقّ ان یرضوه» (Allah and His Messenger are more worthy that they should please Him), and He did not say: "that they should please both of them."

126) «یحسبون كل صیحةٍ علیهم» (They think every cry is against them) (Al-Munafiqun, 4).

Ayahs

۞ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ عَـٰهَدَ ٱللَّهَ لَئِنْ ءَاتَىٰنَا مِن فَضْلِهِۦ لَنَصَّدَّقَنَّ وَلَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۷٥)
Among them are those who made a pledge with Allah: ‘If He gives us out of His grace, we will surely give the zakat and we will surely be among the righteous.’ (75)
فَلَمَّآ ءَاتَىٰهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦ بَخِلُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَتَوَلَّوا۟ وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ (۷٦)
But when He gave them out of His grace, they begrudged it and turned away, being disregardful. (76)

Key Points

A poor Muslim from Medina, (Tha'labah ibn Hatib), requested the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to pray that Allah would make him wealthy. The Prophet said: A small amount of wealth for which you render thanks is better than a large amount of wealth whose thanks you cannot fulfill. Tha'labah said: If God grants it, I will give all the obligatory rights due upon it. Due to the prayer of the Prophet, his wealth increased to the point where he could no longer participate in Friday and congregational prayers. When the agent collecting Zakat went to him, he told him: We became Muslims so that we would not have to pay Jizyah (poll tax)! [127] Fakhr Razi says: Tha'labah regretted his action and brought his Zakat to the Prophet, but the Prophet did not accept it. Indeed, man does not know what is beneficial and good for him; thus, sometimes he insists on something that is detrimental to him. Therefore, one must be content with what Allah has given. A similar instance of failing to fulfill a covenant with God is mentioned in Surah Al-A'raf: «دعوا اللَّه ربّهما لئن اتیتنا صالحاً لنكوننّ من الشاكرین. فلمّا آتاهما صالحاً جعلا له شركاءَ» [128] A husband and wife pledged that if Allah gave them a righteous child, they would be grateful to Him, but when they were granted a righteous child, they ascribed partners to God and forgot the covenant.

127) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 128) Al-A'raf, 189 - 190.

Messages

1- Man is ungrateful and a covenant-breaker. «عاهداللَّه... لنصّدقنّ، بخلوا به»

2- Covenants and promises made during poverty and distress are not highly reliable. «فلمّا آتاهم... بخلوا»

3- If there is no merit or capacity, divine blessings turn into misfortune for man. Wealth is valuable only if it does not become a cause for downfall. «اتاهم من فضله ...تولّوا و هم معرضون»

4- Attachment to the world leads man to a bad end. Whoever is stingy in paying Zakat and charities gradually turns away from religion. «بخلوا... و هم معرضون»

Ayah

فَأَعْقَبَهُمْ نِفَاقًا فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يَلْقَوْنَهُۥ بِمَآ أَخْلَفُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا وَعَدُوهُ وَبِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْذِبُونَ (۷۷)
So He caused hypocrisy to ensue in their hearts until the day they will encounter Him, because of their going back on what they had promised Allah and because of the lies they used to tell. (77)

Messages

1- Breaking a promise with Allah and being stingy towards the deprived leads a person to a bad consequence. «بخلوا به... فاعقبهم نفاقاً»

2- Hypocrisy has degrees: verbal hypocrisy, behavioral hypocrisy, and heart hypocrisy. «فى قلوبهم»

3- Hypocrisy is sometimes temporary and sometimes permanent and perpetual. [129] «الى یوم یلقونه»

4- Breaking covenants and lying bring about the spirit of hypocrisy. Sin brings about sin. «فاعقبهم نفاقاً... بما اخلفوا... وبما كانوا یكذبون»

5- The source of man's misfortunes is himself. «بما اخلفوا، بما كانوا یكذبون»

129) Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The meaning of «یوم یلقونه» is the Day of Resurrection. Bihar, Vol. 90, p. 132.

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّهُمْ وَنَجْوَىٰهُمْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَّـٰمُ ٱلْغُيُوبِ (۷۸)
Do they not know that Allah knows their secret [thoughts] and [hears] their secret talks, and that Allah is knower of all that is Unseen? (78)

Key Points

| Perhaps the meaning of «سِرّ» (secret) is the inner disbelief of the hypocrites, and the meaning of «نَجوى » (secret counsel) is their group conspiracy, and the purpose of «غُیوب» (unseen things) is their goals and intentions. [130]

130) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- The hypocrites strive to keep their conspiracies hidden, but Allah warns them about their secrets. «ألم یعلموا...»

2- Incomplete knowledge leads to incomplete performance (action). «اخلفوا اللّه ما وعدوه... ألم یعلموا...»

Yes, if a person knows that Allah is aware of his open and hidden deeds, his piety (taqwa) increases, his hypocrisy vanishes, and he will not be reproached.

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ ٱلْمُطَّوِّعِينَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِى ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتِ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ فَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنْهُمْ ۙ سَخِرَ ٱللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۷۹)
Those who blame the voluntary donors from among the faithful concerning the charities—and as for those who do not find [anything] except [what] their means [permit], they ridicule them—Allah shall put them to ridicule, and there is a painful punishment for them. (79)

Key Points

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله requested aid from the people for the front (Jihad). The wealthy provided significant help, while the poor provided little aid. The hypocrites considered the aid of the wealthy to be ostentatious (Riya'), criticizing and slandering it, and they mocked the aid of the destitute. [131] Among them was Abu Aqil al-Ansari, who, by working extra hours, brought some dates to the Prophet for aid to the front. The hypocrites mocked him as well. Possessing wealth and riches is not a prerequisite for spending (in charity/Infaq); rather, one must possess sincerity (Ikhlas), faith, and generosity. We read in Ayah 79 that Tha'labah, despite all his wealth, did not pay the obligatory Divine right, while Abu Aqil, who was a simple laborer, sincerely aided the front of Islam with a few dates. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The meaning of Allah mocking them, "سخر اللّه منهم" (Sakhira Allahu minhum), is that Allah gives them the punishment for their mockery. [132]

131) "یَلمِزون" (Yalmazūn), which means to slander or criticize, refers to the wealthy believers, and "یَسخرون" (Yaskharūn) refers to the poor believers. 132) Bihar, Vol. 3, p. 319.

Messages

1- Mockery is the work of the hypocrites. They do not help themselves, and they also question the aid provided by others. "الذین یلمزون"

2- Believers spend (in charity) willingly and eagerly. "المطوعین" (meaning, out of desire/willingness)

3- Undermining the morale of those who aid the front is forbidden (Haram) and an act of hypocrisy. "یلمزون المطوعین ... لهم عذاب الیم"

4- Hypocrites seek to discourage and demotivate people regarding Jihad through propaganda and mockery. "یلمزون ... فیسخرون" [133]

5- In the view of the hypocrites, the amount of wealth is important, not the motives and characteristics; therefore, they criticize the small spending of the poor believers. "یلمزون ... الذین لایجدون الاّ جُهدهم"

6- To meet the needs of society, all available resources must be utilized. "جهدهم"

7- The responsibility of every person is commensurate with his ability. "لایجدون الاّ جهدهم"

8- We must fulfill our duty and not fear the mockery of the enemy. "لایجدون الاّ جهدهم" because Allah will give them their recompense. "سخراللّه منهم"

9- Punishment must be proportionate to the action. "یسخرون, سخراللَّه"

133) In Ayah 7 of Surah Al-Munafiqun, we read: "هم الذین یقولون لاتُنفقوا على مَن عند رسول اللَّه حتّى ینفضّوا" (They are the ones who say, 'Do not spend on those who are with the Messenger of Allah until they disperse.') (Meaning: Do not give financial aid to the companions of the Prophet until they scatter.)

Ayah

ٱسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ أَوْ لَا تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ إِن تَسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةً فَلَن يَغْفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۸٠)
Whether you plead forgiveness for them or do not plead forgiveness for them, even if you plead forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah shall never forgive them because they defied Allah and His Apostle; and Allah does not guide the transgressing lot. (80)

Key Points

The number seventy is a symbol of abundance and multiplicity, not an expression of a specific count. Meaning, no matter how much you seek forgiveness (Istighfar) for them, it is ineffective, not that they would be forgiven if, for example, it reached seventy-one times. Similar to Ayah 6 of Surah Al-Munafiqun: «سواء علیهم استغفرت لهم ام لم تستغفر لهم لن یغفر اللَّه لهم» (It is the same for them whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them – never will Allah forgive them), where no number is mentioned. It is also mentioned in narrations that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: If I knew that seeking forgiveness more than seventy times would save them, I would seek forgiveness. [134] A person reaches a point in degradation where nothing can save him, like a patient whose soul has departed; the efforts of all doctors are ineffective.

134) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The sin of mocking religion is so severe that even the Istighfar (seeking forgiveness) of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is ineffective. «فیسخرون منهم... فلن یغفراللَّه لهم»

2- Unless a person transforms himself and finds an inner revolution, the prayer of the Prophet is also ineffective. «فلن یغفراللَّه ...كفروا باللَّه»

3- Compassion for the stubborn hypocrite is useless. «ان تستغفر... فلن یغفراللَّه»

4- Allah is not stingy in guidance; the hypocrites have lost their capacity (for it). «كفروا»

5- Disbelief (Kufr) prevents forgiveness (Maghfirah), and transgression (Fisq) prevents guidance. «كفروا، فاسقین»

Ayah

فَرِحَ ٱلْمُخَلَّفُونَ بِمَقْعَدِهِمْ خِلَـٰفَ رَسُولِ ٱللَّهِ وَكَرِهُوٓا۟ أَن يُجَـٰهِدُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَقَالُوا۟ لَا تَنفِرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْحَرِّ ۗ قُلْ نَارُ جَهَنَّمَ أَشَدُّ حَرًّا ۚ لَّوْ كَانُوا۟ يَفْقَهُونَ (۸۱)
Those who were left behind boasted for sitting back against [the command of] the Apostle of Allah, and were reluctant to wage jihad with their possessions and persons in the way of Allah, and they said, ‘Do not go forth in this heat.’ Say, The fire of hell is severer in heat, should they understand. (81)

Key Points

The occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) for this Ayah concerns the Battle of Tabuk. This Ayah mentions three signs for the hypocrites (Munafiqun): A: They are happy about not going to the front (Jabhah) instead of being regretful. B: Jihad with wealth and life is burdensome for them. C: They prevent others from going to the front. According to the previous Ayahs, the hypocrites prevented others from giving financial aid; corresponding to this Ayah, they also violate the command to be present at the front and discourage others.

Messages

1- Joy over not going to the front and violating the command of the Prophet is a sign of hypocrisy. «فرح المخلّفون» 2- Worse than not going to the front is breaking the sanctity of leadership and violating his command. «بمقعدهم خلاف رسول اللَّه», He did not say: «بمقعدهم عن الجهاد» 3- Unquestioning obedience to the Messenger of Allah is necessary. «بمقعدهم خلاف رسول اللَّه» 4- A fearful believer is distinct from a hypocrite. The fearful person provides financial aid and has a heartfelt desire for Jihad. But the hypocrites neither aid nor desire to aid. «كرهوا ان یجاهدوا» 5- To deter people from Jihad, the hypocrites use discouraging propaganda and disruption, citing problems like hot weather as an excuse. «قالوا لاتنفروا فى الحرّ» 6- On the eve of war, we must be prepared to quickly neutralize the rumors and discouraging words of the hypocrites. «قالوا، قل» 7- When responding to the enemy's propaganda, we should use their own terminology. «الحرّ، اشد حرّاً» 8- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a factor driving movement toward the front. «قل نار جهنم» True believers never consider hot weather an obstacle to Jihad. 9- Hypocrites are superficial and lack deep understanding. «لو كانوا یفقهون»

Ayah

فَلْيَضْحَكُوا۟ قَلِيلًا وَلْيَبْكُوا۟ كَثِيرًا جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۸۲)
So let them laugh a little; much will they weep as a requital for what they used to earn. (82)

Key Points

If the hypocrites knew what rewards they have forfeited and what opportunities and blessings they have been ungrateful for due to abandoning Jihad, they ought to rejoice little and weep much. Even their lifelong weeping is insignificant compared to the prolonged weeping of the Day of Resurrection!

Messages

1- Wailing and weeping over afflictions is the worldly punishment for those who lagged behind from Jihad. «فرح المخلّفون... و لیبكوا كثیراً جزاء»

2- The punishment is commensurate with the deed. «فرح المخلّفون .. ولیبكوا كثیراً»

3- The few days of laughter and joy enjoyed by the hypocrite lead to prolonged regret and weeping. «فرح المخلّفون ...و لیبكوا كثیراً»

Ayah

فَإِن رَّجَعَكَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَىٰ طَآئِفَةٍ مِّنْهُمْ فَٱسْتَـْٔذَنُوكَ لِلْخُرُوجِ فَقُل لَّن تَخْرُجُوا۟ مَعِىَ أَبَدًا وَلَن تُقَـٰتِلُوا۟ مَعِىَ عَدُوًّا ۖ إِنَّكُمْ رَضِيتُم بِٱلْقُعُودِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ فَٱقْعُدُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلْخَـٰلِفِينَ (۸۳)
If Allah brings you back [from the battlefront] to a group of them and they seek your permission to go forth, say, ‘You shall never go forth with me, and you shall not fight with me against any enemy. You were indeed pleased to sit back the first time, so sit back with those who stay behind.’ (83)

Key Points

The term khālif (خالف) means both one who lags behind from war, one who opposes, and also refers to someone who lacks the ability to go to the front due to old age, illness, or incapacity. Whoever sincerely repents is accepted, but the hypocrites requested to go to the front out of hypocrisy (riyā), pretense, and revolutionary showmanship. It is inferred from this Ayah that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله personally departed from Medina for the Battle of Tabuk and returned there at the end of the war. Furthermore, this Ayah informs about the future encounter with the hypocrites, so that the Prophet may expose their hypocritical faces.

Messages

1- We should not trust the hypocrites' requests to participate in Jihad. «فان رجعك... فاستأذنوك فقل لن تخروجوا...» (If Allah brings you back... and they ask your permission, say: You shall never go forth...) They ask permission to stay behind when the army moves out, and they ask permission to leave and move out when the army returns!

2- We should beware of those who were fugitives yesterday and are volunteers for the front today. «لن تخرجوا معى ابداً» (You shall never go forth with me.)

3- We should not forget the bad record of individuals. «رضیتم بالقعود اوّل مرّة» (You were content to stay behind the first time.)

4- We should humiliate and boycott the hypocrites. «فاقعدوا مع الخالفین» (Then sit with those who remain behind.) Fighting alongside the Prophet is a divine success (tawfīq) from which hypocrites, who rejoice in abandoning the front, must be permanently deprived. «لن تخرجوا معى ابداً و لن تقاتلوا معى» (You shall never go forth with me, nor shall you fight with me.)

Ayah

وَلَا تُصَلِّ عَلَىٰٓ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُم مَّاتَ أَبَدًا وَلَا تَقُمْ عَلَىٰ قَبْرِهِۦٓ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَمَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (۸٤)
And never pray over any of them when he dies, nor stand at his graveside. They indeed defied Allah and His Apostle and died as transgressors. (84)

Key Points

It was the practice (Sīrah) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله to attend the funeral and burial ceremonies of deceased Muslims, to pray for them, and to offer the funeral prayer (Ṣalāt al-Janāzah) over their bodies. Through this Ayah, Allah forbade His Prophet from attending the ceremonies of deceased hypocrites. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used to pray for the deceased after the fourth Takbīr (in the funeral prayer), but after the revelation of this Ayah, He no longer performed this action. [135]

135) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 181.

Messages

1- One of the methods of combating hypocrites is passive resistance (negative struggle). «لاتُصلّ، لاتقم»

2- The deceased hypocrite must also be humiliated. We should not participate in their funeral processions nor visit their graves. «لاتصلّ، لاتقم»

3- We should use the leverage of prayer to punish transgressors. «لاتصلّ على احد منهم»

4- The funeral prayer (Ṣalāt al-Mayyit) and visiting graves are signs of honor and respect for the believer after death and are commendable acts; since the hypocrite holds no honor, He said: «لا تصلّ، لاتقم»

5- The hypocrite is an unbeliever (kāfir) and a transgressor (fāsiq). «كفروا، فاسقون»

6- The final outcome is important. The danger lies when a person dies without repentance and while in a state of transgression (fisq). «ماتوا و هم فاسقون»

Ayah

وَلَا تُعْجِبْكَ أَمْوَٰلُهُمْ وَأَوْلَـٰدُهُمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ أَن يُعَذِّبَهُم بِهَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَتَزْهَقَ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَهُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۸٥)
Let not their possessions or their children impress you. Allah only desires to punish them with these in this world, and that their souls may depart while they are faithless. (85)

Key Points

The hypocrites during the early days of Islam possessed abundant wealth, children, and resources, but the Muslims did not. There was a danger that the Muslims might become captivated (by these things), which is why this verse and verse 55 of this Surah (which is similar to this verse with a slight difference in wording) warn the Muslims. "زَهوق" (zahūq) means the departure (of the soul) accompanied by hardship, regret, and sorrow.

Messages

1- We should not feel inferior because of the resources and possessions of others. «لاتعجبك»

2- Wealth and children are sometimes a source of trial and torment, not comfort and happiness. «یعذّبهم بها فى الدنیا»

3- Allah's punishment in this world is sometimes administered through those very worldly manifestations. «یرید اللَّه ان یعذّبهم بها فى الدنیا»

4- Death is the departure of the soul from the body, not the annihilation of the soul. «تزهق انفسهم»

5- A lifetime of disbelief (kufr) and ingratitude causes a person to leave this world as a disbeliever at the moment of death. «تزهق انفسهم و هم كافرون»

6- The criterion for values is a good ultimate outcome (husn al-'āqibah) and dying with faith, not the fleeting worldly glitter of a few days. «تزهق انفسهم و هم كافرون»

Ayahs

وَإِذَآ أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ أَنْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَجَـٰهِدُوا۟ مَعَ رَسُولِهِ ٱسْتَـْٔذَنَكَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلطَّوْلِ مِنْهُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ ذَرْنَا نَكُن مَّعَ ٱلْقَـٰعِدِينَ (۸٦)
When a surah is sent down [declaring]: ‘Have faith in Allah, and wage jihad along with His Apostle, the affluent among them ask you for leave, and say, ‘Let us remain with those who sit back.’ (86)
رَضُوا۟ بِأَن يَكُونُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلْخَوَالِفِ وَطُبِعَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ (۸۷)
They are pleased to be with those who stay back, and their hearts have been sealed. So they do not understand. (87)

Key Points

The word «طَوْل» (Tawl) means resources and capability. «اولوا الطّول» (Ulul-Tawl) means the capable and the wealthy. The intent of Surah is a collection of verses that express a specific topic. Therefore, a part of a Surah is also sometimes called a Surah.

Messages

1- Jihad (striving) is a prerequisite for faith in Allah. «امنوا باللَّه و جاهدوا»

2- The Prophet was at the forefront of others on the battlefields. «مع رسوله»

3- Jihad must be by the command of the leader of the Muslims and alongside and in step with him. «جاهدوا مع رسوله...»

4- We should not justify our weakness, hypocrisy, or fear of going to Jihad by seeking permission for exemption. «استأذنك»

5- The pampered affluent fear Jihad the most; we should not place hope in them. «استأذنك اولوا الطول»

6- The hypocrites have dead hearts (or, their hearts are sealed). «طبع على قلوبهم»

7- Worldliness, excessive comfort-seeking, and the spirit of hypocrisy deprive a person of correct insight and deep understanding. «لایفقهون»

Ayah

لَـٰكِنِ ٱلرَّسُولُ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ جَـٰهَدُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلْخَيْرَٰتُ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۸۸)
But the Apostle and the faithful who are with him wage jihad with their possessions and persons, and to such belong all the blessings, and it is they who are the felicitous. (88)

Messages

1- We should not worry about the non-participation of the hypocrites and the affluent in the war. «لكن الرسول...»

2- Let the hypocrites not imagine that Islam will remain unsupported due to their failure to join Jihad. «لكن الرسول...»

3- The leader is at the forefront of the combatants. «الرسول والذین آمنوا معه»

4- Faith in the Prophet alone is not sufficient; accompanying him is also necessary. «امنوا معه»

5- Jihad must be comprehensive in all dimensions. «جاهدوا باموالهم وانفسهم»

6- Prosperity is achieved only in the shadow of faith and Jihad. «اولئك هم المفلحون»

7- War and Jihad are the source of the descent of divine goodness and blessings upon the combatants. «اولئك لهم الخیرات»

8- The Mujahidin are prosperous, whether they achieve victory or suffer apparent defeat. «جاهدوا، مفلحون»

Ayah

أَعَدَّ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۸۹)
Allah has prepared for them gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever]. That is the great success. (89)

Messages

1- The reward for those who strive (Mujahideen) has been prepared in advance. «اعدّ اللَّه لهم جنّات»

2- Paradise (Jannah) has been created and exists right now. «اعدّ اللَّه لهم جنّات»

3- We should utilize natural instincts and desires for the path of growth and guidance. (Man naturally seeks gardens and rivers.) «جنّات تجرى من تحتها الانهار»

4- Permanent residence in the Paradise of Divine Mercy is true felicity. «الفوز العظیم»

Ayah

وَجَآءَ ٱلْمُعَذِّرُونَ مِنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ لِيُؤْذَنَ لَهُمْ وَقَعَدَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ ۚ سَيُصِيبُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۹٠)
Some of the Bedouins who sought to be excused came, so that they may be granted leave [to stay back]; while those who lied to Allah and His Apostle sat back. Soon there shall visit the faithless among them a painful punishment. (90)

Key Points

The word al-mu'adhdhirūn (مُعذّرون) can mean both al-mu'tadhirūn (معتَذرون), referring to those who offer genuine excuses and have a valid reason, and those who fabricate excuses. [136] Some had genuine excuses and sought permission (leave) for that reason, but others did not participate in Jihad without an excuse, and the punishment is for this latter group. A‘rāb (اَعراب) refers to the Bedouins who live in the deserts and are far removed from urban civilization.

136) In the terminology of Arabic literature, it is either from the Bāb Taf‘īl (which implies fabricating a false excuse) or from the Bāb Ifti‘āl (which implies having a genuine excuse). (Tafsir Rahnama)

Messages

1- During the Battle of Tabūk, there was a general mobilization order, and anyone who did not participate had to state his excuse to the Prophet and formally seek permission. jā’a al-mu‘adhdhirūn (جاء المعذّرون)

2- Jihad is a governmental matter, not an individual one; therefore, both presence on the front and leaving it must be with the permission of the leader of the Muslims. [137] jā’a al-mu‘adhdhirūn... liyu’dhana lahum (جاء المعذّرون ... لیؤذن لهم)

3- Those who disregard the obligation of Jihad and seek to flee are lying about their faith. kadhābū (كَذَبوا)

4- Lying is not only by the tongue; a person's actions sometimes indicate the falsehood of their claim. qa‘ada alladhīna kadhabū (قعد الذین كذبوا)

5- Those who make excuses to evade duty will soon be punished. al-mu‘adhdhirūn... sayuṣīb... ‘adhābun alīm (المعذّرون... سیصیب... عذابٌ الیم)

137) Regarding presence on the front, we read in the previous verses: fa-ista’dhanūka lil-khurūji fa-qul lan takhrujū ma‘ī abadan (فاستأذنوك للخروج فقل لن تخرجوا معى ابدا)

Ayah

لَّيْسَ عَلَى ٱلضُّعَفَآءِ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْمَرْضَىٰ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ مَا يُنفِقُونَ حَرَجٌ إِذَا نَصَحُوا۟ لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ۚ مَا عَلَى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ مِن سَبِيلٍ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۹۱)
There is no blame on the weak, nor on the sick, nor on those who do not find anything to spend, so long as they are sincere to Allah and His Apostle. There is no [cause for] blaming the virtuous, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (91)

Key Points

A blind old man came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "I have no one to take my hand and lead me to the battlefield. I am weak and elderly. Is my excuse acceptable?" The Prophet remained silent until this Ayah was revealed. How astonishing are the lovers of Islam, whose consciences are still uneasy despite their old age and blindness, and who ask the Prophet about abandoning the front! It is possible that the meaning of "lacking means" is that they cannot provide for the living expenses of their wives and children without their presence in the family, and if they go to the front, their families will go hungry. (138)

In Islam, there is no command that causes hardship (haraj) or is beyond one's capacity. Islam is flexible and has no dead ends. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Man is not obligated to perform any task that he is unable to do," and then He recited this Ayah. (139)

(138) Tafsir Rahnama. (139) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 165.

Messages

1- Divine obligations are commensurate with human capacity. «لیس على الضعفاء....حرج» (The weak and the sick, who have physical incapacities, are exempt from Jihad with their lives, and the poor, who have financial incapacities, are exempt from Jihad with their wealth.)

2- Goodwill (sincerity/advising) is never waived. The weak must also support in whatever way they can. Even the elderly blind person is obligated to offer prayers with his tongue and heart and show goodwill toward the fighters. «اذانصحوا»

3- Those who are excused from Jihad but have a desire for it are among the doers of good. «المحسنین»

4- Those from whom harm results without fault or negligence, and who possess good intentions, shall not be held accountable and shall not pay compensation. (140) «ما على المحسنین من سبیل»

(140) For example, an honest employee who is caught by robbers while transferring bank money, or a porter who suffers an accident while carrying a load. Therefore, any corrective action taken with good and selfless intentions, where unforeseen accidents and damages occur, should not require compensation. In jurisprudence (Fiqh), there is also a rule called "Ihsan (doing good) and the negation of Haraj (hardship)."

Ayah

وَلَا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا مَآ أَتَوْكَ لِتَحْمِلَهُمْ قُلْتَ لَآ أَجِدُ مَآ أَحْمِلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ تَوَلَّوا۟ وَّأَعْيُنُهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ ٱلدَّمْعِ حَزَنًا أَلَّا يَجِدُوا۟ مَا يُنفِقُونَ (۹۲)
Nor [is there any blame] on those to whom, when they came to you to provide them with a mount, you said, ‘I do not find any mount for you,’ and they turned back, their eyes flowing with tears, grieved because they did not find any means to spend. (92)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about those who lacked the financial ability to aid the Jihad. In this Ayah, the discussion is about the poor who, due to poverty, lack a mount (transport) to be present on the front lines. Therefore, they grieve and shed tears, and for this reason, they share in the reward of the fighters. Just as Hasan al-Basri says: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله used to tell the fighters of the Battle of Tabuk: In every wealth you spent and every harm you suffered, a group of those who remained behind in Medina share with you, because they were sincerely desirous of participating on the front lines. [141]

141) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur.

Messages

1- Providing the war resources for the fighters is the responsibility of the government. «اَتوك لتحملهم»

2- The worth of human beings lies in their motivations and spirits, not merely in their actions or material and financial resources. «اعینهم تفیض من الدّمع » (Their eyes overflow with tears.) (The Ayah praises those who lack money and resources but possess faith and the burning desire and passion to go to the front lines.)

3- A believer is distressed and weeping over his inability to participate on the front lines. «تفیض من الدمع»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّمَا ٱلسَّبِيلُ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَـْٔذِنُونَكَ وَهُمْ أَغْنِيَآءُ ۚ رَضُوا۟ بِأَن يَكُونُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلْخَوَالِفِ وَطَبَعَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۹۳)
The blame lies only on those who ask leave of you [to stay behind] though they are well-off. They are pleased to be with those who stay back; Allah has set a seal on their hearts, so they do not know [the outcome of their conduct]. (93)

Key Points

How vast is the difference between the believer and the hypocrite! In the previous Ayah, we saw that the believer cries and sheds tears because he lacks the means to go to the battlefield, [as stated in] «اعینهم تفیض من الدّمع», but in this Ayah, we read that the wealthy hypocrites insist on abandoning the battlefield and seek permission from the Prophet!!

Messages

1- The poor and the rich are equal in going to the battlefield, and wealth is neither a privilege for abandoning the battlefield nor an excuse for it. «...على الذین یستأذنونك و هم اغنیاء»

2- Shirking responsibility leads to evil consequences and the loss of correct understanding. «طبع، لایعلمون»

Ayah

يَعْتَذِرُونَ إِلَيْكُمْ إِذَا رَجَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ ۚ قُل لَّا تَعْتَذِرُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكُمْ قَدْ نَبَّأَنَا ٱللَّهُ مِنْ أَخْبَارِكُمْ ۚ وَسَيَرَى ٱللَّهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُۥ ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَـٰلِمِ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۹٤)
They will offer you excuses when you return to them. Say, ‘Do not make excuses; we will never believe you. Allah has informed us of your state of affairs. Allah and His Apostle will observe your conduct, then you will be returned to the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, and He will inform you concerning what you used to do.’ (94)

Key Points

Approximately eighty of the hypocrites did not participate in the Battle of Tabuk. When the Prophet and the Muslims returned from the war, they presented various excuses to justify their actions. The verse (Ayah) was revealed, instructing (the believers) not to believe what they say and to leave them to Allah.

Messages

1- Muslims must be so powerful that those who violate (the command) feel obliged to apologize. «یعتذرون»

2- We must deal decisively with hypocrites who fabricate excuses. «لاتعتذروا لن نؤمن لكم»

3- Allah informs His Prophet about the news of the hypocrites and the deeds of the people through the unseen (Ghayb). «قد نبّأنا اللّه»

4- After the end of the war, the fabrication of excuses and pretexts begins. «یعتذرون ...اذا رجعتم»

5- Allah’s knowledge regarding the unseen (Ghayb) and the witnessed (Shahadah)—the manifest and the hidden—is one, and nothing is concealed from Him. «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»

6- Remembering the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah) is one of the best factors for building up a person. «تُردّون، فینبّئكم»

7- Man is responsible for all his deeds, and the Day of Resurrection is the day of exposure (or disgrace). «فینبّئكم...»

Ayah

سَيَحْلِفُونَ بِٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ إِذَا ٱنقَلَبْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ لِتُعْرِضُوا۟ عَنْهُمْ ۖ فَأَعْرِضُوا۟ عَنْهُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ رِجْسٌ ۖ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۹٥)
They will swear to you by Allah when you return to them, that you may leave them alone. So leave them alone. They are indeed filth, and their refuge shall be hell, a requital for what they used to earn. (95)

Key Points

I'rāḍ (turning away/disregard) is either based on magnanimity and forgiveness, or based on wrath and indifference. In this Ayah, it is used in both meanings; the hypocrites requested I'rāḍ (overlooking) their mistake, [but] Allah commanded a wrathful I'rāḍ (turning away). [142] The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, after returning from Tabuk, commanded the people not to associate with the hypocrites who abandoned the front. [143] In the previous Ayah, their excuses and pretexts were not accepted. In this Ayah, because they found themselves in a position of weakness and humiliation, they resorted to swearing oaths.

142) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi. 143) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

1- Not every oath should be trusted. «سیحلفون باللَّه ...فاعرضوا عنهم»

2- Hypocrites misuse sacred things and the Name of Allah. «سیحلفون باللَّه»

3- Muslims must practice Tawallī (loving for Allah) and Tabarrī (disassociating for Allah) and take a firm stance. «اعرضوا عنهم»

4- We must cut ties with those who abandon the front and Jihad without excuse, boycott them, isolate them, and make life difficult for them. «اعرضوا عنهم»

5- We must distance ourselves from corrupt individuals and environments, because the spirit of hypocrisy and human flaws are transmitted to others. «اعرضوا عنهم انّهم رجس»

6- Hypocrisy is inner impurity and a sickness of the soul. «انّهم رجس»

7- Hell is the recompense and result of man's own actions, not Divine vengeance. «جزاءً بماكانوا یكسبون»

Ayah

يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ لِتَرْضَوْا۟ عَنْهُمْ ۖ فَإِن تَرْضَوْا۟ عَنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَرْضَىٰ عَنِ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۹٦)
They swear to you that you may be reconciled to them. But even if you are reconciled to them, Allah shall not be reconciled to the transgressing lot. (96)

Key Points

Perhaps their oath in the previous Ayah was intended so that they would not be exposed (or challenged). In this Ayah, besides being overlooked and not being challenged by the Muslims, they also seek the Muslims' heartfelt satisfaction. «لترضوا عنهم» (that you may be satisfied with them). The hypocrites did not value the heartfelt satisfaction of the Muslims; their insistence on satisfaction was merely to remain safe from the Muslims' punitive reaction, so that they could maintain their social standing and influence in society! The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مَن اِلتمسَ رضى اللَّه بسخطِ الناسِ رضىَ اللَّه عنه و ارضى عنه الناسَ و من التمس رضى الناس بسخطِ اللَّهِ سخط اللَّه علیه واسَخَط علیه الناسَ» Whoever seeks Allah's satisfaction at the expense of people's anger, Allah becomes satisfied with him and makes the people satisfied with him. And whoever seeks to gain people's satisfaction at the expense of Allah's anger, Allah becomes dissatisfied with him and makes the hearts of the people angry and dissatisfied with him. [144]

144) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The hypocrites seek to gain the people's approval and maintain their standing among them, and they have no concern for Allah's satisfaction or repentance. «لترضوا عنهم»

2- We must seek divine satisfaction, not the satisfaction of the people, because the forgiveness of the people is not important; Allah's pardon and wrath are important. «فان ترضوا عنهم فان اللَّه لا یرضى»

3- Since Allah is not satisfied with the hypocrites, those who become satisfied with the hypocrites have separated their path from Allah. «فان ترضوا... فان اللَّه لا یرضى»

4- Gaining the satisfaction of the believers does not always necessitate gaining the satisfaction of Allah. «فان ترضوا... فان اللَّه لا یرضى»

5- Never be satisfied with the transgressor (fasiq) as long as he persists in transgression. «فان اللَّه لا یرضى عن القوم الفاسقین»

Ayah

ٱلْأَعْرَابُ أَشَدُّ كُفْرًا وَنِفَاقًا وَأَجْدَرُ أَلَّا يَعْلَمُوا۟ حُدُودَ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۹۷)
The Bedouins are more obdurate in unfaith and hypocrisy, and more apt to be ignorant of the precepts that Allah has sent down to His Apostle, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (97)

Key Points

"Al-A'rāb" (الاعراب) means the desert dwellers (Bedouins). Its singular form is "A'rābī" (اعرابی), not "Arab" (عرب). Being an A'rābī signifies distance from religious culture and the teachings of the school (of thought/faith). The word "Al-A'rāb" has appeared ten times in the Qur'an, and except for one instance, all other occurrences, like the narrations (Riwāyāt), are condemnatory. It is stated in a Hadith: «تفقّهوا فى الدّین فانّه مَن لم یتفقّه فى الدین فهو اعرابى» (Gain deep understanding in religion, for whoever does not gain deep understanding in religion is an A'rābī). Become knowledgeable in religion, because whoever lacks a deep religious understanding is an A'rābī. In another Hadith, we read: «نحن بنوهاشم و شیعتنا العربُ و سایر الناسِ اعرابٌ» Our companions and followers are Arabs, and others are A'rāb. (145) The people during the era of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله were of two groups: city dwellers and desert dwellers (Bedouins). Since the preceding verses were about the victory of the Muslims in the Battle of Tabūk, this verse perhaps indicates that you should not be heedless of the ignorant and neighboring desert dwellers, who might sometimes incite unrest at the provocation of enemies.

145) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Being a desert dweller leads to distance from religious culture and manners. «الاعراب اشدّكفراً»

2- A person's social and cultural environment is effective in his outlook and stance regarding truths and knowledge. «الاعراب اشدّكفراً»

3- Ignorant individuals who are far from culture sometimes become tools in the hands of the disbelievers and hypocrites, becoming even more illogical than them. «الاعراب اشدّ كفراً و نفاقاً»

4- Disbelief (Kufr) and hypocrisy (Nifāq) have degrees. «اشدّ كفراً و نفاقاً»

5- One of the factors leading to disbelief and hypocrisy is ignorance. «اشدّ كفراً... اَلاّ یعلموا»

6- Knowing the rulings of religion is necessary, otherwise we are A'rābī. «یعلموا حدود ما انزل اللَّه»

7- City dwellers who are ignorant of the divine limits are considered equivalent to the A'rāb and desert dwellers, or even worse than them. «اَلاّ یعلموا حدود ما انزل اللَّه»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مَا يُنفِقُ مَغْرَمًا وَيَتَرَبَّصُ بِكُمُ ٱلدَّوَآئِرَ ۚ عَلَيْهِمْ دَآئِرَةُ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۹۸)
Among the Bedouins are those who regard what they spend as a loss, and they watch for a reversal of your fortunes. Theirs shall be an adverse turn of fortune, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (98)

Key Points

| «مَغرَم», derived from «غرامت» (indemnity/liability), means adherence or persistence. The debtor and the creditor who do not leave each other are called «غَریم». And the debt that adheres to a person until he pays it is called «مَغرم». «دَوائر» (plural of «دائرة») refers to calamities or incidents that surround a person from every side.

Messages

1- The hypocrite (munāfiq) and he who lacks knowledge and faith in the divine limits and Islamic culture considers spending (infāq) a loss. «یتخذ ما ینفق مغرماً»

2- The hypocrite is envious and desires nothing but evil for others. «یتربّص بكم الدّوائر»

3- Whoever wishes evil for others will himself be afflicted by it. «یتربّص بكم الدوائر، علیهم دائرة السوء»

4- Allah is aware of man's sayings and characteristics. «اللَّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَيَتَّخِذُ مَا يُنفِقُ قُرُبَـٰتٍ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَصَلَوَٰتِ ٱلرَّسُولِ ۚ أَلَآ إِنَّهَا قُرْبَةٌ لَّهُمْ ۚ سَيُدْخِلُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى رَحْمَتِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۹۹)
Yet among the Bedouins are [also] those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, and regard what they spend as [a means of attaining] nearness to Allah and the blessings of the Apostle. Now, it shall indeed bring them nearness, and Allah will admit them into His mercy. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (99)

Messages

1- If we criticize a group, we should not forget the good people among them. Following the statement, "الاعراب اشدّ كفراً" (The Bedouins are stronger in disbelief), He says: "و من الاعراب من یؤمن باللَّه..." (And among the Bedouins are some who believe in Allah...).

2- Bedouin life and the social environment do not take away man's choice for growth. "و من الاعراب من یؤمن باللَّه..."

3- Faith is the prerequisite for spending (in charity) and tranquility. "یؤمن، یتّخذ ما ینفق قربات"

4- We should utilize material resources to attain divine proximity. "یتّخذ ماینفق قربات"

5- What causes proximity to Allah is sincere intention, not mere action. Both the believer and the hypocrite spend (in charity), but the spending of the believer is praiseworthy. "اَلا انّها قربة"

6- Working to gain the satisfaction of the Messenger of Allah does not contradict Tawhid (monotheism). "انّهاقربة"

7- The believing spender (in charity) is included in the supplication of the Messenger of Allah. "صلواتِ الرسول"

8- The believer is immersed in Divine Mercy, "فى رحمته," while the hypocrite is immersed in bitter misfortunes. "علیهم دائرة السَوء" (previous Ayah)

9- The sincere spending of the Bedouins, who come from an uncultured environment, is more worthy of appreciation and praise. "الا، انّ، فى رحمته، غفور، رحیم"

Ayah

وَٱلسَّـٰبِقُونَ ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُهَـٰجِرِينَ وَٱلْأَنصَارِ وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوهُم بِإِحْسَـٰنٍ رَّضِىَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى تَحْتَهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۱٠٠)
The early vanguard of the Emigrants and the Helpers and those who followed them in virtue—Allah is pleased with them and they are pleased with Him, and He has prepared for them gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. That is the great success. (100)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the Muslims of early Islam are divided into three groups: 1- The pioneers in Islam and Hijrah (migration). 2- The pioneers in supporting the Prophet and aiding the Muhajirun (migrants). 3- The later ones who followed their path. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: First, Allah mentioned the names of the early Muhajirun, then the names of the Ansar (helpers) and supporters, and then the names of their followers. Thus, He stated the position and status of each group in its proper place. (146) By the consensus of all Muslims, the first Muslim woman was Lady Khadijah, and the first Muslim man, by the consensus of the Shi'a and the view of many of the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunnis), is Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام. (147) As has been said: There is no disagreement among historians that Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام is the first Muslim man. (148) Of course, some people say: What value does the faith of a ten-year-old adolescent have? In response to them, it must be said: The prophethood of Prophet Isa (Jesus) and Prophet Yahya (John) was also in childhood, and it had value!

146) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 41. 147) Narrations are found in Al-Ghadir, (Vol. 3, pp. 220 - 240) and Ihqaq al-Haqq, (Vol. 3, pp. 114 - 120). 148) Tafsir Qurtubi, Vol. 5, p. 3075; quoted from Mustadrak Hakim.

Messages

1- Precedence in good deeds is valuable, and the position of pioneers in movements must be preserved. «السابقون الأوّلون»

2- Not all Muhajirun and Ansar are subject to Divine pleasure and praise by the Quran; rather, only some are. «مِن المهاجرین...» (The word «مِن» means 'some' or 'a part of'.)

3- Only following the goodness and perfections of predecessors is valuable, not every form of following or imitation. «اتّبعوهم باحسان»

4- If a believer is satisfied with the Divine decree, Allah is also satisfied with him. «رضى اللَّه عنهم ورضوا عنه»

5- Paradise is achieved through faith, precedence, Hijrah, support (Nusrah), and following with goodness (Ihsan). «السابقون... اعدّ لهم جنّات...»

Ayah

وَمِمَّنْ حَوْلَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ مُنَـٰفِقُونَ ۖ وَمِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْمَدِينَةِ ۖ مَرَدُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنِّفَاقِ لَا تَعْلَمُهُمْ ۖ نَحْنُ نَعْلَمُهُمْ ۚ سَنُعَذِّبُهُم مَّرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ يُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَذَابٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱٠۱)
There are hypocrites among the Bedouins around you and among the townspeople of Madinah, steeped in hypocrisy. You do not know them; We know them, and We will punish them twice, then they shall be consigned to a great punishment. (101)

Key Points

  • «مَرَدوا» means practicing and training for an action until it creates a characteristic (or habit). The two punishments, «سنعذّبهم مرّتین» (We will punish them twice), are: one, disgrace among the people, and two, dying a difficult death. As mentioned in Ayah 50 of Surah Al-Anfal: «یَضربون وجوههم و اَدبارهم» (they strike their faces and their backs), the angels of death slap their faces and backs. And perhaps the intent of the two punishments is spiritual torment and physical torment.

Messages

1- We must be watchful of the hypocrites who are near and around us. «حولكم»

2- The disbelief and hypocrisy of the desert dwellers is severe but open, but the hypocrisy of the city dwellers is more mysterious (or secretive). «من اهل المدینة مردوا على النّفاق لا تعلمهم»

3- Hypocrisy has stages; sometimes it is superficial and sometimes it is deep-rooted. «مردوا على النفاق...»

4- The Prophet does not know the unseen without divine grace and teaching. «لاتعلمهم»

5- Those who insist on deviation and become accustomed to it will have a greater punishment. «مردوا على النفاق، سنعذّبهم مرّتین»

6- The hypocrite is afflicted in this world and the Hereafter, and his affliction is not far off. «سنعذّبهم مرّتین ثمّ یردون الى عذاب عظیم»

Ayah

وَءَاخَرُونَ ٱعْتَرَفُوا۟ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ خَلَطُوا۟ عَمَلًا صَـٰلِحًا وَءَاخَرَ سَيِّئًا عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٠۲)
[There are] others who have confessed to their sins, having mixed up righteous conduct with other that was evil. Maybe Allah will accept their repentance. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (102)

Key Points

A few companions of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله failed to participate in the Battle of Tabuk, not out of hypocrisy, but due to their attachment to worldly life. When the critical verses were revealed, they regretted their action and, as a sign of repentance, tied themselves to a pillar in the mosque. Until Allah accepted their repentance, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله untied the ropes, and they were forgiven.

Messages

1- Not all companions of the Prophet were just; some committed evil deeds. «خلطوا عملاً صالحاً و آخر سیّئاً»

2- A person should not only see their strengths among their deeds; rather, they must seek to compensate for their weaknesses and errors. «اعترفوا... خلطوا»

3- Confession of sin, self-criticism, and having good and righteous deeds pave the way for Divine forgiveness. «اعترفوا، یتوب علیهم»

4- Although Allah has promised forgiveness, a person must remain between fear and hope. «عسى اللَّه ان یتوب علیهم»

5- The remorseful wrongdoer is in need of hope, and the embrace of Islam is open to accept him. «اعترفوا... انّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

خُذْ مِنْ أَمْوَٰلِهِمْ صَدَقَةً تُطَهِّرُهُمْ وَتُزَكِّيهِم بِهَا وَصَلِّ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ إِنَّ صَلَوٰتَكَ سَكَنٌ لَّهُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱٠۳)
Take charity from their possessions to cleanse them and purify them thereby, and bless them. Indeed your blessing is a comfort to them, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (103)

Key Points

This Ayah was revealed in Medina during the month of Ramadan in the second year of the Hijra. The Prophet said: Let it be announced that Allah has made Zakat obligatory just like Salat (prayer). After one year, He also commanded the Muslims to pay their Zakat. The Prophet sends blessings upon those who pay Zakat: «صلّ علیهم», but Allah sends blessings upon the Mujahidin (strugglers) who are covered in blood and dust and resist (the enemy): «اولئك علیهم صلوات من ربّهم» (149)

(149) Al-Baqarah, 157.

Messages

1- One of the duties of the Islamic ruler is to collect Zakat from the people. «خذ»

2- Islam accepts private ownership. «اموالهم»

3- Zakat is a small portion of your wealth; pay it with encouragement. «من اموالهم»

4- Paying Zakat is a sign of a person's sincerity in claiming faith. «صدقة»

5- Zakat is a factor in purifying the soul from stinginess, worldliness, and love of wealth. «تطهّرهم»

6- Zakat leads to the growth of moral virtues in the individual and society. «تزكیّهم»

7- Poverty is the cause of many corruptions, and providing for the needy through Zakat purifies society from some of these corruptions. «تطهرّهم»

8- The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله sends blessings and salutations upon ordinary people. This is because valuing people encourages them towards good deeds and grants them dignity. «صلّ علیهم»

9- The relationship between the taxpayer and the tax collector must be emotional and spiritual. «خذ، صلّ»

10- The prayer of the Prophet on behalf of the people is answered. «صلّ علیهم انّ صلوتك سكن لهم»

11- Let us not say that paying Zakat was merely their duty; we should also express gratitude. «خذ، صلّ علیهم»

12- Encouragement does not always have to be material; sometimes spiritual encouragement is more effective. «انّ صلوتك سكن لهم»

13- Encouragement lightens the burden of the obligation. «سكن لهم»

14- The source of commands and obligations is Divine knowledge. «واللّه سمیع علیم»

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ يَقْبَلُ ٱلتَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَأْخُذُ ٱلصَّدَقَـٰتِ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٠٤)
Do they not know that it is Allah who accepts the repentance of His servants and receives the charities, and that it is Allah who is the All-clement, the All-merciful? (104)

Key Points

Some of those who did not participate in the Battle of Tabuk came to the Prophet and requested that He accept their repentance (tawbah). This Ayah states: The Acceptor of repentance is Allah, not anyone else, and the charities (sadaqat) and Zakat that are given to the Prophet and the Imam, their Receiver is, in reality, Allah. Since the Prophet receives Zakat by the command of Allah, Allah is, in reality, the Receiver of Zakat. This is similar to the Ayah concerning the pledge of allegiance (Bay'ah): «انّ الذین یبایعونك انّما یبایعون اللَّه» [150] Whoever pledges allegiance to you, O Prophet, has, as it were, pledged allegiance to Allah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Charity (sadaqah) does not settle in the hand of the poor person unless it settles in the Hand of Allah. He then recited this Ayah. [151] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked whether the collection of Zakat was exclusive to the Prophet or if it continued after him. He replied: Yes, it continues. [152]

150) Al-Fath, 10. 151) Tafsir Burhan. 152) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Even the Prophet of Allah does not have the right to accept repentance, let alone priests and church authorities. This status belongs exclusively to Allah. «هو یقبل التوبة»

2- Paying Zakat is a prerequisite for genuine repentance. «یقبل التوبة... ویأخذالصدقات»

3- Inner transformation precedes material aid. «یقبل التوبة...یأخذالصدقات»

4- Repentance is not merely regret; it must be followed by reform and action. «یقبل التوبه، یأخذ الصدقات»

5- Since the Receiver of charities is Allah, we should give with encouragement and pay the best quality goods in the best manner. «انّ اللّه... یأخذ الصدقات»

6- Zakat is an Islamic tax that possesses sanctity, because its Receiver is Allah. «یأخذ الصدقات»

Ayah

وَقُلِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ فَسَيَرَى ٱللَّهُ عَمَلَكُمْ وَرَسُولُهُۥ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ۖ وَسَتُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَـٰلِمِ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٠٥)
And say, ‘Go on working: Allah will see your conduct, and His Apostle and the faithful [as well], and you will be returned to the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, and He will inform you concerning what you used to do. (105)

Key Points

This Ayah, which expresses the awareness of Allah, the Prophet, and the believers regarding our actions, articulates the same Shi'a belief concerning the "presentation of deeds" (عرضه ى اعمال) to the Awliya' (saints/friends) of Allah. This presentation occurs daily, weekly, or monthly. If our deeds are good, the Awliya' of Allah rejoice over us; and if they are bad, they become worried and saddened. Belief in this presentation of deeds is effective in fostering piety (taqwa) and modesty (haya). Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: O people! Do not distress the Messenger of Allah with your sins. [153] According to narrations (riwayat), the intent behind "the believers" (المؤمنون) are the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), whom Allah informs of our deeds. [154]

153) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 154) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq.

Messages

1- Man is free in action, not compelled. «اعملوا»

2- Attention to the fact that our deeds are under the supervision of Allah prevents sin. «سيرى اللَّه عملكم»

3- The greater the number of observers of deeds, the greater a person's shame and modesty regarding wrongdoing, especially if the observers are Allah, the Prophet, and the believers. «سيرى اللَّه، و رسوله، والمؤمنون»

4- Action is the criterion for evaluation. What is presented to the Awliya' of Allah in this world is action, and what is accounted for on the Day of Judgment is also action. «اعملوا، فسيرى اللّه عملكم، فینبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون»

5- Human deeds possess realities that will become clear to him on the Day of Judgment. «فسيرى اللّه عملكم، فینبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون»

Ayah

وَءَاخَرُونَ مُرْجَوْنَ لِأَمْرِ ٱللَّهِ إِمَّا يُعَذِّبُهُمْ وَإِمَّا يَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱٠٦)
[There are] others waiting Allah’s edict: either He shall punish them, or turn to them clemently, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (106)

Key Points

Murjaun (مُرجَون) is derived from Irja' (ارجاء), meaning delay and suspension, a delay accompanied by hope (raja'). Allah has mentioned three groups in these verses: A: Mysterious hypocrites who are in Medina and have not been identified. (Ayah 101) B: Deviants who confessed their sin and hope for repentance. (Ayah 102) C: Deviants who do not insist on sin and have not repented; their fate rests with Allah. (The Ayah under discussion) According to narrations, [155] the Ayah refers to the killers of Hadrat Hamzah or Ja'far al-Tayyar, who regretted their actions and embraced Islam, but faith had not yet permeated their hearts such that they would certainly be among the people of Paradise. Alternatively, it relates to those who did not participate in the Battle of Tabuk and only regretted it but did not confess verbally.

155) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 407.

Messages

1- Allah's Hand is open regarding the forgiveness or punishment of sinners. «امّا یعذّبهم وامّا یتوب علیهم»

2- In educating different wrongdoers, different approaches must be taken. One group must be placed between fear and hope. «امّا یعذّبهم و امّا یتوب علیهم»

3- Divine wrath or grace is based on knowledge and wisdom, not vengeance! «علیم حكیم»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مَسْجِدًا ضِرَارًا وَكُفْرًا وَتَفْرِيقًۢا بَيْنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَإِرْصَادًا لِّمَنْ حَارَبَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ وَلَيَحْلِفُنَّ إِنْ أَرَدْنَآ إِلَّا ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱٠۷)
As for those who took to a mosque for sabotage and for defiance, and to cause division among the faithful, and for the purpose of ambush [used] by those who have fought Allah and His Apostle before—they will surely swear, ‘We desired nothing but good,’ and Allah bears witness that they are indeed liars. (107)

Key Points

The Ayah refers to the story of Masjid al-Dirar (the Mosque of Harm), which the hypocrites built opposite the Mosque of Quba, under the pretext of accommodating the weak, the sick, or those affected by rainy days. In reality, it was their base for gathering. They asked the Prophet, on the verge of his departure for the Battle of Tabuk, to pray there and inaugurate it. After the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله returned from Tabuk, the Ayah was revealed, exposing their wicked intention. The Prophet commanded that the mosque be burned, destroyed, and its location turned into a garbage dump. [156]

According to some narrations, the construction of Masjid al-Dirar in Medina was ordered by Abu Amir. He was the father of Hanzalah Ghaseel al-Mala'ikah (the one washed by the angels), a Christian ascetic who had influence among the Khazraj tribe. With the Prophet's صلى الله عليه وآله migration and the spread and influence of Islam, he began opposing the Prophet and played a hypocritical role in the Battle of Uhud. Eventually, he fled to Mecca and from there went to Rome, seeking help from the Roman Emperor to overthrow Islam. Astonishingly, he was the leader of the hypocrites, while his son Hanzalah was devoted to Islam and the Prophet and died as a martyr! [157]

When inviting the Prophet to pray in Masjid al-Dirar, they raised three issues: love for congregational prayer, love for the weak, and love for the leader and his prayer. All three were hypocritical. Hypocrites have always been like this; against Moses, Samiri plots; against the Prophet's Mosque, they build the Umayyad Mosque; and against Ali عليه السلام, they raise the Quran on spears. Just as the calf of Samiri was burned, the mosque of the hypocrites was also burned, serving as a lesson for history. In Kufa and Sham (Syria), mosques were also built to celebrate Yazid's victory over Imam Husayn عليه السلام, which our Imams called the accursed mosques!

Any kind of harm (Dirar) is forbidden in Islam, "لاضرر و لاضرار فى الأسلام" [158] including: A: Harm to life. "لایرید بكم العسر" [159] B: Harm to people. "لاتعاونوا على الاثم والعدوان" [160] C: Harm to the wife. "لاتضاروهنّ لتضیّقوا علیهن" [161] D: Harm to the child. "لاتضارّ والدة بولدها" [162] E: Harm to the heirs. "من بعد وصیّةٍ یوصى بها او دینٍ غیرمضارّ" [163] F: Harmful teachings. "یتعلّمون مایضرهم ولاینفعهم" [164] G: Harm in transactions and debts. "ولایضارّ كاتب ولاشهید" [165] H: Harm to the faith/school of thought and unity. "اتّخذوا مسجداً ضراراً"

156) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan, Al-Manar, and Nur al-Thaqalayn. 157) Tafsir-e Nemuneh. 158) Wasa'il, Vol. 26, p. 14. 159) Al-Baqarah, 185. 160) Al-Ma'idah, 2. 161) Al-Talaq, 6. 162) Al-Baqarah, 233. 163) An-Nisa, 12. 164) Al-Baqarah, 102. 165) Al-Baqarah, 282.

Messages

  1. The enemy exploits the mosque and religion against religion itself. Let us not be deceived by the appearance of slogans and titles! "اتّخذوا مسجداً ضراراً"
  2. The plotters sought to exploit even the Prophet's prayer for their own benefit. (Considering the occasion of revelation)
  3. Building a mosque is not the main issue; the motives of the founders and custodians must be pure. "اتّخذوا مسجداً ضراراً و..."
  4. In Islam, any kind of harm is forbidden, even if it is under the title of a mosque. "اتّخذوا مسجداً ضراراً"
  5. The respect and sanctity of the mosque are removed when it is exploited by enemies. (Considering the occasion of revelation)
  6. Allah supports His Prophet and His religion and exposes the plots of the hypocrites through revelation. "و الذین اتّخذوا مسجداً ضراراً..."
  7. Any mosque that causes division among Muslims is a Mosque of Harm (Masjid al-Dirar). "اتّخذوا مسجداً... تفریقاً بین المؤمنین"
  8. Hypocrites serve the disbelievers. "ارصاداً لمن حارب اللّه"
  9. During war with external enemies, we must not neglect the plots of internal enemies. The hypocrites asked the Prophet to officially inaugurate their base on the eve of the Battle of Tabuk, but the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله left Ali عليه السلام in his place in Medina, and upon his return, he destroyed Masjid al-Dirar.
  10. Breaking the unity of Muslims is equivalent to disbelief (Kufr). "كفراً و تفریقاً بین المؤمنین"
  11. False swearing is the method of the hypocrites. "لیحلفنّ"
  12. Let us not be deceived by the enemy's self-righteous claims and propaganda. "انْ اردنا الاّ الحسنى"
  13. Lying is a characteristic of disbelievers and hypocrites. "انّهم لكاذبون"

Ayah

لَا تَقُمْ فِيهِ أَبَدًا ۚ لَّمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ أَحَقُّ أَن تَقُومَ فِيهِ ۚ فِيهِ رِجَالٌ يُحِبُّونَ أَن يَتَطَهَّرُوا۟ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُطَّهِّرِينَ (۱٠۸)
Do not stand in it ever! A mosque founded on Godwariness from the [very] first day is worthier that you stand in it [for prayer]. Therein are men who love to keep pure, and Allah loves those who keep pure. (108)

Key Points

The mosque that was founded upon piety from its very beginning is the Mosque of "Quba," which was built during the migration (Hijra) to Medina. [166] Fakhr Razi says: If a history of piety is the source of value for a mosque, then a person's precedence in faith and piety is of greater value, and Ali عليه السلام, who was a believer from the very first day, is superior to those who became Muslims after years of polytheism (shirk). [167]

166) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 167) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- We should not stand for prayer in a mosque that serves as a base for opponents of the Islamic system. «لاتقم فیه ابداً»

2- Worship is not separate from politics. Even through prayer, one must not strengthen falsehood. «لا تقم فیه ابداً» (Supporting disbelief (kufr), hypocrisy, and division is forbidden (haram).)

3- The leader is an example for others. The Prophet should not enter the Mosque of Dissent (Masjid al-Dirar) so that others also refrain from entering. Therefore, the address of the Ayah is directed to the Prophet. «لا تقم فیه»

4- The leader of the community must be proactive in taking a stance. «لا تقم فیه»

5- If we prevent people from going to centers of corruption, we must provide healthy and beneficial places as alternatives and guide them toward those. «لاتقم، تقوم»

6- The value of everything depends on the goals, motivations, and intentions of its founders. «اُسس على التقوى»

7- The mosque is a setting for purification from physical and spiritual impurities. «فیه رجال یحبّون ان یتطهرّوا»

8- Association with the righteous is valuable. «فیه رجال...»

9- The value of places depends on the individuals who frequent them. The value of a mosque lies in its worshippers, not its dome and minarets. «لمسجدٌ ... احقّ ان تقوم، فیه رجال یحبون ان یتطهروا»

10- Prayer and the mosque are means of refinement (tahdhib) and purity. «مسجد فیه... یتطهّروا»

11- The desire for perfection and purity is itself a perfection. «یحبّون ان یتطهّروا»

12- The pure are beloved by Allah. «یحب المتطهّرین»

Ayah

أَفَمَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيَـٰنَهُۥ عَلَىٰ تَقْوَىٰ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِضْوَٰنٍ خَيْرٌ أَم مَّنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيَـٰنَهُۥ عَلَىٰ شَفَا جُرُفٍ هَارٍ فَٱنْهَارَ بِهِۦ فِى نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٠۹)
Is he who founds his building on Godwariness and [the pursuit of Allah’s] pleasure better-off or he who founds his building on the brink of a collapsing bank which collapses with him into the fire of hell? Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (109)

Key Points

The previous Ayah compared the Mosque of Dirar (Harm) and the Mosque of Quba, and this Ayah compares the founders of those two mosques. The word "شَفا" (shafā) means edge or side. "جُرُف" (juruf) means the bank of a river or well where the water has eroded the base, creating a precipice (or cliff). And "هار" (hār) means weak or crumbling.

Messages

1- The value of deeds lies in their intention, not their outward appearance. The Mosque of Quba was built for Allah, while the Mosque of Dirar was built with the motive of causing division! «اسس بنیانه على التقوى»

2- The foundation of falsehood is destined to collapse. «بنیانه على شفا جرفٍ هار»

3- Sometimes a mosque casts its own founders into the depths of Hell. «فانهار به فى نارِ جهنّم»

4- Misusing religious centers and the religious beliefs of the people is oppression (or injustice). «الظالمین»

Ayah

لَا يَزَالُ بُنْيَـٰنُهُمُ ٱلَّذِى بَنَوْا۟ رِيبَةً فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ إِلَّآ أَن تَقَطَّعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۱۱٠)
The building they have built will never cease to be [a source of] disquiet in their hearts until their hearts are cut into pieces, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (110)

Messages

1- The foundations of the hypocrites' beliefs are weak and accompanied by doubt. «بنوا ریبةً»

2- Heart and spiritual diseases sometimes turn into a stable and unchanging characteristic. «لایزال... ریبة فى قلوبهم»

3- Heart and spiritual diseases are not cured by apparent and verbal repentance. «الاّ ان تقطّع قلوبهم»

4- Even if the structure of hypocrisy is uprooted, the effects of hypocrisy that have taken root in the hearts remain until the death of the hypocrite arrives. «الا ان تقطّع قلوبهم»

5- Hypocrites possess organizations and bases. «بنیانهم»

6- The hypocrite lacks peace. «ریبةً»

7- Allah is aware of the hypocrites' motive in building the Mosque of Harm (Masjid al-Dirar). «علیم»

Ayah
