۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱشْتَرَىٰ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَٰلَهُم بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةَ ۚ يُقَـٰتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ ۖ وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْءَانِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَٱسْتَبْشِرُوا۟ بِبَيْعِكُمُ ٱلَّذِى بَايَعْتُم بِهِۦ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۱۱۱)
Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful their souls and their possessions for paradise to be theirs: they fight in the way of Allah, kill, and are killed. A promise binding upon Him in the Torah and the Evangel and the Quran. And who is truer to his promise than Allah? So rejoice in the bargain you have made with Him, and that is the great success. (111)

Key Points

The world is a marketplace, and people are the traders within it. The sellers are the people, and the Buyer is Allah. The commodity of the transaction is wealth and life (soul), and the price of the transaction is Paradise. If they sell to Allah, it is entirely profit; if they sell to others, it is complete loss.

Trading with Allah has several advantages:

1- We ourselves, our abilities, and our possessions are from Him, so it is not appropriate that we give them to anyone other than Him.

2- Allah buys even the smallest amount. «مثقال ذرّة خیراً یره»

3- He rectifies the defects of the commodity and does not expose (the seller).

4- He buys at the price of Paradise. Allah enters the bidding process when buying and offers a rate such that selling to anyone other than Him is foolish and a loss.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: There is no price for your bodies except Paradise, so do not sell yourselves for less than Paradise. [168]

168) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- When we have a Buyer like Allah, why should we turn to others? «انّ اللَّه اشترى»

2- Allah buys only from the believer, not from the hypocrite or the disbeliever. «من المؤمنین»

3- In divine acceptance, striving with one's life (Jihad al-Nafs) precedes striving with one's wealth (Jihad al-Mal). «انفسهم واموالهم» (Everyone possesses life, but not everyone possesses wealth and riches.)

4- Paradise is attained through Jihad, the sword, and the offering of life and wealth to Allah. «لهم الجنّة یقاتلون فى سبیل اللَّه»

5- If the goal of Jihad is Allah, then being killed or not being killed makes no difference. «یقاتلون فى سبیل اللَّه فیَقتلون و یُقتلون»

6- The believer is always ready to go to the front line. «یقاتلون» (The imperfect tense [present/future] indicates continuity and persistence.)

7- The believer's goal in fighting is first to destroy falsehood and its adherents, and ultimately, martyrdom. «یَقتلون و یُقتلون»

8- In trading with Allah, the profit is certain and definite. «وعداً علیه حقّاً» In the bidding with Allah, everything is truth and to the benefit of man, unlike others whose intentions are either not serious, or are unmotivated and false, or are exaggerated, or are based on whim, or are meant to repel a rival.

9- The value of Jihad, the Mujahidin (striving ones), and the martyrs is not exclusive to Islam; it is also mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel. If it is not found in those books today, it indicates their distortion. «فى التورات والأنجیل»

10- Although we have no right over Allah, He has placed rights upon Himself for our sake. «وعداً علیه حقّاً»

11- The transaction is a binding contract (Aqd Lazim) whose fulfillment is obligatory. «وعداً، حقّاً، اوفى »

12- Allah has made the sincere Mujahidin the party to His transaction. «بایعتم» (You have traded/contracted) is used, and He did not say «بعتم» (You have sold).

13- The best glad tidings are the exchange of the perishable for the everlasting, and the world for the Hereafter; anything else is loss and regret. «فاستبشروا»

14- Alongside criticizing the hypocrites who evade Jihad, the striving believers must be appreciated and thanked. «فاستبشروا»

15- The greatest success is only achieved through trading with Allah. «ذلك هو الفوزالعظیم»

Ayah

ٱلتَّـٰٓئِبُونَ ٱلْعَـٰبِدُونَ ٱلْحَـٰمِدُونَ ٱلسَّـٰٓئِحُونَ ٱلرَّٰكِعُونَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدُونَ ٱلْـَٔامِرُونَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَٱلنَّاهُونَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ وَٱلْحَـٰفِظُونَ لِحُدُودِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۱۲)
[The faithful are] penitent, devout, celebrators of Allah’s praise, wayfarers, who bow [and] prostrate [in prayer], bid what is right and forbid what is wrong, and keep Allah’s bounds—and give good news to the faithful. (112)

Key Points

In this Ayah, nine characteristics are mentioned for the striving believers (mujahideen): they repent from sins, and they enter the sphere of worship. With their tongue, they offer praise (hamd); with their feet, they move; and with their bodies, they perform bowing (rukūʿ) and prostration (sujūd). After self-purification, they engage in reforming society through enjoining good (amr bi al-maʿrūf) and do not transgress the boundaries of divine laws. Repentance from sin opens the path to worship; a praising tongue, coupled with dynamism and movement, brings readiness for bowing and prostration before Allah; and enjoining good and forbidding evil (nahy ʿan al-munkar) safeguards the divine boundaries in society. The word "التائبون" (The Repentant) indicates that the believers who sell their lives and wealth to Allah can enter this field of transaction with God through an internal revolution, self-reconstruction, and turning away from past wrongful deeds. [169] ʿAbbād al-Baṣrī saw Imam Sajjad عليه السلام on the road to Mecca and said to the Imam: "تركتَ الجهادَ و صعوبَته واَقبلتَ الى الحجِّ و لِینَه؟" (Have you abandoned Jihād and its difficulty and turned toward Hajj and its ease?) He then recited the Ayah "ان اللَّه اشترى..." (Indeed, Allah has purchased...). The Imam said: Recite the rest of it as well! ʿAbbād recited the Ayah "التائبون...". The Imam said: If our companions possessed these characteristics and perfections, it would be obligatory upon us to rise up, and in that case, Jihād would be superior to Hajj. [170] It is mentioned in a narration that the meaning of "السائحون" (The Travelers/Wanderers) is those who fast. [171]

169) Just as in the event of Karbala, individuals like Zuhayr and Ḥurr, despite not having brilliant past records, surrendered their lives and shone brightly through a turning point and a transaction with Allah. 170) Wasā'il, Vol. 15, p. 46. 171) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. The condition for Allah's faithfulness to His covenant is the steadfastness of the believers in preserving the divine boundaries. "و من اوفى بعهده... التائبون..."

  2. The Greater Jihād (self-purification) is a prerequisite for the Lesser Jihād (fighting) on the fronts. "یقاتلون... التائبون..."

  3. Islam is a comprehensive religion; it places tears alongside the sword, and worship alongside traveling/striving. "یقاتلون... التائبون العابدون... السائحون"

  4. What is desired is that perfections become ingrained habits (malakah) in man. "التائبون العابدون..." (All the attributes are presented in the form of the active participle [ism fāʿil], not the verb, to indicate continuity.)

  5. Strengthening the morale of the Islamic fighters is achieved through the remembrance of Allah and worship, not through wine, music, and the like, which are found on the enemy's front. "یقاتلون، التّائبون العابدون،..."

  6. The condition for the acceptance of worship is repentance from sin. "التائبون العابدون"

  7. Movement, effort, striving (siyāḥah), migration, and self-purification are valuable in the path of attaining perfection and proximity to Allah. "السائحون" (Men of God are neither stagnant nor immobilized.)

  8. The soldier and fighter of Islam must be devoted to prayer and worship. "یقاتلون، الراكعون الساجدون"

  9. In the path of perfection, self-purification comes first, followed by societal reform. "التائبون العابدون ... الامرون بالمعروف ..."

  10. Engagement with the enemy at the borders should not distract us from internal corruption and evils. "یقاتلون، الآمرون بالمعروف ..."

  11. To preserve the divine boundaries, one must both fight the external enemy and combat internal corruption. "یقاتلون، الناهون عن المنكر، الحافظون لحدود اللَّه"

  12. Fighters are more responsible than others for preventing social corruption. "یقاتلون، الناهون عن المنكر"

Ayah

مَا كَانَ لِلنَّبِىِّ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَن يَسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ لِلْمُشْرِكِينَ وَلَوْ كَانُوٓا۟ أُو۟لِى قُرْبَىٰ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱۱۳)
The Prophet and the faithful may not plead for the forgiveness of the polytheists, even if they should be [their] relatives, after it has become clear to them that they will be the inmates of hell. (113)

Key Points

Some commentators have relied upon some fabricated narrations from unreliable narrators and considered this Ayah to be about Abu Talib! While we have dozens of proofs and narrations regarding the faith of Abu Talib. Sa'id ibn Musayyab, who is the narrator of this tradition, is an enemy of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام, and the only fault of Abu Talib is that he is the father of Ali علیه السلام!

Messages

1- The Prophet and other Muslims are equal before the law. «ما كان للنّبى و الذین امنوا»

2- Polytheism (Shirk) is an unforgivable sin, and even the Prophet's seeking of forgiveness for the polytheists is ineffective. «ماكان... ان یستغفروا للمشركین»

3- Ideological ties are more important than emotional ties. Familial affections must not override the ideology. «ما كان... ولو كانوا اولى قربى»

4- Kinship with the Prophet does not prevent Hellfire. «ولو كانوا اولى قربى»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ ٱسْتِغْفَارُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ إِلَّا عَن مَّوْعِدَةٍ وَعَدَهَآ إِيَّاهُ فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُۥٓ أَنَّهُۥ عَدُوٌّ لِّلَّهِ تَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُ ۚ إِنَّ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ لَأَوَّٰهٌ حَلِيمٌ (۱۱٤)
Abraham’s pleading forgiveness for his father was only to fulfill a promise he had made him. So when it became manifest to him that he was an enemy of God, he repudiated him. Indeed Abraham was most plaintive and forbearing. (114)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was that the Prophet and the believers do not have the right to pray for polytheists, even if they are their relatives. This Ayah answers a doubt: why did Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) pray for his polytheist uncle and promise him forgiveness (seeking forgiveness)? «سأستغفر لك ربّى» (I shall ask my Lord for forgiveness for you) [172]. This Ayah states that Ibrahim’s promise was in the hope of his guidance. But since the uncle died while in polytheism (shirk), Ibrahim abandoned seeking forgiveness.

Question: Why did Prophet Ibrahim pray for him even after his uncle’s death? «ربّنا اغفر لى ولوالدىّ» (Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents) [173]?

Answer: Wālid (والد) refers to the biological father, but Ab (أب) is also used for father, teacher, uncle, father-in-law, and grandfather. Ibrahim’s prayer was for his biological father, not his polytheist uncle. The Quran uses the term «أب» (Ab) for Ibrahim’s uncle in eleven Ayahs, to show under whose guardianship Ibrahim عليه السلام was, but that he was not influenced (by him).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Ibrahim عليه السلام was a man of much prayer and weeping, and was an «اوّاه» (oft-returning/sighing). [174]

172) Maryam, 47. 173) Ibrahim, 41. 174) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Whatever causes suspicion toward the friends of Allah (Awliyā’ Allāh) must be removed, and a correct justification must be provided. «موعدةٍ وعدها» (a promise which he had promised him)

2- Fulfilling a promise is necessary, even toward a disbeliever. «موعدة وعدها»

3- The knowledge of the Prophets is limited. «فلمّا تبیّن له...» (But when it became clear to him...)

4- We should not lose our forbearance in the face of the harsh behavior of disbelievers. [175] «تبرأ منه ...اوّاه حلیم» (he disassociated himself from him... oft-returning, forbearing)

5- Disassociating from the uncle for the sake of Allah is a sign of Ibrahim’s love and humility toward God. «اوّاه»

175) Ibrahim’s uncle threatened him, saying, "I will surely stone you." «لئن لم تنته لأرجمّنك» (Maryam, 46). But Ibrahim remained patient.

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُضِلَّ قَوْمًۢا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَىٰهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُبَيِّنَ لَهُم مَّا يَتَّقُونَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۱۱٥)
Allah does not lead any people astray after He has guided them until He has made clear to them what they should beware of. Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. (115)

Key Points

The Ayah either describes the condition of those who had become Muslims but passed away before becoming aware of their duties (176), or it refers to the previous Ayah and the necessity of disassociating oneself from the enemies of Allah, implying that if you remain indifferent after this warning, you yourselves will be misguided and join the enemies of Allah. It is also possible that the Ayah is meant to relieve the anxiety of those who sought forgiveness (Istighfar) for the polytheists before knowing that it was forbidden. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Allah has made it obligatory upon the Imam that whenever He becomes concerned about His own death, He must introduce the next infallible Imam to the people, and then He recited this Ayah (177).

176) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 177) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- If we do not observe the divine limits, the danger of misguidance lies ahead. «لیضلّ قوماً... حتى یُبیّن لهم» 2- Allah's misguidance (Idlal) is the punishment for ignoring His guidance. Conscious opposition to divine directives is the basis for Allah withdrawing His guidance. «ماكان اللَّه لیضلّ ... حتّى یُبیّن» 3- Divine punishment occurs after clarification, enlightenment, and the completion of the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah). «حتى یُبیّن» 4- Divine guidance is complete; in addition to explaining the means of felicity, it also warns about factors of danger. «یُبیّن لهم ما یتّقون» Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah makes known to the people the means of His satisfaction and His wrath (178). 5- The divine command to abstain from something originates from His knowledge and wisdom. «إنَّ اللَّه بكلِّ شى ءٍ علیم»

178) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 163.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ ۚ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۱۱٦)
Indeed to Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and brings death. And besides Allah you do not have any guardian or helper. (116)

Messages

1- Bringing to life and causing to die, the life and death of hearts (guidance and misguidance), are in the hand of Allah. «یحیى و یمیت»

2- Instead of strengthening relationships with polytheist relatives, be mindful of the relationship with a God Who is the Owner of the heavens and the earth, and they have no power to offer any kind of help against this God. «له ملك السموات... مالكم من دون اللَّه من ولى...»

Ayah

لَّقَد تَّابَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ وَٱلْمُهَـٰجِرِينَ وَٱلْأَنصَارِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوهُ فِى سَاعَةِ ٱلْعُسْرَةِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا كَادَ يَزِيغُ قُلُوبُ فَرِيقٍ مِّنْهُمْ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِهِمْ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۱۷)
Certainly Allah turned clemently to the Prophet and the Emigrants and the Helpers, who followed him in the hour of difficulty, after the hearts of a part of them were about to swerve. Then He turned clemently to them—indeed, He is most kind and merciful to them (117)

Key Points

The Battle of Tabuk involved many difficulties, including: the long distance, the heat of the weather, the season for harvesting fruits and crops, and the awe and power of the enemy. Therefore, some people who were not hypocrites preferred the shade of their homes, cool water, and their wives over the front lines and the hardships of the journey, and they did not go. An example is Abu Khaythama, who, upon realizing his mistake, made an effort and, after a delay of a few days, reached the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Muslims. The hypocrites in the Battle of Tabuk were in three groups: 1- A group did not go to the front line under the pretext of being tempted by the beautiful Roman girls. 2- A group discouraged people from going. 3- Some used the heat of the weather as an excuse, or sought permission to stay behind. But in contrast, a group went lovingly alongside the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and if they lacked the means for the journey, they wept out of sorrow. The story of Abu Dharr al-Ghifari being left behind, who eventually reached the Prophet, relates to this very incident. In the Battle of Tabuk, the situation became so severe that sometimes several people would suck on a single date [179]. Perhaps because of this Ayah and the following Ayah, which discuss the acceptance of repentance (Tawbah), this Surah has been named Tawbah (Repentance) in addition to Bara'ah (Immunity).

179) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Allah grants His special favor to the Prophet and the fighters in dangerous situations. «تاب اللَّه ...»

2- All human beings, even the Prophets, are in need of Divine favor. «تاب اللَّه على النبى و المهاجرین والأنصار»

3- Divine repentance (Tawbah) means His special favor upon the Prophet and the believers, which sometimes manifests in the form of pardon and forgiveness toward sinners. «تاب اللَّه»

4- The sign of true faith is obedience to the leader during difficult circumstances. «اتبعوه فى ساعة العسرة»

5- The hardships of life are nothing more than a moment or an hour, and they pass. «ساعة العسرة»

6- Man is exposed to error and deviation at every moment, so he must turn to Allah. «كاد یزیغ قلوب فریقٍ»

7- The only way to be saved from downfall is Divine favor. «تاب اللَّه...ثم تاب علیهم»

Ayah

وَعَلَى ٱلثَّلَـٰثَةِ ٱلَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّآ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱۱۸)
—and to the three who were left behind. When the earth became narrow for them with [all] its expanse, and their own souls weighed heavily on them, and they knew that there was no refuge from Allah except in Him, then He turned clemently toward them so that they might be penitent. Indeed Allah is the All-clement, the All-merciful. (118)

Key Points

The three Muslims who did not participate in Tabuk, [180] regretted their action and came to the Prophet to apologize. However, the Prophet did not speak to them and ordered that no one should speak to them, nor should their wives approach them. They went to the mountains surrounding Medina and, separated from one another, engaged in supplication and weeping for forgiveness (Istighfar). After fifty days, Allah accepted their repentance. [181]

180) Ka'b ibn Malik, Murarah ibn Rabi', and Hilal ibn Umayyah. 181) Bihar, Vol. 21, p. 237.

Messages

1- Those who disregard war and the command of the leadership must be punished and temporarily left behind (ostracized). «خُلّفوا»

2- Estrangement, disregard, and boycott are among the educational methods for offenders and criminals. «ضاقت علیهم الارض... ظنّوا ان لا ملجأمن اللَّه»

3- Negative engagement (passive resistance) with offenders makes society an effective, unguarded prison for them. «ضاقت علیهم الأرض»

4- Although the Prophet is the manifestation of Divine Mercy, as an educator (Murabbi), He also uses the leverage of estrangement. «ضاقت علیهم انفسهم»

5- One of the punishments is the torment of conscience. «ضاقت علیهم انفسهم»

6- Despair of people paves the way for repentance and turning toward God. «لاملجأ ... ثم تابَ»

7- The success (Tawfiq) of repentance is also under the shade of Divine grace and favor. First, Allah returns His favor to the person, «تاب علیهم» (He turned toward them), so that the person finds the opportunity for regret, apology, and repentance, «لیتوبوا» (so that they may repent), and as soon as he repents, Allah again accepts his repentance. «انّ اللّه هو التوّاب»

8- After a period of negative engagement, disregard, and boycott, we must create suitable conditions for the rehabilitation of the offenders so that they may return, and thereafter, we should accept them kindly. «تاب علیهم لیتوبوا انّ اللّه هو التوّاب»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَكُونُوا۟ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۱۱۹)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah, and be with the Truthful. (119)

Key Points

It is stated in Shi'a and Sunni narrations that the intent of al-Sādiqīn (the truthful ones) is Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله and the Family of Muhammad علیهم السلام. Ayah 177 of Surah Al-Baqarah considers the people of faith, charity (infāq), prayer (salāt), fulfillment of covenant, and patience in the face of difficulties to be truthful (sādiq). And Ayah 15 of Surah Al-Hijr and Ayah 8 of Surah Al-Hashr consider the suffering and self-sacrificing emigrants (Muhājirūn) to be "truthful" (sādiq).

Messages

1- Friendship, companionship, and association with the truthful are factors in human development and prevention from deviation. «اتقوااللَّه و كونوا مع الصادقین»

2- We must not separate ourselves from the Divine leaders. «كونوا مع الصادقین»

3- The Divine leaders are infallible (ma'sūm), otherwise Allah would not have commanded us to be with them. (182) «كونوا مع الصادقین»

4- The perfection of society is achieved through faith, piety (taqwā), and obedience to the infallible leader. «آمنوا، اتقوا، مع الصادقین»

5- In every era, there must be an infallible one (ma'sūm) whom Muslims accompany. «كونوا مع الصادقین»

6- The status of truthfulness (sidq) is such that Allah has used al-Sādiqīn instead of al-Ma'sūmīn (the infallible ones), and has praised those who have passed the stages of faith and piety with this title. «آمنوا، اتّقوا، الصادقین»

(182) It is astonishing that Fakhr al-Rāzī says: The infallible one is not a specific individual, but rather the consensus (ijmā') of the Ummah is infallible from error!! Does the gathering of fallible and wicked individuals bring about infallibility?

Ayah

مَا كَانَ لِأَهْلِ ٱلْمَدِينَةِ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَعْرَابِ أَن يَتَخَلَّفُوا۟ عَن رَّسُولِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا يَرْغَبُوا۟ بِأَنفُسِهِمْ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ لَا يُصِيبُهُمْ ظَمَأٌ وَلَا نَصَبٌ وَلَا مَخْمَصَةٌ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا يَطَـُٔونَ مَوْطِئًا يَغِيظُ ٱلْكُفَّارَ وَلَا يَنَالُونَ مِنْ عَدُوٍّ نَّيْلًا إِلَّا كُتِبَ لَهُم بِهِۦ عَمَلٌ صَـٰلِحٌ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۲٠)
It is not fitting for the people of Madinah and the Bedouins around them to hang back behind the Apostle of Allah and prefer their own lives to his life. That is because they neither experience any thirst, nor fatigue, nor hunger, in the way of Allah, nor do they tread any ground enraging the faithless, nor do they gain any ground against an enemy but a righteous deed is written for them on its account. Indeed Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous. (120)

Key Points

The principles derived from this Ayah regarding warfare are: 1- Belief that all hardships of war are righteous deeds and lead to divine pleasure. 2- Protecting the commander. 3- Resistance and endurance against hardships. 4- Humiliating and angering the enemy.

Messages

1- The companions and associates of the leader, who possess greater knowledge and resources, bear greater responsibility. «ما كان لاهل المدینة... ان یتخلّفوا»

2- In defending Islam, the distinction between city and village is irrelevant. «اهل المدینة و من حولهم»

3- Unquestioning obedience to the Prophet is necessary, and no one has the right to violate the governmental command of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. «ما كان لاهل المدینة... ان یتخلّفوا»

4- One of the functions of prophethood (Risalah) is the command of all forces in war. «یتخلّفواعن رسول اللَّه»

5- Protecting the life of the Prophet is more essential than protecting the lives of the Muslims. Muslims must be willing to sacrifice themselves for the heavenly leader. «لایرغبوا...»

6- A Muslim must be ready to endure every hardship and pressure in the path of their belief. «ظمأ، نصب، مخمصة...»

7- The Islamic society must be anti-disbelief (anti-Kufr). «یغیظ الكفّار»

8- The marches and collective movements of Muslims that cause the disbelievers (Kuffar) to become angry and fearful are rewarded by Allah. «موطئاً یغیظ الكفّار»

9- Not only defense and combat, but all movements, efforts, and sufferings on the front are recorded by Allah and result in reward (Ajr). «لایصیبهم ظمأ ولانصب ولامخمصة... عمل صالح»

10- The preliminaries of Jihad and combat readiness are also righteous deeds and worship. «لا یطئون موطئاً... عمل صالح»

11- The truly virtuous person (Muhsin) is revolutionary and belongs to the front lines. «اجر المحسنین» (Self-sacrifice is a sign of perfect virtue.)

12- Receiving divine reward is achieved through enduring hardships. Without enduring hardship, treasure cannot be attained. «ظمأ، نصب، مخمصة، ...اجر المحسنین»

13- Belief in Allah's pleasure and reward eases difficulties. «ان اللّه لا یضیع اجر المحسنین»

Ayah

وَلَا يُنفِقُونَ نَفَقَةً صَغِيرَةً وَلَا كَبِيرَةً وَلَا يَقْطَعُونَ وَادِيًا إِلَّا كُتِبَ لَهُمْ لِيَجْزِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۲۱)
And neither do they incur any expense, big or small, nor do they cross any valley, but it is written to their account, so that Allah may reward them by the best of what they used to do. (121)

Key Points

The meaning of "احسن ما كانوا یعلمون" (the best of what they used to do) is either that the reward of Allah is better than the action of the mujahideen (striving ones), or that the best deed they perform is Jihad with wealth and life.

Messages

  1. In spending (in charity), the quantity and amount are not important; whether it is small or large, it has a reward with Allah. «صغیرةً و لاكبیرة»

  2. Let us not fear that a good deed will remain unrewarded, for all human actions are recorded. «لاینفقون، لایقطعون، الاّ كتب لهم»

  3. Moving towards the fronts (of battle/Jihad) is among the best deeds. «احسن ما كانوا یعملون»

  4. The Divine reward is better and higher than human action. «لیجزیهم اللَّه احسن...»

  5. Persistence in righteous deeds causes the increase of their reward. «ماكانوا یعملون»

  6. It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that He advances His school (of thought/religion) through the efforts of the believers. (If there was no need for their effort, all this encouragement in these two verses would not have been necessary.)

Ayah

۞ وَمَا كَانَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ لِيَنفِرُوا۟ كَآفَّةً ۚ فَلَوْلَا نَفَرَ مِن كُلِّ فِرْقَةٍ مِّنْهُمْ طَآئِفَةٌ لِّيَتَفَقَّهُوا۟ فِى ٱلدِّينِ وَلِيُنذِرُوا۟ قَوْمَهُمْ إِذَا رَجَعُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَحْذَرُونَ (۱۲۲)
Yet it is not for the faithful to go forth en masse. But why should not there a group from each of their sections go forth to become learned in religion, and to warn their people when they return to them, so that they may beware? (122)

Key Points

Following the revelation of verses that severely criticized those who lagged behind in war, and due to the Prophet's strictness concerning them, thereafter and in subsequent wars, all Muslims prepared for Jihad and left the Prophet alone in Medina. This verse served as a control mechanism for their mass departure to the front. [183] All Bedouin Muslims used to come to the city to learn the religion, and this caused migration to the city and its negative consequences. The verse was revealed stating that some should come to the city to become familiar with the religion, then return to their homeland and guide others. "Religion" (Dīn) is the collection of divine laws and the apparent and hidden regulations of Islam. Islam, which is the only religion accepted by Allah, «انّ الدین عنداللَّه الاسلام» [184] means submission to God. Tafaqquh (deep understanding) in religion means striving to gain profound knowledge of Islamic beliefs and rulings. Tafaqquh in religion may sometimes be for worldly gain, status, love of study and discussion, entertainment, or keeping up with friends; and sometimes it may be for the sake of religion, Allah, Paradise, and the salvation of the Ummah. The verse considers the second type valuable, which is warning the people and inviting them to the path of Allah. «لینذروا قومهم» Fiqh (jurisprudence) is so important that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, when sending off Ali عليه السلام to Yemen, commanded him to teach the people Fiqh, «فقّههم فى الدین» [185] and prayed for him thus: «الّلهم فقّهه فى الدین» [186], even though he was the most knowledgeable jurist among the people. Just as Imam Ali عليه السلام advised his son: «تَفقَّه فى الدّین فان الفقهاء ورثة الانبیاء» [187] Imam Husayn عليه السلام also praised Allah on the night of Ashura with these words: Praise be to God Who made us jurists (faqīh) in religion. [188] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked: If an incident were to befall the Imam, what is the duty of the people? The Imam recited this verse, stating that from every city and tribe, people must set out to recognize the Imam. [189]

183) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 184) Āl ‘Imrān, 19. 185) Mustadrak al-Wasā’il, Hadith 36369. 186) Bihār, Vol. 66, p. 92. 187) Bihār, Vol. 1, p. 216. 188) Mawsu‘at Kalimāt al-Imām. 189) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- In planning and management, we must be comprehensive so that attention to one issue does not cause disruption in other issues. «ما كان المؤمنون لینفروا كافّة»

2- Migration (Hijrah) is concomitant with faith. Migrate either to defend the religion or to gain knowledge of the religion. «فلولا نفر من كلّ فرقة»

3- Individuals must go from every region to academic centers to gain knowledge of Islam, so that there is a sufficient presence of religious scholars in all areas. «من كل فرقة طائفة»

4- A scholar who does not migrate is not a complete faqīh. «نَفَر» The Imam said concerning someone who stayed at home: «كیف یتفّقه فى الدین؟». [190]

5- Tafaqquh in religion and teaching religion to the people is Wajib Kifā’ī (a communal obligation). «فلولا نفر منهم طائفة» (Jihad and Ijtihad are both communal obligations. [191] Therefore, planning must be such that the academic system for students of religious sciences is not disrupted.)

6- The goal of learning Fiqh is to warn the people, make them aware, and save them from negligence and indifference toward problems. «لینذروا»

7- Even during wartime, intellectual, doctrinal, and moral issues must not be neglected, nor should the external enemy distract us from the internal enemy (ignorance and negligence). «ما كان المؤمنون لینفروا ... فلو لانفر...لیتفقّهوا»

8- Fiqh is not merely the learning of religious rulings, but all religious knowledge is subject to research, precision, and deep insight. «لیتفقّهوا فى الدّین»

9- Religious knowledge must be deep and profound. «لیتفقّهوا فى الدین»

10- The prerequisite for religious propagation is deep awareness of it. «لیتفقّهوا... لینذروا»

11- The statements of a knowledgeable Islamic jurist (faqīh) can be trusted. «لیتفقّهوا... لینذروا» (It is possible that there is only one person in the region, thus Khabar Wāhid [single report] has authority.)

12- Fiqh in religion is valuable only when the faqīh is aware of the times and knowledgeable about the enemy's deceptions and methods of infiltration, so that he can warn and caution. «لیتفقهوا ...لینذروا»

13- Two migrations are necessary for seminary students: one toward the seminaries, and the other from the seminaries toward the cities. Therefore, remaining permanently in the seminaries is not permissible. «فلو لانفر... لیتفقّهوا، لینذروا... اذا رجعوا»

14- For propagation and cultural work, one's birthplace is the priority. «لینذروا قومهم»

15- Scholars must go to the people and not wait for their invitation. «رجعوا الیهم»

16- The focus of propagation must be the creation of piety (taqwa) and the remembrance of the Hereafter. «لینذروا... لعلّهم یحذرون»

17- Jurists should not expect absolute obedience from all people, because a group will never come to the path of religion and Allah. «لعلّهم یحذرون»

190) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

191) Imam Ali عليه السلام said: If an incident occurs and the presence of everyone is needed at the front, it becomes obligatory upon everyone. Bihār, Vol. 97, p. 48.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قَـٰتِلُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ يَلُونَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْكُفَّارِ وَلْيَجِدُوا۟ فِيكُمْ غِلْظَةً ۚ وَٱعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۲۳)
O you who have faith! Fight the faithless who are in your vicinity, and let them find severity in you, and know that Allah is with the Godwary. (123)

Key Points

Engaging with and fighting the closer enemies has advantages, including: requiring less cost and fewer resources, having more information about them, easier preparation of the front, greater public readiness to confront them, their danger being more immediate, faster access to the war zone and means of repelling them, and the fact that they are both an enemy themselves and can be an arm for more distant enemies.

Ghilzah (غِلظة) means firmness, power, and awe, not hard-heartedness or ill-temper. Therefore, in military matters, maneuvers, displays of power, parades, uniforms, slogans, and anything that demonstrates the strength of the forces of Islam are praiseworthy. The Prophet of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله also commanded the Muslims, during the Conquest of Mecca, to march in parade before Abu Sufyan's eyes so that the morale of the polytheists would be weakened by demonstrating the power and splendor of the army of Islam.

The principle of al-Aqrab fal-Aqrab (الاقربُ فالاقرب), meaning prioritizing what is closer to a person, is relevant in all matters of Islamic teachings: in charity (infaq) and alms (sadaqah), first the orphaned and poor relative; in paying Zakat, first the local and nearby needy; in war, first the closer enemies; in invitation and propagation (da'wah and tabligh), first the close relatives and people of the region; in the mosque, precedence for the mosque's neighbor; at the dining cloth and in hospitality, the food closest to the person; and in congregational prayer and the first row, those who are closer to the Imam in terms of virtues.

This verse speaks of liberation Jihad, not defensive Jihad. Therefore, it addresses the characteristics of the Islamic fighters, not the conditions of the disbelievers' attack. (192)

Many examples are seen in the Prophet's conduct where He advised the army of Islam: to be pious, not to harm the women of the disbelievers, and not to damage trees. He was even severely distressed once when He saw the corpse of a disbelieving girl. (193)

192) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran. 193) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran.

Messages

1- Faith must be accompanied by action and struggle. «یا ایها الذین آمنوا قاتلوا»

2- Repelling the evil of the closer enemies has priority. «یلونكم من الكفّار» (The military strategy of Islam is first to confront the closer enemies, just as in intellectual and ideological struggle, one must first respond to the prevalent and existing doubts.)

3- Everything necessary to create firmness and severity must be prepared, whether it is the bravery of the fighters, advanced tools and weapons, or advanced and specialized training. «ولیجدوا فیكم غلظة»

4- The army of Islam must possess firmness and awe. «غلظة»

5- Inner courage must be accompanied by outward firmness. «و لیجدوا»

6- Islamic firmness and resolve are accompanied by piety (taqwa). «ان اللَّه مع المتقین»

7- Jihad is a manifestation of Divine piety. «قاتلوا... ان اللَّه مع المتقین»

8- Since instincts, desires, and grudges may arise during war, piety is an essential condition for the fighters. «انّ اللَّه مع المتقین»

Ayah

وَإِذَا مَآ أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ أَيُّكُمْ زَادَتْهُ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ إِيمَـٰنًا ۚ فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ فَزَادَتْهُمْ إِيمَـٰنًا وَهُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ (۱۲٤)
Whenever a surah is sent down, there are some of them who say, ‘Which of you did it increase in faith?’ As for those who have faith, it increases them in faith, and they rejoice. (124)

Messages

1- Mockery and a negative stance upon the revelation of the Quran are signs of hypocrisy. «اُنزلت سورة... یقول ایّكم زادته...»

2- Hypocrites transfer their negative morale to others through their questions. «یقول ایّكم زادته هذه ایماناً...»

3- Believers must be prepared to answer the questions and doubts posed by the hypocrites. «ایّكم زادته هذه ایماناً ...»

4- Faith has levels and is subject to increase and decrease, just as hypocrisy is subject to expansion and increase. «فزادتهم ایماناً»

5- The Quran is a good means for distinguishing the believer from the hypocrite. «فامّا الذین امنوا فزادتهم ایماناً»

6- The feeling of joy and spiritual vitality after hearing the verses of the Quran is a sign of the perfection and growth of faith. «فزادتهم ایماناً و هم یستبشرون»

7- The Quran is a source of glad tidings and good news for the people of faith. «و هم یستبشرون»

Ayah

وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَتْهُمْ رِجْسًا إِلَىٰ رِجْسِهِمْ وَمَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۱۲٥)
But as for those in whose heart is a sickness, it only adds defilement to their defilement, and they die while they are faithless. (125)

Key Points

If the carcass of a dead body falls into a pool, the more rain falls upon it, the fouler it becomes. This foul smell is not due to the rain, but rather due to that carrion. The spirit of obstinacy and arrogance in man causes the sick-hearted, upon the revelation of the verses of the Quran, to become more arrogant and show greater obstinacy, fanaticism, and enmity. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "رِجساً الى رِجسهم" (defilement upon their defilement) is "شكّاً الى شكّهم" (doubt upon their doubt). [194]

194) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Spiritual illnesses are like physical illnesses; if we do not seek a remedy, they spread and destroy the person. "فزادتهم رجساً الى رجسهم" (It increased them in defilement upon their defilement)

2- Disbelief, concealing the truth, and ingratitude are dangerous illnesses of the human soul and heart. "فى قلوبهم مرض... كافرون" (In their hearts is a disease... disbelievers)

3- The hypocrite has an evil end. "ماتوا و هم كافرون" (They died while they were disbelievers)

Ayah

أَوَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَنَّهُمْ يُفْتَنُونَ فِى كُلِّ عَامٍ مَّرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ ثُمَّ لَا يَتُوبُونَ وَلَا هُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (۱۲٦)
Do they not see that they are tried once or twice every year? Yet they neither repent, nor do they take admonition. (126)

Messages

1- It is among the Divine traditions to test human beings at all times and for everyone. «فى كلّ عام»

2- The philosophy behind Divine events and tests is repentance and awareness. «لایتوبون، لایذّكّرون»

3- Failing to take heed from Divine tests is a sign of hypocrisy and sickness of the heart, and it warrants blame and reproach. «لا یتوبون و لا هم یذكّرون»

4- Hardness of heart and an evil end are the results of hypocrisy. «لایتوبون، لا یذكّرون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا مَآ أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ نَّظَرَ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ هَلْ يَرَىٰكُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ ثُمَّ ٱنصَرَفُوا۟ ۚ صَرَفَ ٱللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُم بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ (۱۲۷)
Whenever a surah is sent down they look at one another: ‘Is anybody observing you?’ Then they slip away. Allah has turned aside their hearts, for they are a people who do not understand. (127)

Key Points

Hypocrites are not affected by the reminders in the verses of the Quran, like a burnt-out lamp that will not light up no matter how much it is connected to electricity.

Messages

1- Hypocrites feel anxiety and distress about the exposure of their wicked nature and seek concealment. «نظر بعضهم الى بعض هل یراكم من احد»

2- Hypocrites dislike the revelation of divine verses. «نظر بعضهم الى بعض»

3- Hypocrites flee from Quranic sessions. «انزلت سورة... انصرفوا» Distance from the Quran is a sign of hypocrisy and sickness of the heart.

4- Divine wrath is due to the human spirit that flees guidance. «انصرفوا، صرف اللَّه...»

5- The reason for turning away from the Quran is the lack of correct perception and understanding. «قوم لایفقهون»

Ayah

لَقَدْ جَآءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ عَزِيزٌ عَلَيْهِ مَا عَنِتُّمْ حَرِيصٌ عَلَيْكُم بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۲۸)
There has certainly come to you an apostle from among yourselves. Grievous to him is your distress; he has deep concern for you, and is most kind and merciful to the faithful. (128)

Key Points

Allah has not applied two of His Names—Ra’ūf (Most Kind) and Raḥīm (Most Merciful)—to any prophet other than the Prophet of Islam. Attention to the attributes of the heavenly leaders mentioned in this verse, and comparing them with the attributes of other human leaders, demonstrates Allah's favor upon humanity and the necessity of absolute obedience to such leaders.

Messages

1- The Messenger of Allah arises from among the people themselves. «من انفسكم»

2- The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is concerned about the suffering of the Ummah (community). «عزیز علیه ماعنتم»

3- Islamic leaders must sympathize and stand with the people during hardships and difficulties. «عزیز علیه ماعنتم»

4- The Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله spares no effort in guiding and instructing the people. «حریص علیكم»

5- Factors contributing to the influence of speech upon others include goodwill, compassion, kindness, lack of expectation (disinterest in worldly reward), and humility. «عزیز علیه، حریص علیكم، رؤف رحیم»

6- The Islamic leader is Ra’ūf (Kind) and Raḥīm (Merciful) only toward the believers, not toward everyone; rather, he is severe and harsh toward the enemy. «بالمؤمنین رؤف رحیم»

Ayah

فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَقُلْ حَسْبِىَ ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ۖ وَهُوَ رَبُّ ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۱۲۹)
But if they turn their backs [on you], say, ‘Allah is sufficient for me. There is no god except Him. In Him I have put my trust and He is the Lord of the Great Throne.’ (129)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the compassion and sympathy of the Prophet. In this Ayah, [Allah] states: Let no one imagine that the fervor, effort, and compassion of the Prophet towards the people was due to a need for them, because even if all people turn away from Him, Allah is with Him. Just as if all people face the sun or turn their backs to it, it has no effect on the sun. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of the «عرش عظیم» (Great Throne) is the «مُلك عظیم» (Great Dominion/Sovereignty). [195] The God Who preserves and manages the great system of existence can also keep a small human being under His favors. Imam Husayn عليه السلام, addressing Allah in the Du'a of 'Arafah, says: «ماذا وَجَد مَن فَقدك و ماذا فَقَد مَن وجدك؟» (What has he found who has lost You, and what has he lost who has found You?) Whoever does not have You, what does he have? And whoever has You, what does he lack?

195) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Tawhid Saduq, p. 321.

Messages

1- The turning away of people from religion should not affect our faith and morale. «فان تولّوا فقل حسبى اللَّه»

2- He who has Allah, what does he lack? «حسبى اللَّه»

3- Reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) is the key to overcoming difficulties. « فان تولّوا فقل... علیه توكّلت»

«اَلحَمدُللَّه ربّ العالَمیِن»

Portrait of Surah Yunus

The tenth Surah of the Holy Quran, which was revealed in Mecca during the early period of the Prophetic mission (Bi'that), is named "Yunus" (Jonah). This Surah has 109 Ayahs, and its main topics revolve around Monotheism (Tawhid) and the truthfulness of the Quran, responding to the deniers of revelation, warning the polytheists, explaining the greatness of creation and the Creator, the instability of the world, and drawing attention to the Hereafter.

The previous Surah, namely At-Tawbah, described the behavior of the hypocrites and their punishment; this Surah addresses the behavior of the polytheists.
